body:
### Feature hasn't been suggested before.

- [x] I have verified this feature I'm about to request hasn't been suggested before.

### Describe the enhancement you want to request

Following up on the implementation of local codebase indexing provided by @shssoichiro , I believe that it would be very useful if a dialog window pops up in e.g. VS Code (or in the TUI) to ask the user whether it wants Kilo to index codebade/workspace or not, provided that there is a local codebase indexing already configured and enabled.

From my point of view, it would serve two purposes:

1) The user will get to decide whether it is really necessary to index the codebase or not, thus potentially saving space within the vector database
2) If the user has opened a code project at a wrong workspace level by accident, then it can act upon it before Kilo unnecessarily starts indexing the codebase (this information should be included in that opened dialog window that is popped up)

comments.body: Did the old extension make you click the indexing icon to start indexing the first time you indexed a workspace? I think I remember that being a thing. I'm trying to think of the best UX for this, because I don't love popups, but I do see the use case.

No, I didn't think so. I also double-checked the legacy repository and I got the following response: Well, I understand there are cons with popups... What about notifications informing about workspace not being indexed and allow the user to either enable codebase indexing or not? That is far less invasive to popups, I guess?

title: [FEATURE]: Add a dialog window asking to confirm whether Kilo Code should index the codebase/workspace or not

body:
### Feature hasn't been suggested before.

- [x] I have verified this feature I'm about to request hasn't been suggested before.

### Describe the enhancement you want to request

When plan mode finishes making a plan, it asks whether it should "continue here" or "start new session". This question is hardcoded.
The proposed setting options would be: ask (current hard coded behavior), current, new. The last 2 would permit the plan mode agent to proceed without human interaction.

From Devin (DeepWiki):

> Currently, there is no configuration setting to make the planning agent always hand off to a new context without asking. The logic is hardcoded in PlanFollowup.ask to prompt you for a choice between Continue here and Start new session plan-followup.ts:468-474 .
> 
> To achieve this behavior, the code in packages/opencode/src/kilocode/plan-followup.ts would need to be modified to bypass the prompt call and directly execute the startNew logic plan-followup.ts:480-490 .
> 
> **Technical Overview**
> The handoff process is managed by the PlanFollowup namespace in the kilocode package.
> 
> **Handoff Logic Flow**
> When an agent finishes a planning task (often triggered by the plan_exit tool), the SessionPrompt loop detects the need for a follow-up prompt.ts:1435-1438 . This triggers PlanFollowup.ask, which performs the following steps:
> 
> Plan Resolution: It identifies the generated plan from the assistant's messages plan-followup.ts:462-463 .
> User Prompting: It calls prompt(), which uses the Question.Service to display the selection UI to you plan-followup.ts:468 .
> Branching:
> New Session: If you select Start new session, it calls startNew(), which creates a fresh Session, generates a handover summary via an LLM call, and initializes the new session with the plan plan-followup.ts:480-490 .
> Continue: If you select Continue here, it injects an implementation instruction into the current session and retargets the prompt queue plan-followup.ts:492-505 .
> 
> **Key Components**
> Component	File	Responsibility
> PlanFollowup.ask	packages/opencode/src/kilocode/plan-followup.ts	Orchestrates the handoff question and response handling plan-followup.ts:451 .
> startNew	packages/opencode/src/kilocode/plan-followup.ts	Handles the creation of a new session and implementation context plan-followup.ts:366-449 .
> plan_exit	packages/opencode/src/tool/plan-exit.txt	The tool used by agents to signal that planning is complete plan-exit.txt:1-13 .
>
> **Notes**
> The prompt function is a wrapper around PlanFollowupRuntime.question.ask, which eventually communicates with the VS Code webview or CLI TUI plan-followup.ts:37-40 .
> Automating this would require adding a user preference or a flag to bypass the Question.Service interaction in PlanFollowup.ask.
title: [FEATURE]: config setting for plan mode handoff

body:
Upstream unified share fetching via an Effect Service called ShareNext API; Kilo uses a separate ingest endpoint, blocking adoption of the new architecture.

The work needed is:
- Cloud: Replace `ingest.kilosessions.ai/session/<id>` (returns nested `{ info, messages }`) with `/api/shares/<id>/data` returning a flat ShareData[] array of typed session/message/part items.
- Kilocode Repo: Use upstream's ShareNext fetch path in import.ts (drops the custom fetch + `KILO_SESSION_INGEST_URL` env var, gains auth headers via `shouldAttachShareAuthHeaders`).

comments.body: @kilocode-bot Start a boilerplate implementation of this

A boilerplate implementation has been opened in **[PR #10349](https://github.com/Kilo-Org/kilocode/pull/10349)**.

Here's what was scaffolded:

* **New `share-next-api.ts`** — `ShareData` discriminated union, `shouldAttachShareAuthHeaders()`, and `fetchShareData()` stub pointing to the new `/api/shares/:id/data` endpoint
* **Updated `import.ts`** — wired up the new fetch path, dropped the `KILO_SESSION_INGEST_URL` env var dependency

Next steps to complete the implementation:

1. Fill in the real auth token logic in `fetchShareData()`
2. Implement `transformShareData()` to map the flat `ShareData[]` array into the import format
3. Hook up the Effect Service layer for `ShareNext API`
4. Add tests

title: Update cloud session ingest endpoints to Share

body:
### Question

## Summary

I'm using NVIDIA NIM models through Kilo (VS Code extension + CLI). NVIDIA NIM has 11 Vision/Multimodal models that support image-to-text, but Kilo never passes images to any model — even though the models natively support multimodal inputs.

**Reference:** https://build.nvidia.com/models?filters=usecase%3Ausecase_image_to_text

## Vision Models on NVIDIA NIM that Kilo CANNOT use

1. **Moonshot AI** — kimi-k2 (1T multimodal MoE: image/video understanding)
2. 2. **NVIDIA** — nemotron-3-nano-omni-30b-a3b-reasoning (omni-modal: images, video, speech, text)
3. 3. **Mistral AI** — mistral-large-3-675b-instruct-2512 (VLM)
4. 4. **Mistral AI** — ministral-14b-instruct-2512 (VLM)
5. 5. **NVIDIA** — nemotron-nano-12b-v2-vl (multi-image + video understanding)
6. 6. **Mistral AI** — mistral-medium-3-instruct (multimodal)
7. 7. **Meta** — llama-4-maverick-17b-128e-instruct (multimodal, multilingual)
8. 8. **Microsoft** — phi-4-multimodal-instruct (image + audio inputs)
9. 9. **Meta** — llama-3.2-11b-vision-instruct (vision-language)
10. 10. **Meta** — llama-3.2-90b-vision-instruct (vision-language)
11. 11. **Google** — paligemma (vision-language)
**11 models total** — all support image inputs, zero work with Kilo's text-only pipeline.

## The Problem

When I use **opencode** (competing tool), it:
- Detects that the model supports multimodal inputs
- - Passes screenshots via chrome-devtools MCP
- - - Lets the model analyze UI layouts, code screenshots, diagrams, etc.
When I use **Kilo** (VS Code extension OR CLI), the same models:
- Never receive image data
- - Limited to text-only
- - - Cannot analyze any visual content despite model capability
## Questions

1. **Roadmap:** Is there a plan for multimodal/vision support in Kilo (both VS Code extension AND CLI)?
2. 2. **Architecture:** Why does Kilo block image inputs even when the model supports them?
3. 3. **Workarounds:** Any config flags, plugins, to pass images through?
4. 4. **VS Code vs CLI:** Will both get this, or only one?
5. 5. **Parity:** opencode already supports chrome-devtools screenshots + NVIDIA NIM vision. When will Kilo match this?
## Expected

Kilo should detect multimodal model support (via API metadata) and allow passing images/screenshots to the model through MCP tools.

## Environment
- Kilo CLI: Latest
- - Kilo VS Code Extension: Latest
- - - Model Provider: NVIDIA NIM (all 11 multimodal models above)
- - - - Comparison: opencode works with vision on same models + same NVIDIA NIM backend
title: Why doesn't Kilo support vision/multimodal capabilities when the underlying models do?

body: ### Description I have been using Kilo Code with local codebase indexing (implemented by @shssoichiro ) enabled for a great while and I have observed that it barely uses `semantic_search` at all, even for very open-ended question such as _"How is authentication implemented in Kilo Code?"_. Previously in the legacy version of Kilo Code, it would have used `codebase_search` (the old name to current `semantic_search`) for such questions with significantly higher probability. To provide an example while using Claude Opus 4.6: I asked Kilo Code to provide me an answer to the prompt _"How is authentication implemented for @kilocode-fork/ ?"_. The first thing it immediately does is to create an Explore Agent that in turn uses glob/grep commands before using a lot of read operations and further glob/grep commands (see the image for full context): Ideally, it should have started to use the `semantic_search` tool to get some better suggestions on files it should start digging into using e.g. grep/glob/read, but this is not the case. I did then confront Kilo Code and asked about why it didn't use `semantic_search` in this scenario and then I got the following answer: It seems to me that it treats e.g. Explore Agent and `semantic_search` tool as opposing to each other and only one of them can be used at a time during the session instead of using these two altogether as separate calls. If my memory isn't betraying me here, surely do the subagents have access to `semantic_search` that they could call? Strange that the Explore Agent seems to omit the `semantic_search` tool when not explicitly asked to do use it. ### Plugins VS Code ### Kilo version 7.2.52 ### Steps to reproduce _No response_ ### Screenshot and/or share link _No response_ ### Operating System Windows 11 ### Terminal Git Bash in Windows Terminal

comments.body: This is a challenge I've noticed as well. My workaround was to add a personal rule to encourage it to use semantic search at the start of exploration, but that shouldn't be necessary. I'll review the tool descriptions and see how they can be improved to encourage better use of semantic search.
The team has merged a fix that we hope should address this. Let us know if you continue to see this behavior. I'll keep an eye out for it as well.
Splendid! Glad to hear that a fix has already been implemented for this. Shall we close this issue now or should we wait with closing this issue down until the updated behavior has been tested?
We can leave it open until we feel comfortable that it's resolved. It's the type of issue that needs real world usage to really verify, since we need a decent sample size

@shssoichiro , I have tested a little with OpenAI models (GPT-5.5 in particular) and it seems to be using semantic_search more often than the Anthropic models. I am not sure if Anthropic models are trained to rely on grep/glob patterns to high extent, but that is my observation from what I can see on my side. But in my opinion, they definitely should leverage semantic_search if it is exposed to them.

Isn't possible to e.g. give more context about why semantic_search is exposed to them? For example, maybe we should clarify that semantic_search tool is there to reduce the search space and to reduce time/effort/cost?

Fresh community validation from 2026-06-03 indicates this behavior is still occurring after the previous fix. In the reported setup, codebase indexing and AI codesearch were both enabled, but GPT-5.5/5.4 continued to choose `glob`, `grep`, and `read` rather than `semantic_search`.

The installed Kilo version was not captured, so this does not yet confirm whether the reporter had the latest build. Keeping this open still looks appropriate until we can validate the updated tool-selection behavior in a current release.

As mentioned in the opening issue, I suspect it could be the renaming of it to a semantic search from "codebase_search". The models were able to find it automatically or if I had to occasionally call it out, I would simply say "search tool".   Models in Kilo code can't find it under "search tool," I must say semantic search.  I find that renaming things can be pretty invasive, I do not do this lightly.

I would have loved for the naming to not be confusing. The reason it was renamed to "semantic search" is because at the same time this tool was being developed, a separate codebase search tool was added due to some integration with Warpgrep. So now, both search tools exist in Kilo under separate names, although the Warpgrep-based one is off by default and still experimental.

title: `semantic_search` tool barely used by Kilo Code while local codebase indexing is enabled

body:
## Summary

Kimi-2.6 consistently returns `"The engine is currently overloaded, please try again later (Xs)"` on a specific workspace **only when using Kilo Code**. Other models (Mimo-2.7-pro, Claude, GPT-4o) work fine on the exact same repository. The issue started the same minute Kilo Code `7.2.52` was installed, suggesting a change in context-assembly or plugin auto-install behavior in that version is bloating the request payload sent to Kimi.

## Environment

| Item | Value |
|---|---|
| **OS** | Windows 11 |
| **Shell** | PowerShell |
| **VS Code** | Latest stable |
| **Kilo Code extension** | `7.2.52` (installed `13 May 2026 18:04:28`) |
| **Kimi VS Code extension** | `moonshot-ai.kimi-code` installed |
| **Model failing** | Kimi-2.6 (Code Agent / Test Engineer) |
| **Models working** | Mimo-2.7-pro, Claude Sonnet 4, GPT-4o |
| **Workspace** | Next.js + Prisma + shadcn project |
| **Total workspace files** | ~46,878 files |
| **Root `node_modules/`** | 37,863 files / 976 MB (gitignored, **not** in git) |
| **`.kilo/node_modules/`** | 3,445 files / 48.55 MB (auto-recreated by Kilo on startup) |

## Reproduction Steps

1. Open the workspace in VS Code with Kilo Code `7.2.52`.
2. Set model to **Kimi-2.6** (Code Agent or Test Engineer).
3. Send any prompt.
4. Observe: `"The engine is currently overloaded, please try again later (4s)"` — countdown matches `Retry-After` header from `api.kimi.com/coding/`.
5. Switch model to Claude, GPT-4o, or Mimo-2.7-pro on the same prompt in the same workspace → works immediately.
6. Close VS Code, delete `.kilo/node_modules/` and `.kilo/package.json`, reopen VS Code → Kilo recreates both files/folders within seconds.
7. Retry Kimi-2.6 → same overload error.

## Observed Behavior

- **Model-specific failure**: Only Kimi-2.6 fails; all other models succeed on identical prompts.
- **Workspace-specific**: Error occurs exclusively in this repository; smaller repos with Kimi-2.6 work fine.
- **Persistent across restarts**: The error survives full VS Code restarts, cache clears, and `.kilo/` partial cleanups.
- **Auto-recreation**: `.kilo/package.json` (containing `"@kilocode/plugin": "7.2.52"`) and `.kilo/node_modules/` are regenerated automatically after deletion.
- **File locks prevent cleanup**: `.kilo/node_modules/` is locked by `kilo.exe` processes (PIDs 32080, 35712) while VS Code runs.

## Key Findings from Diagnosis

### 1. Extension hardcoded exclusions do not cover `.kilo/`
In `dist/extension.js` line 694, the exclusion list is:
```js
["node_modules",".git",".vscode","dist","build","out",".next","coverage",".nyc_output","tmp","temp",".cache"]
```
**`.kilo/` is absent** from this list, yet `.kilo/` now contains a full `node_modules/` tree (3,445 files) starting in `7.2.52`.

### 2. Plugin auto-install introduced in 7.2.52
`.kilo/package.json` is auto-generated with:
```json
{ "dependencies": { "@kilocode/plugin": "7.2.52" } }
```
This triggers installation of `effect`, `msgpackr`, `fast-check`, `kubernetes-types`, and others into `.kilo/node_modules/` — **48.55 MB of files that are never referenced by the application codebase** (grep across `src/` returned zero imports).

### 3. Context payload likely includes file-tree listing
Kilo appears to send a workspace file-tree listing (or directory scan) as part of the system prompt context. With 46,878 total workspace files, this may exceed Kimi's per-request token/context limits, causing the backend to reject with "overloaded." Other models may have higher limits or different routing that tolerates the payload.

### 4. Timeline correlation
Kilo `7.2.52` install timestamp: `13 May 2026 18:04:28`  
User reports issue began: ~3 days ago, around the same time.

### 5. VS Code settings routing Claude Code → Kimi
```json
"ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL": "https://api.kimi.com/coding/"
"ANTHROPIC_MODEL": "kimi-for-coding"
"kilo-code.new.claudeCodeCompat": true
```
This means even "Claude" mode may route through Kimi's infrastructure, yet it works — reinforcing that the issue is **Kimi-2.6 model-specific payload size**, not the Kimi backend itself.

## Expected Behavior

- Kilo should **not** include `.kilo/node_modules/` or `.kilo/worktrees/` in context assembly / file-tree scanning.
- Plugin auto-install into `.kilo/node_modules/` should either:
  - Respect a user-facing opt-out setting, or
  - Be excluded from workspace context by default.
- Kimi-2.6 should work on a 46k-file workspace just like other models.

## Proposed Fix / Suggested Scope

1. **Add `.kilo/` to the hardcoded exclusion list** in the extension scanner (or respect `.gitignore` for `.kilo/` the same way it respects `node_modules/`).
2. **Audit context-assembly code** for `scanDirectory|listFiles|fileTree|buildContext` to ensure excluded paths are pruned before payload construction.
3. **Provide a setting** to disable plugin auto-install (`@kilocode/plugin`) into `.kilo/node_modules/` for users who do not develop Kilo plugins.
4. **Respect `.kilo/.gitignore`** for context reads: `.kilo/.gitignore` already lists `node_modules`, `package.json`, `bun.lock`, but the extension appears to read these files for config anyway.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Kimi-2.6 can process prompts in a workspace with 40k+ files without "engine overloaded" errors.
- [ ] `.kilo/node_modules/` is excluded from workspace context/file-tree scanning.
- [ ] Users can prevent auto-creation of `.kilo/package.json` and `.kilo/node_modules/` via a setting.
- [ ] Behavior is unchanged for users who *do* want plugin development support.

## Related Information

- **VS Code extension path**: `C:\Users\...\Extensions\kilocode.kilo-code-7.2.52-win32-x64\`
- **Auto-generated `.kilo/.gitignore`**: `node_modules\npackage.json\nbun.lock\n.gitignore`
- **Kilo processes holding locks**: `kilo.exe` PIDs 32080, 35712; 6× `node.exe` (MCP server `@modelcontextprotocol/server-github`)
- **Workspace `.gitignore`**: properly excludes `node_modules/` (line 41) and `.kilo/` (line 237)
title: Kimi-2.6 "engine overloaded" on large workspaces — .kilo/node_modules/ likely bloating context payload (v7.2.52)

This way, we can know what the session is for. Currently, it's like this and it's impossible to identify. Renaming also requires going into the content to view it and then renaming again. The operation process is very cumbersome. Secondly, I set Chinese and asked questions in Chinese as well, but the answers are often a mixture of English and Chinese, or even more English. body:
## Summary

Add a phase-2 prompt-injection probe for tool output before it enters future model context.

The runtime classifier protects tool execution decisions; this probe adds defense-in-depth for poisoned file/web/MCP output.

## Scope

- Hook near tool-result conversion before output enters model context, likely around `packages/opencode/src/session/message-v2.ts`.
- Start with deterministic heuristics for common injection language and risky payloads.
- Warn the agent by prepending a clearly scoped warning to suspicious tool output.
- Avoid blocking output by default.
- Keep logic in Kilo-owned modules with a minimal shared hook.
- Leave optional model-based ambiguous-output probing for a later iteration if needed.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Suspicious tool output gets a warning before future model context sees it.
- Benign outputs are not modified by common cases.
- Probe does not persist or copy secrets beyond existing tool-result handling.
- Tests cover representative injection strings and benign examples.
- Classifier-denied tool calls do not feed poisoned output into the probe path.

## References

- Parent issue: #9138
- Design phase: Prompt-Injection Probe Phase 2
title: Add prompt-injection probe for tool output

body:
## Summary

Add CLI commands for inspecting and validating permission classifier policy.

These commands should make classifier behavior explainable before users enable it broadly.

## Scope

- Add a Kilo-owned command group, likely `packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/classifier.ts`.
- Register the command group with a minimal shared hook.
- Implement `kilo classifier defaults` to print built-in policy defaults as JSON.
- Implement `kilo classifier config` to print effective expanded config, including `"$defaults"` expansion.
- Include diagnostics for broad allow pruning so users understand why an action still reaches the classifier.
- Implement `kilo classifier critique` to run an LLM policy review or report clearly when no model is available.
- Consider `kilo classifier status` for recent decision state if runtime state is available in-process.

## Acceptance Criteria

- `kilo classifier defaults` and `kilo classifier config` are deterministic and testable.
- `critique` does not mutate config.
- Commands work without VS Code.
- Tests cover defaults/config output and at least one critique no-model/error path.
- Shared OpenCode command registration remains minimal and marked.

## References

- Parent issue: #9138
- Design phase: CLI Commands
title: Add permission classifier CLI inspection commands

body:
## Summary

Show classifier approvals and denials inline in conversations.

This brings back the useful legacy Gatekeeper visibility while fitting the current Kilo message/event architecture.

## Scope

- Add classifier decision events, e.g. `permission.classifier.decided`.
- Include session, permission, patterns/tool, decision, reason, model, stage, cost, and counter metadata.
- Map SSE events in the VS Code extension.
- Add webview message/context types for classifier decisions.
- Render compact approval and denial cards; denials should be prominent, approvals collapsed or subtle by default.
- Avoid synthetic system-role messages; prefer events/message parts/cards consistent with current UI.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Classifier denials and approvals can be seen in the conversation timeline.
- Denial UI includes concise reason, stage/model, and cost when available.
- Approvals do not create excessive noise in long sessions.
- Event payloads are stable enough for TUI/VS Code consumers.
- Tests cover event emission and UI rendering/serialization where practical.

## References

- Parent issue: #9138
- Design phase: Events and Inline Display
title: Render permission classifier decisions inline

body:
## Summary

Migrate legacy Kilo Gatekeeper/Yolo settings into the new permission classifier config.

This should preserve intent for users upgrading from the legacy extension while avoiding silent fallback to unsafe approve-all behavior.

## Scope

- Extend legacy setting types if old `yoloMode` and `yoloGatekeeperApiConfigId` keys are present in migrated storage.
- Update migration detection, cleanup, and result reporting.
- Resolve the legacy gatekeeper API/model profile where possible.
- Map `yoloMode: true` plus gatekeeper profile to `permission_classifier.enabled = true` and the resolved model.
- For `yoloMode: true` without a gatekeeper profile, warn explicitly and offer classifier defaults or an intentional allow-everything fallback.
- Update migration wizard copy and progress rows.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Existing auto-approval migration still works.
- Legacy gatekeeper settings migrate when recoverable.
- Unguarded legacy YOLO mode is never silently converted into unsafe classifier-disabled behavior.
- Migration results mention classifier settings separately from static permission rules.
- VS Code migration tests cover both gatekeeper-present and gatekeeper-missing cases.

## References

- Parent issue: #9138
- Related issue: #7684
- Design phase: Legacy Migration
title: Migrate legacy Gatekeeper settings to permission classifier

body:
## Summary

Expose permission classifier status and recent denials in the TUI.

This should make classifier mode understandable during terminal sessions without requiring VS Code.

## Scope

- Add a `/classifier` TUI command after runtime and CLI inspection commands exist.
- Display enabled/disabled/paused/escalated state.
- Display selected model(s), mode, and policy summary.
- Show recent classifier denials with reason text and counters.
- Make classifier-denied tools visually distinguishable from ordinary tool failures where practical.

## Acceptance Criteria

- `/classifier` works in the TUI and does not require external services unless a critique action is explicitly invoked.
- Denied classifier tool errors include enough context for a user to understand the block.
- TUI tests or focused component tests cover command registration/status formatting.
- Existing TUI permission prompt behavior remains unchanged when classifier is disabled.

## References

- Parent issue: #9138
- Design phase: TUI Surface
title: Expose permission classifier status in the TUI

body:
## Summary

Add VS Code settings and status surfaces for the permission classifier.

This should let users enable classifier mode, configure its policy, and distinguish classifier mode from the existing prompt auto-reply toggle.

## Scope

- Extend `packages/kilo-vscode/webview-ui/src/components/settings/AutoApproveTab.tsx` or add a focused classifier settings component.
- Add controls for enabled state, model selection, stage mode, and policy lists: `environment`, `allow`, `soft_deny`, `hard_deny`.
- Persist settings through the existing config context and CLI config API after SDK regeneration.
- Update the auto-approve/shield indicator to show off/enabled/paused/escalated classifier state.
- Clearly distinguish classifier-gated auto mode from the current runtime prompt auto-reply behavior in copy and hover details.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Users can enable classifier and edit policy lists from VS Code settings.
- Existing permission matrix editing still works.
- The indicator shows classifier status and model details without implying unsafe prompt auto-reply.
- VS Code typecheck, lint, and relevant unit tests pass.

## References

- Parent issue: #9138
- Design phase: VS Code Settings and Status
title: Add VS Code settings and indicator for permission classifier

body:
## Summary

Implement the opt-in runtime foundation for the LLM permission classifier described in #9138.

This is the first and highest-priority sub-issue. It should ship the core permission-pipeline behavior without CLI/TUI/VS Code product surfaces.

## Scope

- Add a top-level `permission_classifier` config block.
- Add Kilo-owned classifier modules under `packages/opencode/src/kilocode/permission/classifier/`.
- Add a narrow shared hook in `packages/opencode/src/permission/index.ts` before a risky request silently auto-approves.
- Support default policy expansion with `"$defaults"`.
- Build reasoning-blind classifier input from user messages and bare tool-call payload only.
- Implement two-stage classification with single-stage compatibility.
- Return classifier denials as tool errors and continue the agent loop.
- Track consecutive and total denials; escalate at 3 consecutive or 20 total.
- Fall back to normal `ask` on classifier model error, timeout, or unparseable response.
- Treat broad allow rules for shell/interpreters/package scripts/task as classifier-gated.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Classifier is disabled by default and no behavior changes when absent/disabled.
- Safe tools and in-project edits bypass classifier.
- Deterministic `deny` and protected config-path behavior remain authoritative.
- Runtime tests cover config, defaults, scope, transcript, state, and permission-hook behavior.
- `bun run typecheck` passes from `packages/opencode/`.
- `bun run script/check-opencode-annotations.ts` passes from repo root.
- SDK/schema generation is run if the config schema changes generated outputs.

## References

- Parent issue: #9138
- Runtime plan: `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-14-permission-classifier-runtime.md`
title: Implement permission classifier runtime foundation

body:
## Summary

Track the full LLM permission classifier rollout from #9138 as a parent implementation issue with reviewable implementation slices.

This decomposes the approved design and runtime implementation plan into phases so we can ship the highest-risk runtime path first, then layer CLI inspection, TUI/VS Code surfaces, migration, inline decision display, and prompt-injection probing without a single oversized PR.

## Context

- Source request: #9138
- Related earlier request: #7684
- Design doc uploaded below in the comment titled **Design Doc**.
- Runtime plan uploaded below in the comments titled **Runtime Implementation Plan**.

## Goals

- Gate risky auto-approved tool calls with an LLM-backed classifier.
- Keep deterministic permission `deny` rules and Kilo hard guards authoritative.
- Use reasoning-blind classifier inputs: user messages plus bare tool-call payloads only.
- Support two-stage classification with fail-closed fallback to normal `ask`.
- Return denials as tool errors so the agent can choose a safer path.
- Escalate at 3 consecutive or 20 total classifier denials.
- Keep Kilo-specific logic in Kilo-owned paths and shared OpenCode changes narrow.

## Implementation Issues

| Phase | Issue | Scope |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | #10249 | Runtime classifier MVP: config schema, policy defaults, scope checks, transcript builder, model runner, permission hook, counters, escalation, tests. |
| 2 | #10254 | CLI inspection commands: `kilo classifier defaults`, `config`, `critique`, and optional `status`. |
| 3 | #10251 | TUI classifier status: `/classifier`, recent denials, model/status/counter visibility. |
| 4 | #10250 | VS Code settings and indicator: settings UI, status states, model display, clear distinction from prompt auto-reply. |
| 5 | #10253 | Inline classifier decisions: events plus compact approval/denial rendering with reason/model/cost. |
| 6 | #10252 | Legacy gatekeeper migration: map old `yoloMode`/gatekeeper settings to classifier config or warn explicitly. |
| 7 | #10255 | Prompt-injection probe: warning layer for suspicious tool output before future model context. |

## Acceptance Criteria

- Each implementation issue is independently shippable and testable.
- Runtime issue #10249 lands first behind opt-in config.
- Follow-up UI/CLI surfaces do not block the runtime foundation.
- Existing permission behavior remains unchanged when classifier config is absent or disabled.
- Shared upstream-owned files contain only minimal `kilocode_change` hooks.

## Native Sub-Issue Status

The issues above are intended to be GitHub sub-issues of this parent. The native `addSubIssue` mutation currently requires additional repository permissions for this token, so this body keeps the hierarchy explicit until a maintainer links them natively.

comments.body:
## Design Doc

Uploaded from `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-14-permission-classifier-design.md`.

# LLM Permission Classifier Design

## Context

Issue [#9138](https://github.com/Kilo-Org/kilocode/issues/9138) asks Kilo to bring back the legacy AI Gatekeeper as a Claude Code auto-mode style permission classifier. The problem is approval fatigue: Kilo currently has granular rule-based permission prompts and a blunt `allowEverything`/auto-approve path, but no middle layer that evaluates whether an otherwise auto-approved risky action still aligns with the user's intent.

The feature should be designed as a comprehensive multi-phase rollout, implemented in small PRs with a runtime-first initial slice. Related issue [#7684](https://github.com/Kilo-Org/kilocode/issues/7684) is the older request to reintroduce AI Gatekeeper for CLI and VS Code.

## Goals

- Add an LLM-backed classifier that gates risky actions that would otherwise auto-approve.
- Keep deterministic user and managed permission rules authoritative: `deny` still blocks, hard ask/plan rules still block, and classifier cannot override them.
- Preserve Claude Code's core safety properties: tiered short-circuiting, reasoning-blind classifier input, two-stage classification, fail-closed behavior, deny-and-continue semantics, and escalation after repeated denials.
- Minimize upstream merge conflicts by putting classifier logic in Kilo-owned paths and only adding narrow hooks to shared OpenCode files.
- Plan the full product surface: CLI runtime, config commands, TUI status, VS Code settings/status, migration, inline decision display, and phase-2 prompt-injection probing.

## Non-Goals

- Do not make classifier decisions override explicit `deny` rules or Kilo hard guards.
- Do not reintroduce legacy `yoloMode` as an unguarded auto-approve mode.
- Do not build a perfect prompt-injection probe in the first runtime PR.
- Do not persist classifier counters in the session database unless later product requirements demand cross-process durability.
- Do not add broad Kilo-specific behavior inline in shared upstream files.

## Current System Summary

`packages/opencode/src/permission/index.ts` is the central permission gate. `Permission.ask()` evaluates rules, returns immediately when all patterns resolve to `allow`, throws `DeniedError` for `deny`, and publishes `permission.asked` only when a human prompt is needed. This is the right runtime hook because built-in tools, MCP tools, subagent spawns, bash, web fetches, and external-directory checks all converge there.

Important current call paths:

- `packages/opencode/src/session/prompt.ts:410` wires tool `ctx.ask()` to `Permission.ask()` for built-in tools.
- `packages/opencode/src/session/prompt.ts:487` asks before MCP tool execution.
- `packages/opencode/src/tool/bash.ts:288` asks for `external_directory` and `packages/opencode/src/tool/bash.ts:297` asks for `bash`.
- `packages/opencode/src/tool/webfetch.ts:40` asks for `webfetch`.
- `packages/opencode/src/tool/task.ts:47` asks for `task` before subagent delegation.
- `packages/opencode/src/session/processor.ts:231` converts permission failures into tool error parts.
- `packages/opencode/src/session/message-v2.ts:1009` sends errored tool calls back to the model as `output-error`.

Kilo-specific permission hardening already lives under `packages/opencode/src/kilocode/permission/`, including config-path protection and external-directory/read handling. New classifier logic should follow that pattern.

## Recommended Architecture

### Configuration

Add a top-level Kilo-specific config block named `permission_classifier` rather than `permission.classifier`.

Rationale: `packages/opencode/src/config/permission.ts` treats every child of `permission` as a permission rule. A nested `classifier` object would require a carve-out in both CLI and webview permission rule types, and would make the existing Auto Approve permission matrix accidentally preserve/render non-rule data. A top-level block is simpler, safer, and easier to promote later.

Proposed user config shape:

```jsonc
{
  "permission_classifier": {
    "enabled": true,
    "model": "kilo/kilo-auto/small",
    "stage1_model": null,
    "stage2_model": null,
    "environment": ["$defaults"],
    "allow": ["$defaults"],
    "soft_deny": ["$defaults"],
    "hard_deny": ["$defaults"],
    "mode": "two_stage",
    "timeout": 10000,
    "max_tokens": 512
  }
}
```

Fields:

- `enabled?: boolean`: default `false` until the UI/commands make activation explicit.
- `model?: null | ConfigModelID`: default model for both stages when stage-specific values are unset.
- `stage1_model?: null | ConfigModelID`: optional fast yes/no model override.
- `stage2_model?: null | ConfigModelID`: optional reasoning/structured decision model override.
- `environment?: string[]`: trusted infrastructure descriptions. `"$defaults"` expands to the working directory and git remotes.
- `allow?: string[]`: exceptions to soft block rules. `"$defaults"` expands default exceptions.
- `soft_deny?: string[]`: rules user intent can override. `"$defaults"` expands default soft denials.
- `hard_deny?: string[]`: unconditional classifier policy rules. This is not in the issue body's initial three prose slots, but Claude's current docs include it and it is useful for future compatibility.
- `mode?: "single_stage" | "two_stage"`: default `two_stage`; single-stage remains available for lower scope/cost.
- `timeout?: PositiveInt`: model call timeout in milliseconds.
- `max_tokens?: PositiveInt`: cap for structured reasoning output.

Because `Config.Info` is shared upstream-owned code, adding this schema in `packages/opencode/src/config/config.ts` needs narrow `kilocode_change` markers. Per repo guidance, the cloud JSON schema must also be mirrored later in `apps/web/src/app/config.json/extras.ts`.

### Defaults and Policy Expansion

Add Kilo-owned defaults under `packages/opencode/src/kilocode/permission/classifier/defaults.ts`.

Default `environment`:

- Current working directory.
- Current repo git remotes, if available.

Default `allow` examples:

- Install packages already declared in repo manifests or lockfiles.
- Run package manager install/test/build commands that operate in the current project without publishing or deploying.
- Push commits to the session working branch when the branch and remote are already associated with the repo.
- Standard credential flows where the user explicitly needs authentication and no secrets are revealed in tool output.
- Use trusted package registries and source-control remotes declared in the environment.

Default `soft_deny` groups:

- Destroy or exfiltrate: force-push, delete branches/remotes, delete buckets/databases, post code or secrets externally.
- Degrade security posture: disable logging, modify firewall/IAM/security settings, install persistence, change agent permission config to bypass review.
- Cross trust boundaries: use credentials discovered by the agent, run code from untrusted repos, send data to untrusted domains or unknown services.
- Bypass review or affect others: push to `main`/`master`, deploy to production, skip checks, mutate shared infrastructure or jobs not clearly created by this session.

Default `hard_deny` examples:

- Exfiltrate secrets or credential material.
- Modify Kilo permission/classifier config to weaken its own guardrails without explicit user instruction.
- Execute obvious malware/persistence patterns.

Each list supports `"$defaults"`. If a user omits `"$defaults"`, that list is fully replaced. This mirrors Claude Code's documented behavior and avoids silent merging surprises.

### Tiered Short-Circuiting

Classifier applies only to risky requests that would otherwise auto-approve.

Evaluation order inside `Permission.ask()`:

1. Resolve hard vetoes and deterministic deny rules exactly as today.
2. Determine whether every pattern resolves to `allow` and whether the request is not config-protected.
3. If the request still needs a normal human prompt, do not call the classifier.
4. If it would auto-approve, ask the Kilo classifier gate whether it should proceed.
5. Classifier `allow` returns silently.
6. Classifier `deny` fails the tool call with a classifier denial error.
7. Classifier error falls through to normal `ask` rather than auto-allowing.

Do not classify:

- Built-in safe tools: `read`, `grep`, `glob`, `list`, `codesearch`, `semantic_search`, `websearch`, `todoread`, `todowrite`, `lsp`.
- In-project `edit` operations, including `edit`, `write`, and `apply_patch`, when every path is inside the workspace root and not protected by `packages/opencode/src/kilocode/permission/config-paths.ts`.
- Any request that is already `deny` or already requires `ask`.

Classify:

- `bash` commands.
- `webfetch` requests.
- `external_directory` requests with unknown/write access or any out-of-project path not covered by the safe edit exception.
- MCP tools.
- `task` subagent delegation.
- Kilo Agent Manager tool permissions.
- `skill` execution unless the skill is on a narrow safe allowlist.
- Edits outside the project root.

The classifier hook should be a small marked call in `packages/opencode/src/permission/index.ts`, delegating to a Kilo-owned module such as `packages/opencode/src/kilocode/permission/classifier/index.ts`.

### Broad Allow Pruning

When classifier mode is enabled, broad allow rules capable of arbitrary code execution must not bypass classification.

Do not physically rewrite user config at first. Instead, in the classifier gate, treat these rules as requiring classifier evaluation even if they resolve to `allow`:

- `bash: "allow"` or `bash: { "*": "allow" }`.
- Broad interpreter patterns: `python *`, `python3 *`, `node *`, `bun *`, `ruby *`, `perl *`, `sh *`, `bash *`, `zsh *`, `deno *`, `tsx *`, `npx *`.
- Package script wildcard patterns: `npm run *`, `pnpm run *`, `yarn *`, `bun run *`, and similar broad script dispatchers.
- `task: "allow"`, `task: { "*": "allow" }`, or other task rules that allow all subagent types.
- MCP server-wide wildcard allows such as `<server>_*: allow` unless explicitly marked safe later.

Narrow rules still bypass classifier when they are specific enough to be reviewable, for example `bash: { "bun run typecheck": "allow" }` or `bash: { "prettier *": "allow" }`.

This "effective pruning" should be visible through `kilo classifier config` so users can understand why a command still reached the classifier.

### Classifier Input

Create a reasoning-blind classifier transcript:

- Include user messages only, from current session history.
- Include bare tool-call payloads needed to evaluate the current action.
- Exclude assistant prose.
- Exclude previous tool outputs.
- Exclude tool result content that could contain prompt injection.

For the current request, include:

```jsonc
{
  "permission": "bash",
  "patterns": ["git push origin feature"],
  "metadata": {
    "command": "git push origin feature"
  },
  "tool": {
    "messageID": "...",
    "callID": "..."
  }
}
```

For MCP, improve the request metadata in a later slice to include MCP tool args and description. The current wrapper at `packages/opencode/src/session/prompt.ts:487` passes only `{}` metadata and `patterns: ["*"]`, which limits classifier quality.

### Prompt and Decision Contract

Stage 1:

- Uses the expanded policy and reasoning-blind input.
- Emits a single token or structured boolean: block `yes`/`no`.
- Final instruction says to err on the side of blocking.
- If the output cannot be parsed, fail closed to `ask`.

Stage 2:

- Runs only when Stage 1 says block.
- Uses the same prompt/policy/input to maximize provider prompt-cache reuse.
- Returns structured JSON:

```jsonc
{
  "decision": "allow" | "deny",
  "reason": "short user-visible reason",
  "rule": "matching policy summary",
  "confidence": "low" | "medium" | "high"
}
```

Do not expose chain-of-thought. The model may reason internally, but the returned reason must be concise and user-visible.

Decision semantics:

- `allow`: continue silently for runtime v1, with optional event telemetry.
- `deny`: return a tool error instructing the agent to find a safer path and not route around the block.
- classifier model error/timeout/unparseable output: fall back to normal `ask`.

### Model Calls and Cost

Implement model calls in Kilo-owned code using the same provider infrastructure as existing lightweight helpers:

- `packages/opencode/src/kilocode/enhance-prompt.ts:22` shows direct `generateText` usage.
- `packages/opencode/src/agent/agent.ts:359` shows `generateObject` usage.
- `packages/opencode/src/provider/provider.ts:1823` parses `provider/model` strings.
- `packages/opencode/src/session/session.ts:335` can compute cost from AI SDK usage.

Prefer `generateObject` for Stage 2 because the decision contract is structured. Use `generateText` for Stage 1 if single-token behavior is important and cheaper.

Cost handling:

- Compute per-evaluation cost with `Session.getUsage()` when usage metadata is available.
- Store recent decisions in ephemeral classifier state for `kilo classifier status` and UI hover/status surfaces.
- Do not add classifier cost to assistant message total in the first PR unless product wants session cost to include safety overhead. Instead, display it in classifier decision events and status.

### Deny-and-Continue and Escalation

Create a Kilo-owned state module, e.g. `packages/opencode/src/kilocode/permission/classifier/state.ts`, using per-instance state keyed by `SessionID`.

Track:

- `consecutive`: classifier denials since the latest user turn.
- `total`: classifier denials in the current server process for the session.
- `recent`: recent classifier decisions with tool, reason, model, cost, and timestamp.

Reset `consecutive` on each user turn. The minimal hook is near `packages/opencode/src/session/prompt.ts:1301`, after a user message is created. Clear state on session removal if practical.

Denial behavior:

- Throw a dedicated `ClassifierDeniedError` from Kilo-owned classifier code.
- Error message should include existing denial substrings or the UI should learn the new error type. A concise message:
  - `The permission classifier denied this tool call: <reason>. Find a safer path and do not try to route around this boundary.`
- The processor should treat `ClassifierDeniedError` as a non-fatal denied tool error that lets the model continue.

Escalation:

- At `3` consecutive denials or `20` total denials, escalate.
- Interactive sessions: stop the turn and surface a session-level error asking the human to review classifier denials.
- Headless `kilo run`: terminate non-zero with a clear error. Current `packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/run.ts:541` records session errors but does not obviously turn them into non-zero exit; add a small follow-up change to set `process.exitCode = 1` when classifier escalation is observed.

### Events and Inline Display

Add classifier events after the runtime is stable:

- `permission.classifier.decided`: emitted for allow/deny/error decisions.
- Payload: `sessionID`, `permission`, `patterns`, optional `tool`, `decision`, `reason`, `model`, `stage`, `cost`, `consecutive`, `total`.

Initial rendering can be lightweight:

- TUI: show denials inline as failed tool calls and add `/classifier` for recent status.
- VS Code: map SSE events to webview messages and render compact decision cards. Approvals can be collapsed by default; denials should be prominent.

Avoid adding a synthetic system-role message. The webview message model is currently user/assistant only, and parts are the natural extension point.

### CLI Commands

Add a Kilo-owned command group in `packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/classifier.ts` and register it with a small marked line in `packages/opencode/src/index.ts`.

Commands:

- `kilo classifier defaults`: print built-in `environment`, `allow`, `soft_deny`, and `hard_deny` defaults as JSON.
- `kilo classifier config`: print effective expanded config, including `"$defaults"` expansion and broad allow pruning diagnostics.
- `kilo classifier critique`: run an LLM review of user policy entries and flag ambiguous, redundant, or risky rules.
- `kilo classifier status`: optional later command for recent decisions in the active server context if available.

### TUI Surface

Add `/classifier` in the TUI after runtime and CLI commands exist.

It should show:

- Enabled/disabled/paused state.
- Model(s) used.
- Effective policy summary.
- Recent denials and reasons.
- Escalation counters.

The existing command registration pattern lives around `packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/tui/routes/session/index.tsx:480`.

### VS Code Settings and Status

Settings:

- Extend `packages/kilo-vscode/webview-ui/src/components/settings/AutoApproveTab.tsx`, which currently only renders `PermissionEditor`.
- Add a classifier section above the permission matrix:
  - enabled toggle,
  - model selector,
  - stage mode,
  - policy list editors for `environment`, `allow`, `soft_deny`, `hard_deny`,
  - explanatory copy that classifier gates risky auto-approved actions and is not a replacement for `deny` rules.
- Existing config context and `KiloProvider.handleUpdateConfig()` should persist this once the SDK config type includes the new field.

Status:

- Prompt input/shield UI currently uses runtime auto-approve state from `packages/kilo-vscode/src/commands/toggle-auto-approve.ts`.
- The current runtime toggle auto-replies to `permission.asked`, which is different from classifier-gated auto mode. Product copy must distinguish unsafe "approve prompts once" from classifier mode.
- Add classifier state to the auto-approve indicator: off / enabled / paused / escalated. Model name can appear on hover.

Inline decisions:

- Extend SSE event mapping in `packages/kilo-vscode/src/kilo-provider-utils.ts`.
- Add webview message types and context handling.
- Render decision parts/cards in `AssistantMessage` or `@kilocode/kilo-ui` message-part components.

### Legacy Migration

Current legacy migration only covers `autoApprovalEnabled`, command allow/deny lists, and fine-grained auto-approval keys. `LegacySettings` does not currently include `yoloMode` or `yoloGatekeeperApiConfigId`.

Plan:

1. Extend `packages/kilo-vscode/src/legacy-migration/legacy-types.ts` with legacy YOLO/gatekeeper fields if they exist in old storage.
2. Update detection and cleanup lists in `migration-service.ts`.
3. Resolve legacy API profile/model where possible.
4. Migrate:
   - `yoloMode: true` + gatekeeper profile -> `permission_classifier.enabled = true`, `model = resolved provider/model`, default policy lists.
   - `yoloMode: true` without gatekeeper -> warn the user. Offer either classifier with default model or explicit allow-everything fallback.
5. Add migration wizard copy and progress rows in `MigrationWizard.tsx`.

If old storage keys cannot be recovered reliably, document the limitation and handle only keys present in active migration sources.

### Prompt-Injection Probe Phase 2

Add a separate input-layer probe after the runtime classifier has shipped.

Hook target:

- Tool result conversion before results enter future model context, near `packages/opencode/src/session/message-v2.ts:974`.

Initial behavior:

- Heuristic scan for common injection language and risky patterns, such as "ignore previous instructions", credential exfiltration instructions, `curl | bash`, hidden HTML prompt text, and base64 decode/execute chains.
- Prepend a warning to the tool result instructing the agent to treat the content as untrusted and anchor on the user request.

Later behavior:

- Optional small classifier call for ambiguous outputs.
- Warnings only; do not block tool output by default.

## Implementation Phases

### Phase 1: Runtime Classifier MVP

Files likely touched:

- `packages/opencode/src/config/config.ts` for top-level schema.
- `packages/opencode/src/permission/index.ts` for minimal hook.
- `packages/opencode/src/session/prompt.ts` for counter reset on user turn.
- `packages/opencode/src/session/processor.ts` for blocking/escalation handling.
- `packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/run.ts` for non-zero headless escalation.
- New Kilo-owned files under `packages/opencode/src/kilocode/permission/classifier/`.
- Tests under `packages/opencode/test/kilocode/permission/classifier/`.

Acceptance:

- Classifier runs only for risky auto-approved requests.
- Safe tools and in-project edits bypass it.
- Deterministic deny and config-path protection remain authoritative.
- Denial returns a tool error and does not publish `permission.asked`.
- Classifier error/timeout falls back to `ask`.
- Counters reset on user turn and escalate at thresholds.
- Broad allow pruning causes broad bash/task/interpreter allows to be classifier-gated.

### Phase 2: CLI Inspection Commands

Files likely touched:

- New `packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/classifier.ts`.
- Minimal registration in `packages/opencode/src/index.ts`.
- Kilo-owned policy/defaults tests.

Acceptance:

- `kilo classifier defaults` prints defaults.
- `kilo classifier config` prints effective expanded config and pruning diagnostics.
- `kilo classifier critique` runs a structured model review or reports when no model is configured.

### Phase 3: TUI Status

Files likely touched:

- `packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/tui/routes/session/index.tsx`.
- Kilo-owned TUI helper modules if needed.

Acceptance:

- `/classifier` shows state, models, counters, and recent denials.
- Denied tools are visually distinguishable from ordinary tool failures.

### Phase 4: VS Code Settings and Indicator

Files likely touched:

- `packages/kilo-vscode/webview-ui/src/components/settings/AutoApproveTab.tsx`.
- New classifier settings component under `webview-ui/src/components/settings/`.
- Config webview types after SDK generation.
- `packages/kilo-vscode/src/commands/toggle-auto-approve.ts` or prompt input state surfaces for indicator copy.
- i18n strings.

Acceptance:

- Users can enable classifier and edit policy lists from settings.
- The auto-approve indicator distinguishes runtime prompt auto-reply from classifier mode.
- Model name/status appears in hover/details.

### Phase 5: Inline Decision Events

Files likely touched:

- Kilo classifier event definitions.
- SSE mapping in `packages/kilo-vscode/src/kilo-provider-utils.ts`.
- Webview message/session context types.
- `AssistantMessage` or `@kilocode/kilo-ui` message part rendering.

Acceptance:

- Denials and approvals can be displayed inline.
- Denial cards include reason, model, cost, and stage.
- Approvals are compact/collapsible.

### Phase 6: Legacy Migration

Files likely touched:

## Testing Plan

Runtime tests under `packages/opencode/test/kilocode/permission/classifier/`:

- Safe-tool allowlist bypasses classifier.
- In-project edits bypass classifier; protected config edits still ask.
- Broad bash allow invokes classifier.
- Narrow bash allow bypasses classifier.
- Classifier denial returns tool error and does not create pending permission.
- Classifier timeout/error creates a normal pending permission prompt.
- Deterministic deny bypasses classifier and remains denied.
- Counter thresholds escalate and reset on user turn.
- Reasoning-blind transcript excludes assistant text and tool output.

Config/default tests:

- `"$defaults"` expands correctly.
- Omitting `"$defaults"` replaces that list.
- Config schema accepts `permission_classifier` and rejects malformed fields.

VS Code/UI tests later:

- Settings component preserves existing permission matrix config.
- Classifier settings save through existing config context.
- Migration wizard shows classifier choices when legacy keys exist.

## Merge-Minimization Notes

Shared upstream files should contain only narrow hooks:

- `packages/opencode/src/config/config.ts`: add schema entry.
- `packages/opencode/src/permission/index.ts`: call classifier gate before auto-approved return.
- `packages/opencode/src/session/prompt.ts`: reset counters on user turn.
- `packages/opencode/src/session/processor.ts`: recognize classifier denial/escalation.
- `packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/run.ts`: set non-zero exit on escalation.
- `packages/opencode/src/index.ts`: register CLI command group.

- `packages/opencode/test/kilocode/permission/classifier/`.
- `packages/kilo-vscode/` for extension surfaces.

Run `bun run script/check-opencode-annotations.ts` before claiming runtime/shared-code work is complete.

## Resolved Defaults for Implementation

- `permission_classifier.enabled` defaults to `false`; users explicitly enable it through config, CLI commands, or later UI.
- Classifier cost is displayed as classifier overhead in decision/status surfaces for the first runtime slice, not added to assistant message cost totals.
- Broad allow pruning is effective-only at first; no user config is rewritten automatically.
- VS Code's existing runtime auto-approve shield remains a separate prompt auto-reply toggle and should be relabeled clearly when classifier status is introduced.
- Stage 1 uses `stage1_model` when set, then `model`, then the default provider's small model. This keeps config optional while favoring lower-cost classification.

## Runtime Implementation Plan (1/2)

Uploaded from `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-14-permission-classifier-runtime.md`.

# Permission Classifier Runtime Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** Build the runtime-first LLM permission classifier from issue #9138 so risky auto-approved actions are gated by a Kilo-owned classifier before execution.

**Architecture:** Add a top-level `permission_classifier` config block, Kilo-owned classifier modules under `packages/opencode/src/kilocode/permission/classifier/`, and a minimal shared hook in `Permission.ask()`. The first runtime slice includes deterministic short-circuiting, policy/default expansion, reasoning-blind transcript building, a two-stage classifier call, denial/error semantics, counters, and focused tests; CLI/TUI/VS Code product surfaces remain later phases from the design spec.

**Tech Stack:** TypeScript, Effect v4 / effect-smol, Effect Schema, Bun tests, Vercel AI SDK `generateText`/`generateObject`, Kilo provider/model infrastructure, OpenCode permission/session services.

---

## Scope

This plan implements Phase 1 from `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-14-permission-classifier-design.md`: runtime classifier MVP. It intentionally does not implement `kilo classifier` CLI commands, TUI `/classifier`, VS Code settings/status, inline decision cards, legacy migration, or prompt-injection probing. Those remain planned follow-up phases.

## File Structure

Create focused Kilo-owned runtime modules:

- `packages/opencode/src/kilocode/permission/classifier/schema.ts`: public classifier config schema, decision/result schemas, decision error classes, constants.
- `packages/opencode/src/kilocode/permission/classifier/defaults.ts`: default policy lists and `"$defaults"` expansion.
- `packages/opencode/src/kilocode/permission/classifier/state.ts`: per-instance counters and recent decision storage.
- `packages/opencode/src/kilocode/permission/classifier/scope.ts`: safe-tool, in-project edit, broad allow pruning, and "should classify" logic.
- `packages/opencode/src/kilocode/permission/classifier/transcript.ts`: reasoning-blind transcript builder from `MessageV2.stream()`.
- `packages/opencode/src/kilocode/permission/classifier/model.ts`: provider/model resolution and stage 1 / stage 2 LLM calls.
- `packages/opencode/src/kilocode/permission/classifier/index.ts`: orchestration entrypoint used by shared permission code.

Modify narrow shared hooks:

- `packages/opencode/src/config/config.ts`: add top-level `permission_classifier` schema field.
- `packages/opencode/src/permission/index.ts`: import/call classifier before silent auto-approve return, widen permission error type.
- `packages/opencode/src/session/prompt.ts`: reset consecutive denial counter on user turn.
- `packages/opencode/src/session/processor.ts`: classify classifier-denied tool errors as blocked and persist classifier metadata on tool error state.
- `packages/opencode/src/session/message-v2.ts`: convert classifier escalation into an assistant/session error object.
- `packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/run.ts`: set non-zero exit code when classifier escalation appears as a session error.

Tests:

- `packages/opencode/test/kilocode/permission/classifier/defaults.test.ts`: policy defaults and expansion.
- `packages/opencode/test/kilocode/permission/classifier/scope.test.ts`: safe-tool, edit, and broad allow pruning decisions.
- `packages/opencode/test/kilocode/permission/classifier/transcript.test.ts`: reasoning-blind transcript behavior.
- `packages/opencode/test/kilocode/permission/classifier/state.test.ts`: counter reset, recent decisions, thresholds.
- `packages/opencode/test/kilocode/permission/classifier/config.test.ts`: `Config.Info` schema accepts/rejects classifier config.
- `packages/opencode/test/kilocode/permission/classifier/permission-hook.test.ts`: central permission hook behavior with a stub classifier.

```ts
import { Schema } from "effect"
import { zod } from "@/util/effect-zod"
import { NonNegativeInt, PositiveInt, withStatics } from "@/util/schema"
import { ConfigModelID } from "@/config/model-id"
import { MessageID, SessionID } from "@/session/schema"
import { PermissionID } from "@/permission/schema"

export const DEFAULT_REF = "$defaults"
export const CONSECUTIVE_LIMIT = 3
export const TOTAL_LIMIT = 20

export const Mode = Schema.Literals(["single_stage", "two_stage"])
  .annotate({ identifier: "PermissionClassifierMode" })
  .pipe(withStatics((s) => ({ zod: zod(s) })))
export type Mode = Schema.Schema.Type<typeof Mode>

export const Config = Schema.Struct({
  enabled: Schema.optional(Schema.Boolean),
  model: Schema.optional(Schema.NullOr(ConfigModelID)),
  stage1_model: Schema.optional(Schema.NullOr(ConfigModelID)),
  stage2_model: Schema.optional(Schema.NullOr(ConfigModelID)),
  environment: Schema.optional(Schema.mutable(Schema.Array(Schema.String))),
  allow: Schema.optional(Schema.mutable(Schema.Array(Schema.String))),
  soft_deny: Schema.optional(Schema.mutable(Schema.Array(Schema.String))),
  hard_deny: Schema.optional(Schema.mutable(Schema.Array(Schema.String))),
  mode: Schema.optional(Mode),
  timeout: Schema.optional(PositiveInt),
  max_tokens: Schema.optional(PositiveInt),
})
  .annotate({ identifier: "PermissionClassifierConfig" })
  .pipe(withStatics((s) => ({ zod: zod(s) })))
export type Config = Schema.Schema.Type<typeof Config>

export const Decision = Schema.Literals(["allow", "deny", "ask"])
  .annotate({ identifier: "PermissionClassifierDecision" })
  .pipe(withStatics((s) => ({ zod: zod(s) })))
export type Decision = Schema.Schema.Type<typeof Decision>

export const Stage = Schema.Literals(["stage1", "stage2", "error", "skip"])
  .annotate({ identifier: "PermissionClassifierStage" })
  .pipe(withStatics((s) => ({ zod: zod(s) })))
export type Stage = Schema.Schema.Type<typeof Stage>

export const Tool = Schema.Struct({
  messageID: MessageID,
  callID: Schema.String,
})
export type Tool = Schema.Schema.Type<typeof Tool>

export const Snapshot = Schema.Struct({
  permission: Schema.String,
  pattern: Schema.String,
  action: Schema.Literals(["allow", "deny", "ask"]),
})
export type Snapshot = Schema.Schema.Type<typeof Snapshot>

export const Request = Schema.Struct({
  id: Schema.optional(PermissionID),
  sessionID: SessionID,
  permission: Schema.String,
  patterns: Schema.Array(Schema.String),
  metadata: Schema.Record(Schema.String, Schema.Unknown),
  always: Schema.Array(Schema.String),
  tool: Schema.optional(Tool),
})
export type Request = Schema.Schema.Type<typeof Request>

export const Policy = Schema.Struct({
  environment: Schema.Array(Schema.String),
  allow: Schema.Array(Schema.String),
  soft_deny: Schema.Array(Schema.String),
  hard_deny: Schema.Array(Schema.String),
})
export type Policy = Schema.Schema.Type<typeof Policy>

export const TranscriptTurn = Schema.Struct({
  id: MessageID,
  text: Schema.String,
})
export type TranscriptTurn = Schema.Schema.Type<typeof TranscriptTurn>

export const Transcript = Schema.Struct({
  user: Schema.Array(TranscriptTurn),
  request: Request,
})
export type Transcript = Schema.Schema.Type<typeof Transcript>

export const Result = Schema.Struct({
  decision: Decision,
  stage: Stage,
  reason: Schema.String,
  rule: Schema.optional(Schema.String),
  confidence: Schema.optional(Schema.Literals(["low", "medium", "high"])),
  model: Schema.optional(Schema.String),
  cost: Schema.optional(Schema.Finite),
})
export type Result = Schema.Schema.Type<typeof Result>

export const Record = Schema.Struct({
  sessionID: SessionID,
  decision: Decision,
  stage: Stage,
  permission: Schema.String,
  patterns: Schema.Array(Schema.String),
  reason: Schema.String,
  model: Schema.optional(Schema.String),
  cost: Schema.optional(Schema.Finite),
  consecutive: NonNegativeInt,
  total: NonNegativeInt,
  time: NonNegativeInt,
})
export type Record = Schema.Schema.Type<typeof Record>

export class DeniedError extends Schema.TaggedErrorClass<DeniedError>()("PermissionClassifierDeniedError", {
  sessionID: SessionID,
  permission: Schema.String,
  patterns: Schema.Array(Schema.String),
  reason: Schema.String,
  consecutive: NonNegativeInt,
  total: NonNegativeInt,
}) {
  override get message() {
    return `The permission classifier denied this tool call: ${this.reason}. Find a safer path and do not try to route around this boundary.`
  }
}

export class EscalationError extends Schema.TaggedErrorClass<EscalationError>()("PermissionClassifierEscalationError", {
  sessionID: SessionID,
  reason: Schema.String,
  consecutive: NonNegativeInt,
  total: NonNegativeInt,
}) {
  override get message() {
    return `The permission classifier stopped this session after repeated denials (${this.consecutive} consecutive, ${this.total} total): ${this.reason}`
  }
}
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Implement default policy expansion**

Create `packages/opencode/src/kilocode/permission/classifier/defaults.ts`:

```ts
import type { Config, Policy } from "./schema"
import { DEFAULT_REF } from "./schema"

const environment = [
  "Trust the current working directory and files inside the current git worktree.",
  "Trust git remotes configured for the current repository.",
]

const allow = [
  "Installing packages already declared in package manifests or lockfiles is allowed when it stays inside the current project.",
  "Running project-local install, test, typecheck, lint, format, and build commands is allowed when they do not publish, deploy, or modify external infrastructure.",
  "Pushing commits to the session working branch is allowed when the branch and remote already belong to the current repository.",
  "Standard credential flows are allowed when the user explicitly needs authentication and secrets are not printed, copied, or sent to unrelated destinations.",
  "Using trusted package registries and source-control remotes described in the environment is allowed.",
]

const soft = [
  "Block irreversible destructive actions such as force-pushing over shared history, deleting remote branches, deleting cloud buckets, deleting databases, or removing shared infrastructure.",
  "Block exfiltration of repository contents, private files, logs, environment variables, credentials, API keys, tokens, SSH keys, or other secrets to destinations outside the trusted environment.",
  "Block actions that degrade security posture, including disabling logs, weakening firewall or IAM settings, installing persistence, or modifying permission/classifier config to bypass review.",
  "Block crossing trust boundaries by running code from untrusted repositories, using credentials discovered by the agent, or sending data to untrusted domains or unknown services.",
  "Block bypassing review or safeguards, including pushing directly to main or master, deploying to production, skipping verification flags, or mutating shared jobs/resources not created by this session.",
  "Block acting on resources selected by fuzzy matching, inference, or guesswork when the user did not identify the exact target.",
]

const hard = [
  "Never exfiltrate credential material, private keys, API tokens, session cookies, or secret environment variables.",
  "Never weaken the agent's own permission, classifier, sandbox, telemetry, or audit settings unless the user explicitly requested that exact change.",
  "Never install persistence such as login items, cron jobs, launch agents, shell startup hooks, SSH authorized keys, or background daemons without explicit user instruction.",
]

function expandList(value: string[] | undefined, base: string[]) {
  if (!value) return [...base]
  if (!value.includes(DEFAULT_REF)) return [...value]
  return value.flatMap((item) => (item === DEFAULT_REF ? base : [item]))
}

export namespace ClassifierDefaults {
  export const environmentDefaults = environment
  export const allowDefaults = allow
  export const softDenyDefaults = soft
  export const hardDenyDefaults = hard

  export function expand(cfg: Config | undefined): Policy {
    return {
      environment: expandList(cfg?.environment, environment),
      allow: expandList(cfg?.allow, allow),
      soft_deny: expandList(cfg?.soft_deny, soft),
      hard_deny: expandList(cfg?.hard_deny, hard),
    }
  }
}
```

// kilocode_change - new file
import { describe, expect } from "bun:test"
import { Effect, Layer } from "effect"
import { Bus } from "../../../../src/bus"
import { ClassifierState } from "../../../../src/kilocode/permission/classifier/state"
import { SessionID } from "../../../../src/session/schema"
import { testEffect } from "../../../lib/effect"

const env = Layer.mergeAll(Bus.layer)
const it = testEffect(env)
const sid = SessionID.make("session_classifier_state")

describe("ClassifierState", () => {
  it.live("records denials and thresholds", () =>
    Effect.gen(function* () {
      ClassifierState.clear(sid)
      const first = ClassifierState.record({
        sessionID: sid,
        permission: "bash",
        patterns: ["git push"],
        decision: "deny",
        stage: "stage2",
        reason: "force push was not requested",
      })
      const second = ClassifierState.record({
        sessionID: sid,
        permission: "bash",
        patterns: ["git push"],
        decision: "deny",
        stage: "stage2",
        reason: "force push was not requested",
      })
      const third = ClassifierState.record({
        sessionID: sid,
        permission: "bash",
        patterns: ["git push"],
        decision: "deny",
        stage: "stage2",
        reason: "force push was not requested",
      })

      expect(first.consecutive).toBe(1)
      expect(first.total).toBe(1)
      expect(first.escalated).toBe(false)
      expect(second.consecutive).toBe(2)
      expect(third.consecutive).toBe(3)
      expect(third.total).toBe(3)
      expect(third.escalated).toBe(true)
    }),
  )

  it.live("allow resets consecutive but not total", () =>
    Effect.gen(function* () {
      ClassifierState.clear(sid)
      ClassifierState.record({
        sessionID: sid,
        permission: "bash",
        patterns: ["git push"],
        decision: "deny",
        stage: "stage2",
        reason: "blocked",
      })
      const allow = ClassifierState.record({
        sessionID: sid,
        permission: "bash",
        patterns: ["bun run typecheck"],
        decision: "allow",
        stage: "stage1",
        reason: "safe project command",
      })

      expect(allow.consecutive).toBe(0)
      expect(allow.total).toBe(1)
      expect(ClassifierState.get(sid).recent).toHaveLength(2)
    }),
  )

  it.live("reset clears consecutive only", () =>
    Effect.gen(function* () {
      ClassifierState.clear(sid)
      ClassifierState.record({
        sessionID: sid,
        permission: "bash",
        patterns: ["git push"],
        decision: "deny",
        stage: "stage2",
        reason: "blocked",
      })
      ClassifierState.reset(sid)

      expect(ClassifierState.get(sid).consecutive).toBe(0)
      expect(ClassifierState.get(sid).total).toBe(1)
    }),
  )
})

Create `packages/opencode/src/kilocode/permission/classifier/state.ts`:

```ts
import { CONSECUTIVE_LIMIT, TOTAL_LIMIT, type Decision, type Record, type Stage } from "./schema"
import type { SessionID } from "@/session/schema"

const LIMIT = 20

type Input = {
  sessionID: SessionID
  permission: string
  patterns: string[]
  decision: Decision
  stage: Stage
  reason: string
  model?: string
  cost?: number
}

type Entry = {
  consecutive: number
  total: number
  recent: Record[]
}

const entries = new Map<SessionID, Entry>()

function entry(sessionID: SessionID) {
  const hit = entries.get(sessionID)
  if (hit) return hit
  const next = { consecutive: 0, total: 0, recent: [] }
  entries.set(sessionID, next)
  return next
}

export namespace ClassifierState {
  export function get(sessionID: SessionID) {
    const state = entry(sessionID)
    return {
      consecutive: state.consecutive,
      total: state.total,
      recent: [...state.recent],
    }
  }

  export function reset(sessionID: SessionID) {
    const state = entry(sessionID)
    state.consecutive = 0
  }

  export function clear(sessionID: SessionID) {
    entries.delete(sessionID)
  }

  export function record(input: Input) {
    const state = entry(input.sessionID)
    if (input.decision === "deny") {
      state.consecutive++
      state.total++
    } else {
      state.consecutive = 0
    }

    const item: Record = {
      sessionID: input.sessionID,
      decision: input.decision,
      stage: input.stage,
      permission: input.permission,
      patterns: input.patterns,
      reason: input.reason,
      model: input.model,
      cost: input.cost,
      consecutive: state.consecutive,
      total: state.total,
      time: Date.now(),
    }
    state.recent.unshift(item)
    state.recent = state.recent.slice(0, LIMIT)

    return {
      ...item,
      escalated: state.consecutive >= CONSECUTIVE_LIMIT || state.total >= TOTAL_LIMIT,
    }
  }
}
```

Create `packages/opencode/src/kilocode/permission/classifier/scope.ts`:

```ts
import path from "path"
import { AppFileSystem } from "@opencode-ai/core/filesystem"
import type { Permission } from "@/permission"
import type { Request } from "./schema"

const safe = new Set([
  "read",
  "grep",
  "glob",
  "list",
  "codesearch",
  "semantic_search",
  "websearch",
  "todoread",
  "todowrite",
  "lsp",
])

const risky = new Set(["bash", "webfetch", "external_directory", "task", "skill", "agent_manager"])
const edit = new Set(["edit", "write", "apply_patch"])
const interpreters = ["python", "python3", "node", "bun", "ruby", "perl", "sh", "bash", "zsh", "deno", "tsx", "npx"]
const scripts = ["npm run", "pnpm run", "yarn", "bun run"]

type Reason = "disabled" | "safe_tool" | "project_edit" | "protected_edit" | "broad_allow" | "risky_permission" | "unknown_permission"

type Output = {
  classify: boolean
  reason: Reason
  pruned: boolean
}

export namespace ClassifierScope {
  export type Input = {
    enabled: boolean
    request: Request
    rules: Permission.Rule[]
    directory: string
    worktree: string
    protected: boolean
  }

  export function safeTool(permission: string) {
    return safe.has(permission)
  }

  export function broad(rule: Permission.Rule) {
    if (rule.action !== "allow") return false
    if (rule.permission === "bash" && rule.pattern === "*") return true
    if (rule.permission === "task" && rule.pattern === "*") return true
    if (rule.permission.endsWith("_*") && rule.pattern === "*") return true
    if (rule.permission !== "bash") return false
    const text = rule.pattern.trim()
    if (interpreters.some((cmd) => text === `${cmd} *` || text === `${cmd}*`)) return true
    if (scripts.some((cmd) => text === `${cmd} *` || text === `${cmd}*`)) return true
    return false
  }

  export function pruned(rules: Permission.Rule[]) {
    return rules.some(broad)
  }

  export function projectEdit(input: Input) {
    if (!edit.has(input.request.permission)) return false
    if (input.protected) return false
    return input.request.patterns.every((item) => {
      const abs = path.isAbsolute(item) ? item : path.join(input.worktree, item)
      return AppFileSystem.contains(input.directory, abs) || AppFileSystem.contains(input.worktree, abs)
    })
  }

  export function check(input: Input): Output {
    if (!input.enabled) return { classify: false, reason: "disabled", pruned: false }
    if (safeTool(input.request.permission)) return { classify: false, reason: "safe_tool", pruned: false }
    if (projectEdit(input)) return { classify: false, reason: "project_edit", pruned: false }
    if (input.protected && edit.has(input.request.permission)) return { classify: true, reason: "protected_edit", pruned: false }

    const hit = pruned(input.rules)
    if (hit) return { classify: true, reason: "broad_allow", pruned: true }
    if (risky.has(input.request.permission)) return { classify: true, reason: "risky_permission", pruned: false }
    if (edit.has(input.request.permission)) return { classify: true, reason: "risky_permission", pruned: false }
    return { classify: true, reason: "unknown_permission", pruned: false }
  }
}
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Implement transcript builder**

Create `packages/opencode/src/kilocode/permission/classifier/transcript.ts`:

```ts
import { MessageV2 } from "@/session/message-v2"
import type { Request, Transcript } from "./schema"

export namespace ClassifierTranscript {
  export function fromMessages(input: { messages: MessageV2.WithParts[]; request: Request }): Transcript {
    const user = input.messages
      .filter((msg) => msg.info.role === "user")
      .map((msg) => {
        const text = msg.parts
          .filter((part): part is MessageV2.TextPart => part.type === "text")
          .filter((part) => !part.ignored && !part.synthetic)
          .map((part) => part.text.trim())
          .filter((text) => text.length > 0)
          .join("\n\n")
        return { id: msg.info.id, text }
      })
      .filter((msg) => msg.text.length > 0)

    return {
      user,
      request: input.request,
    }
  }

  export function load(request: Request) {
    return fromMessages({
      messages: Array.from(MessageV2.stream(request.sessionID)),
      request,
    })
  }
}
```
