Create `packages/opencode/src/kilocode/permission/classifier/model.ts`:

```ts
import { generateObject, generateText, type LanguageModelUsage, type ProviderMetadata } from "ai"
import { mergeDeep } from "remeda"
import { z } from "zod"
import { Config } from "@/config/config"
import { Provider } from "@/provider/provider"
import { ProviderTransform } from "@/provider/transform"
import { Session } from "@/session/session"
import type { Policy, Result, Transcript } from "./schema"

const Stage2 = z.object({
  decision: z.enum(["allow", "deny"]),
  reason: z.string().min(1),
  rule: z.string().optional(),
  confidence: z.enum(["low", "medium", "high"]).optional(),
})

type RunInput = {
  cfg: Config.Info["permission_classifier"]
  policy: Policy
  transcript: Transcript
}

type RunOutput = Result & {
  usage?: LanguageModelUsage
  metadata?: ProviderMetadata
}

type Runner = (input: RunInput) => Promise<RunOutput>

let runner: Runner | undefined

function prompt(input: RunInput, final: string) {
  return [
    "You are Kilo's permission classifier. Decide whether a tool call should be blocked before execution.",
    "You must judge real-world impact and whether the user explicitly authorized the action.",
    "You see only user messages and the bare tool-call payload. Assistant prose and tool outputs are intentionally excluded.",
    "Environment:",
    ...input.policy.environment.map((item) => `- ${item}`),
    "Allow exceptions:",
    ...input.policy.allow.map((item) => `- ${item}`),
    "Soft deny rules:",
    ...input.policy.soft_deny.map((item) => `- ${item}`),
    "Hard deny rules:",
    ...input.policy.hard_deny.map((item) => `- ${item}`),
    "Classifier input JSON:",
    JSON.stringify(input.transcript, null, 2),
    final,
  ].join("\n")
}

async function resolveModel(cfg: Config.Info["permission_classifier"], stage: "stage1" | "stage2") {
  const spec = stage === "stage1" ? cfg?.stage1_model ?? cfg?.model : cfg?.stage2_model ?? cfg?.model
  if (spec) {
    const parsed = Provider.parseModel(spec)
    return Provider.getModel(parsed.providerID, parsed.modelID)
  }
  const def = await Provider.defaultModel()
  return (await Provider.getSmallModel(def.providerID)) ?? (await Provider.getModel(def.providerID, def.modelID))
}

function cost(model: Provider.Model, usage: LanguageModelUsage | undefined, metadata: ProviderMetadata | undefined) {
  if (!usage) return undefined
  return Session.getUsage({ model, usage, metadata }).cost
}

async function live(input: RunInput): Promise<RunOutput> {
  const first = await resolveModel(input.cfg, "stage1")
  const firstLanguage = await Provider.getLanguage(first)
  const firstText = await generateText({
    model: firstLanguage,
    temperature: first.capabilities.temperature ? 0 : undefined,
    providerOptions: ProviderTransform.providerOptions(first, mergeDeep(ProviderTransform.smallOptions(first), first.options)),
    maxRetries: 1,
    maxOutputTokens: 4,
    prompt: prompt(input, "Answer with exactly one token: yes to block, no to allow. Err on the side of blocking."),
    abortSignal: AbortSignal.timeout(input.cfg?.timeout ?? 10_000),
  })
  const token = firstText.text.trim().toLowerCase()
  if (token.startsWith("n")) {
    return {
      decision: "allow",
      stage: "stage1",
      reason: "Stage 1 allowed the request.",
      model: `${first.providerID}/${first.id}`,
      cost: cost(first, firstText.usage, firstText.providerMetadata),
      usage: firstText.usage,
      metadata: firstText.providerMetadata,
    }
  }
  if (!token.startsWith("y")) {
    return {
      decision: "ask",
      stage: "error",
      reason: `Stage 1 returned an unparseable decision: ${firstText.text}`,
      model: `${first.providerID}/${first.id}`,
      cost: cost(first, firstText.usage, firstText.providerMetadata),
      usage: firstText.usage,
      metadata: firstText.providerMetadata,
    }
  }

  if ((input.cfg?.mode ?? "two_stage") === "single_stage") {
    return {
      decision: "deny",
      stage: "stage1",
      reason: "Stage 1 blocked the request.",
      model: `${first.providerID}/${first.id}`,
      cost: cost(first, firstText.usage, firstText.providerMetadata),
      usage: firstText.usage,
      metadata: firstText.providerMetadata,
    }
  }

  const second = await resolveModel(input.cfg, "stage2")
  const secondLanguage = await Provider.getLanguage(second)
  const secondObject = await generateObject({
    model: secondLanguage,
    temperature: second.capabilities.temperature ? 0 : undefined,
    providerOptions: ProviderTransform.providerOptions(second, mergeDeep(ProviderTransform.smallOptions(second), second.options)),
    maxRetries: 1,
    maxOutputTokens: input.cfg?.max_tokens ?? 512,
    schema: Stage2,
    prompt: prompt(
      input,
      "Stage 1 flagged this action. Return JSON with decision allow or deny, a concise user-visible reason, the matching rule, and confidence. Do not expose chain-of-thought.",
    ),
    abortSignal: AbortSignal.timeout(input.cfg?.timeout ?? 10_000),
  })
  return {
    ...secondObject.object,
    stage: "stage2",
    model: `${second.providerID}/${second.id}`,
    cost: cost(second, secondObject.usage, secondObject.providerMetadata),
    usage: secondObject.usage,
    metadata: secondObject.providerMetadata,
  }
}

export namespace ClassifierModel {
  export function setRunner(next: Runner | undefined) {
    runner = next
  }

  export async function run(input: RunInput): Promise<RunOutput> {
    return runner ? runner(input) : live(input)
  }
}
```

The helper uses the AI SDK v6 result fields already used by this package. Keep the classifier runner isolated in this file so any SDK type mismatch is fixed here without changing permission orchestration.

        if (Exit.isFailure(exit)) expect(Cause.squash(exit.cause)).toBeInstanceOf(DeniedError)

        const permission = yield* Permission.Service
        expect(yield* permission.list()).toEqual([])
        expect(ClassifierState.get(sid).total).toBe(1)
      }),
      { git: true },
    ),
  )

  it.live("allows risky auto-approved request when classifier allows", () =>
    provideTmpdirInstance(() =>
      Effect.gen(function* () {
        ClassifierModel.setRunner(async () => ({
          decision: "allow",
          stage: "stage1",
          reason: "Safe command",
        }))

        const exit = yield* ask({}).pipe(Effect.exit)
        expect(Exit.isSuccess(exit)).toBe(true)

        const permission = yield* Permission.Service
        expect(yield* permission.list()).toEqual([])
      }),
      { git: true },
    ),
  )

  it.live("falls back to ask when classifier errors", () =>
    provideTmpdirInstance(() =>
      Effect.gen(function* () {
        ClassifierModel.setRunner(async () => {
          throw new Error("classifier unavailable")
        })

        const fiber = yield* ask({}).pipe(Effect.forkScoped)
        yield* Effect.sleep("20 millis")
        const permission = yield* Permission.Service
        const list = yield* permission.list()
        expect(list).toHaveLength(1)
        yield* permission.reply({ requestID: list[0].id, reply: "reject" })
        const exit = yield* fiber.await
        expect(Exit.isFailure(exit)).toBe(true)
      }),
      { git: true },
    ),
  )

  it.live("does not classify safe tools", () =>
    provideTmpdirInstance(() =>
      Effect.gen(function* () {
        let called = false
        ClassifierModel.setRunner(async () => {
          called = true
          return { decision: "deny", stage: "stage2", reason: "should not run" }
        })

        const exit = yield* ask({ permission: "read", patterns: ["README.md"], ruleset: [{ permission: "read", pattern: "*", action: "allow" }] }).pipe(Effect.exit)
        expect(Exit.isSuccess(exit)).toBe(true)
        expect(called).toBe(false)
      }),
      { git: true },
    ),
  )
})
```

bun test ./test/kilocode/permission/classifier/permission-hook.test.ts

- [ ] **Step 3: Implement classifier orchestration**

Create `packages/opencode/src/kilocode/permission/classifier/index.ts`:

```ts
import { Cause, Effect, Exit } from "effect"
import { Config } from "@/config/config"
import { InstanceState } from "@/effect/instance-state"
import { Permission } from "@/permission"
import { ClassifierDefaults } from "./defaults"
import { ClassifierModel } from "./model"
import { ClassifierScope } from "./scope"
import { ClassifierState } from "./state"
import { ClassifierTranscript } from "./transcript"
import { DeniedError, EscalationError, type Request } from "./schema"

export namespace PermissionClassifier {
  export const reset = ClassifierState.reset
  export const clear = ClassifierState.clear
  export const get = ClassifierState.get

  export function denied(error: unknown): error is DeniedError {
    return error instanceof DeniedError
  }

  export function escalated(error: unknown): error is EscalationError {
    return error instanceof EscalationError
  }

  export function metadata(error: unknown) {
    if (error instanceof DeniedError) {
      return {
        classifier: {
          denied: true,
          escalated: false,
          reason: error.reason,
          consecutive: error.consecutive,
          total: error.total,
        },
      }
    }
    if (error instanceof EscalationError) {
      return {
        classifier: {
          denied: true,
          escalated: true,
          reason: error.reason,
          consecutive: error.consecutive,
          total: error.total,
        },
      }
    }
    return undefined
  }

  export const evaluate = Effect.fn("PermissionClassifier.evaluate")(function* (input: {
    request: Request
    ruleset: Permission.Ruleset
    approved: Permission.Ruleset
    local: Permission.Ruleset
    protected: boolean
  }) {
    const cfg = yield* Effect.promise(() => Config.get())
    const enabled = cfg.permission_classifier?.enabled === true
    const ctx = yield* InstanceState.context
    const rules = Permission.merge(input.ruleset, input.approved, input.local)
    const scope = ClassifierScope.check({
      enabled,
      request: input.request,
      rules,
      directory: ctx.directory,
      worktree: ctx.worktree,
      protected: input.protected,
    })

    if (!scope.classify) return { decision: "allow" as const, stage: "skip" as const, reason: scope.reason }

    const policy = ClassifierDefaults.expand(cfg.permission_classifier)
    const transcript = ClassifierTranscript.load(input.request)
    const exit = yield* Effect.promise(() =>
      ClassifierModel.run({ cfg: cfg.permission_classifier, policy, transcript }),
    ).pipe(Effect.exit)

    if (Exit.isFailure(exit)) {
      return { decision: "ask" as const, stage: "error" as const, reason: Cause.squash(exit.cause).message }
    }

    const result = exit.value
    if (result.decision !== "deny") {
      ClassifierState.record({
        sessionID: input.request.sessionID,
        permission: input.request.permission,
        patterns: input.request.patterns,
        decision: result.decision,
        stage: result.stage,
        reason: result.reason,
        model: result.model,
        cost: result.cost,
      })
      return result
    }

    const record = ClassifierState.record({
      sessionID: input.request.sessionID,
      permission: input.request.permission,
      patterns: input.request.patterns,
      decision: "deny",
      stage: result.stage,
      reason: result.reason,
      model: result.model,
      cost: result.cost,
    })

    if (record.escalated) {
      return yield* new EscalationError({
        sessionID: input.request.sessionID,
        reason: result.reason,
        consecutive: record.consecutive,
        total: record.total,
      })
    }

    return yield* new DeniedError({
      sessionID: input.request.sessionID,
      permission: input.request.permission,
      patterns: input.request.patterns,
      reason: result.reason,
      consecutive: record.consecutive,
      total: record.total,
    })
  })
}
```

@Githubguy132010 What is this? I posted on the issue last night that I'm working on this. I spent 2 hours last night finalizing a spec. Please communicate actively. I am trying to do so in order to prevent duplicate work so that neither of us wastes our time.

> [@Githubguy132010](https://github.com/Githubguy132010) What is this? I posted on the issue last night that I'm working on this. I spent 2 hours last night finalizing a spec. Please communicate actively. I am trying to do so in order to prevent duplicate work so that neither of us wastes our time.

@shssoichiro my bad, just was trying to split this up into tiny little issues so we can do a PR per review process instead of 1 big PR. Sorry for wasting your time if I did so.
And I thought I'd post the plan plus spec I made.

Thanks, I appreciate the thought, although I think communicating first would have avoided some issues here. I'll review the plan tickets you created and see if they clash with what I've created locally.

> Thanks, I appreciate the thought, although I think communicating first would have avoided some issues here. I'll review the plan tickets you created and see if they clash with what I've created locally.

Alright, from now on I'll be communicating if I'm going to be doing something related to the issue.
To be clear, I'm open to collaborating as well. I'm available on the Kilo Discord as ExpedientFalcon if communicating there is easier. Apologies if I came off as rude, as well.

I reviewed your tickets and identified the following discrepancies. Overall, solid plans that were able to catch a few missing items on my end. Thanks! Here are the resolutions I arrived at:

| ID  | Discrepancy                                                                                                                                                               | Resolution                                                                                                                           | Reasoning                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| --- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| D1  | GitHub uses `permission_classifier` / `classifier`; our plan uses user-facing **Gatekeeper** and top-level `gatekeeper`.                                                  | Keep `gatekeeper`.                                                                                                                   | `permission` top-level config is already used for global permission overrides, creates confusion and implementation difficulties. Between `classifier` and `gatekeeper`, `gatekeeper` is a more recognizable term for users                                                                                                                                           |
| D2  | GitHub defaults to two-stage classifier and stage models; our plan uses single-stage approve/deny.                                                                        | Keep single-stage v1. Note two-stage out of scope/future-compatible only.                                                            | Second stage classifier is separate enough that it can be added as a follow-up feature. Original spec noted this as a stretch feature. Single-stage classifier sufficient for v1, keeping things fast and simple.                                                                                                                                                     |
| D3  | GitHub classifies only auto-approved calls and bypasses `ask` + in-project edits; our plan classifies inspected tools for `allow` and `ask`, including in-project writes. | Keep our stronger behavior.                                                                                                          | Intent of feature includes avoiding "permission fatigue", so continuing to ask user for approval frequently fails to resolve to the goal.                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| D4  | GitHub strips context to user messages + bare tool call; our plan adds context-aware agent prompt, active instructions, runtime summary.                                  | Keep our context-aware addition. Add explicit rationale: trusted instruction context allowed; assistant/tool outputs still excluded. | Context-aware functionality is a user-toggleable addition intended to alleviate the frequently reported issue class where, e.g. Ask mode writes files, and behaviors of that nature.                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| D5  | GitHub adds `hard_deny`, `$defaults`, `stage1_model`, `stage2_model`, `mode`, timeout/token fields. Our config is smaller.                                                | Keep small v1 config unless you want `$defaults`. I’d avoid stage/hard-deny fields now.                                              | We can add the additional fields later if they become necessary, but at the moment they increase the scope and don't seem to have a concrete use case. Users can already hard deny actions via global `permission` config. stage1/stage2 model fields irrelevant for now, as v1 will only include stage1 validation. Timeout/token should use global provider config. |
| D6  | Original/GitHub includes CLI inspection commands: defaults/config/critique/status. Our plan lacks separate CLI story.                                                     | Add new ticket: **Add Gatekeeper CLI inspection commands** using `kilo gatekeeper ...`, not `classifier`.                            | Solid addition to ensure plan covers complete requirements.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| D7  | GitHub has VS Code settings UI for enable/model/policy lists. Our KILO-6 only covers status/decisions/cost.                                                               | Add ticket: **Add Gatekeeper settings UI**.                                                                                          | Solid addition to ensure plan covers complete requirements.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| D8  | GitHub prompt-injection probe is local opencode heuristic before context; our plan makes it Gateway-side with optional model probe.                                       | Keep Gateway-side design. Add explicit non-goal: no local opencode probe in current scope.                                           | Gateway-side probe is in line with original spec. Client side probe can possibly be a later feature for use with non-Kilo providers, but will be less powerful because it will be limited to only the heuristic, no model-based followup.                                                                                                                             |
| D9  | Counter reset semantics differ: GitHub resets consecutive only; our wording says reset counters on user turn.                                                             | Update to: user turn resets consecutive only; total persists per session so 20-total escalation works.                               | Clarification is inline with original spec: "At 3 consecutive or 20 total denials, halt and escalate to the human"                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
Alright, looks good. Will you handle the PR's?
Yes, I'll be working on the implementation over the next several days.

title: Track permission classifier rollout

body:
## Summary

Reduce streaming and session-update noise so token streaming remains responsive while long-running tools and large sessions do not flood the event pipeline, database projection, or UI state.

## Problem

Model text/reasoning deltas should stay fast and smooth, but the current session pipeline can emit noisy full-part updates for tool progress and other high-frequency state changes. Chatty tools can repeatedly update tool metadata, which creates larger session events and unnecessary UI work. Large sessions and concurrent child sessions amplify this overhead.

We need to keep model-token deltas lightweight while coalescing non-token progress updates and reducing full-part replacement churn.

## Requirements

### Backend / CLI

- Keep text and reasoning token streaming as lightweight deltas; do not batch model-token deltas in the backend.
- Throttle/coalesce chatty tool progress updates separately from model-token streaming.
- Add a Kilo-owned helper for tool progress coalescing, for example `packages/opencode/src/kilocode/tool/progress.ts`.
- Use that helper in the bash tool path so `ctx.metadata(...)` is not called for every output chunk.
  - Candidate hot path: `packages/opencode/src/tool/bash.ts` around streaming output handling.
- Always flush the final progress snapshot before a tool completes, errors, or is aborted.
- Keep final `tool-result` / completed part state authoritative; coalescing must not lose final output or metadata.
- Prefer small preview updates for running tools; avoid repeatedly publishing large full tool parts while a command is still running.
- Preserve existing full output truncation and output-path behavior.
- Count and optionally expose per-turn event volume for debugging:
  - `message.part.delta` count
  - `message.part.updated` count
  - average serialized event bytes
  - tool metadata update count by tool

### Event protocol and session state

- Reuse existing `message.part.delta` where possible for append-only text-like updates.
- Avoid adding new event types unless the existing delta event cannot express the update safely.
- If tool-output preview deltas are added, include enough field/path information so clients do not treat every delta as assistant text.
- Ensure `message.part.updated` remains the authoritative state replacement when a part starts, completes, errors, or receives non-append metadata.
- Avoid writing transient per-chunk tool progress to persistent session storage unless needed for recovery.

### VS Code / webview

- Preserve the current focused-session stream scheduler behavior for model text deltas.
- Extend part-delta handling so it can distinguish assistant text/reasoning deltas from tool-output preview deltas.
  - Candidate files:
    - `packages/kilo-vscode/src/kilo-provider-utils.ts`
    - `packages/kilo-vscode/src/kilo-provider/session-stream-scheduler.ts`
    - `packages/kilo-vscode/webview-ui/src/context/session.tsx`
    - `packages/kilo-vscode/webview-ui/src/types/messages/parts.ts`
- Do not remount tool UI for every streamed metadata/progress update.
- Keep background session updates more aggressively coalesced than focused-session updates.
- Flush queued deltas before applying authoritative snapshots from message reload/reconcile.

## Acceptance criteria

- A command producing thousands of stdout/stderr chunks causes a bounded number of UI/progress updates per second.
- Assistant text/reasoning token streaming remains smooth and low-latency.
- Final tool output, exit status, truncation notice, and output path remain correct.
- Running tool previews update at a reasonable cadence without flooding `message.part.updated` events.
- Large sessions with active child sessions remain responsive in the VS Code UI.
- Focused session streaming remains near real-time; background session streaming is coalesced.
- Existing session replay/reload still reconstructs final message and part state correctly.

## Suggested tests

- Add a CLI test for the progress coalescer that simulates thousands of chunks and asserts bounded update count plus final flush.
- Add a bash-tool/session test verifying final output and metadata are preserved after throttled progress updates.
- Add VS Code unit tests for `mapSSEEventToWebviewMessage` and the session reducer when receiving text/reasoning deltas vs. tool-output preview deltas.
- Add a scheduler test verifying focused vs. background session coalescing remains correct.

## Non-goals

- Reducing model output token rate.
- Batching or dropping assistant text/reasoning deltas.
- Changing tool permissions.
- Changing model/provider selection.
- Changing billing behavior.
title: perf(cli): reduce streaming and session update noise

body:
## Summary

Reduce fixed input-token overhead from the default tool surface by exposing fewer tool schemas by default and making additional tools progressively available when needed.

## Problem

Every active tool schema and description contributes to the model request prefix. A broad default tool surface creates recurring input-token cost even when many tools are irrelevant to the current task. Large tool descriptions and optional tools can also slow requests and reduce context available for actual session history.

## Requirements

- Measure serialized tool definition overhead per request, including total bytes and top tools by schema/description size.
- Add a minimal/default-low-overhead mode or profile that exposes only core tools unless the task needs more.
- Keep core file-editing functionality available in the low-overhead profile.
- Make optional tools opt-in or agent-scoped where possible, including task/subagent, web, skill, todo, recall/search/indexing, LSP, MCP, and suggestion/question tools.
- Ensure tool descriptions are concise and avoid duplicating workflow guidance already present in agent prompts or skills.
- Add diagnostics so users/support can see which enabled tools are responsible for fixed context overhead.
- Preserve existing richer agent modes for workflows that need them.

## Acceptance criteria

- A low-overhead agent/profile can run with a materially smaller active tool definition payload than the default rich mode.
- Tool definition overhead is visible in diagnostics by tool name.
- Optional tools do not appear in the model-facing tool list unless enabled by mode, config, or task need.
- Existing default behavior can remain available for users who want the richer workflow.
- MCP and marketplace-installed tools remain supported but do not silently dominate context without diagnostics.

## Suggested implementation areas

- Tool registry and filtering:
  - `packages/opencode/src/tool/registry.ts`
  - `packages/opencode/src/session/llm.ts`
  - `packages/opencode/src/session/prompt.ts`
- Kilo-owned policy/preset helpers:
  - `packages/opencode/src/kilocode/agent/index.ts`
  - `packages/opencode/src/kilocode/tool/registry.ts`
  - new helper under `packages/opencode/src/kilocode/tool/overhead.ts` or similar
- Diagnostics/metrics:
  - include tool schema byte counts in session metrics / context observer work

## Non-goals

- Removing tools from Kilo.
- Breaking existing agents that rely on rich tool access.
- Changing permissions semantics.
- Changing model/provider selection.

comments.body: Implementation note: make sure tool-surface optimizations work well with smaller, less capable models too, not only frontier models. Low-overhead profiles should not hide capabilities behind discovery flows that require advanced planning. Small models need clear core tools, explicit affordances for finding optional tools, and predictable fallback behavior.

title: perf(cli): reduce default tool schema overhead

body:
## Summary

Optimize skill handling so installed skills stay discoverable without adding unnecessary context to every model request.

## Problem

Skills are useful, but their always-visible metadata and loaded instruction content can become a hidden context and cost driver when many skills are installed or when a loaded skill remains in active history longer than needed.

We need clearer limits and observability around skill context overhead while preserving on-demand skill use.

## Requirements

- Keep skill discovery progressive: always-visible context should include only compact skill metadata, not full skill bodies.
- Ensure full skill instructions enter context only when a skill is explicitly invoked or selected as relevant.
- Measure and expose the estimated context contribution from:
  - skill list metadata
  - full loaded skill content
  - repeated/duplicate skill guidance across prompts, instructions, and tool descriptions
- Add warnings or diagnostics when skill metadata or loaded skill content exceeds a reasonable budget.
- Avoid duplicating the same workflow guidance in the system prompt, agent prompt, tool description, and skill body.
- Make loaded skill impact visible in session diagnostics so users/support can see which step loaded a skill and how much context it added.
- Keep behavior compatible with existing skill locations and marketplace-installed skills.

## Acceptance criteria

- A session with many installed skills keeps the always-visible skill listing compact.
- Full skill content is not included in every request by default.
- When a skill is loaded, diagnostics can identify the skill name, load step, and approximate context size added.
- Large skill descriptions or loaded skill bodies surface a clear warning or diagnostic.
- Context/cost analysis can categorize skill overhead separately from system prompt, tool schemas, user messages, tool outputs, and other context.
- Existing skill invocation behavior continues to work.

## Non-goals

- Removing skills.
- Changing marketplace installation behavior.
- Exposing raw private skill content in shared/public diagnostics.
- Rewriting the overall prompt architecture.

comments.body: Implementation note: make sure these skill optimizations work well with smaller, less capable models too, not only frontier models. Progressive skill discovery should stay explicit and easy to follow, with enough compact metadata for small models to pick relevant skills reliably without requiring strong implicit reasoning.

title: perf(cli): keep skill context overhead bounded

body:
## Problem
The CLI startup takes 4-5 seconds before the standard first screen is displayed. This is a noticeable delay that impacts the user experience.

## Goal
Profile the CLI startup sequence to identify what is consuming time during startup, broken down by component with percentage of total startup time.

## Proposed Solution
Implement a startup profiler that:
- Instruments the key stages of the CLI startup sequence (e.g. config loading, MCP server connections, plugin/extension init, API client setup, etc.)
- Measures the elapsed time for each stage
- Reports the breakdown of startup time by component, including the percentage of total startup time each component takes
- Allows the profiling output to be used to prioritise optimisation efforts

## Acceptance Criteria
- A startup profile report can be generated (e.g. via a flag or environment variable)
- The report shows each startup stage, its duration, and its percentage of total startup time
- Findings are documented so the team can act on the biggest contributors to startup latency

labels.name: proposal
labels.description: A community member proposes an improvement and willing to do a Pull Request, if accepted

title: Analyse and profile CLI startup speed

body:
## Summary

Currently, all tools (built-in + every MCP tool from every connected server) are sent to the model on every single request. This is inefficient in terms of context usage, cost, and performance — especially when many MCP servers are connected.

Anthropic's API supports a **tool search** capability that allows dynamic, lazy loading of tools rather than eagerly including all tool definitions in every request. This could significantly reduce context size and improve performance.

Reference: https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/agents-and-tools/tool-use/tool-search-tool

## Current Behavior

- All MCP servers connect at session startup
- All tool definitions (name, description, input schema) for every connected tool are injected into every model request
- There is no filtering, lazy loading, or dynamic shortlisting of tools

## Proposed Behavior

Implement support for Anthropic's `tool_search_tool` beta feature:

- Instead of sending all tool definitions upfront, provide the model with a tool-search mechanism
- The model can query for relevant tools based on the current task/context
- Only the tools actually needed are loaded into context dynamically
- This applies to both built-in tools and MCP-provided tools

## Expected Benefits

- **Reduced context usage** — fewer tokens consumed per request when many tools are available
- **Lower cost** — smaller prompts mean lower API costs
- **Better performance** — less data sent per request
- **Scalability** — users with many MCP servers connected will see the most improvement

## Implementation Notes

- Check Anthropic beta header requirements for `tool_search_tool`
- Each tool should have a searchable description and title
- Tool definitions would be loaded on-demand rather than all at startup
- Consider fallback behavior for non-Anthropic providers that don't support this feature

## References

- https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/agents-and-tools/tool-use/tool-search-tool

comments.body: Implementation note: make sure this optimization works well with smaller, less capable models too, not only frontier models. Tool search and lazy loading should have simple, explicit discovery affordances, clear fallback behavior, and enough metadata for small models to reliably find the right tool without needing sophisticated planning.

title: Implement Tool Search for MCP Tools and Built-in Tools

body:
## Description
When diffs are loaded in the extension's diff viewer and Agent Manager (AM), there is a frequent small but noticeable delay before the diff content is displayed.

## Expected Behavior
Diffs should load and render quickly with minimal or no perceptible delay.

## Actual Behavior
There are 2-3 delays observed when loading diffs, making the experience feel sluggish.

## Context
- Affects both the extension diff viewer and Agent Manager (AM)
- Multiple delays (2-3) have been identified in the loading process
title: Diff viewer in Agent Manager has a noticeable loading delay

body:
## Description
In the extension's diff viewer and Agent Manager, the message "Diff preview loads on demand" is sometimes shown even when the diff is small and should be rendered immediately without requiring user interaction.

## Expected Behavior
Small diffs should be rendered and displayed automatically without showing the "Diff preview loads on demand" placeholder.

## Actual Behavior
The "Diff preview loads on demand" message appears even for small diffs that should be auto-loaded.

## Context
- Affects both the extension diff viewer and Agent Manager (AM)
title: Diff viewer shows 'Diff preview loads on demand' for small diffs

body:
## Feature Request

Users have requested automatic support for `DESIGN.md` files in Kilo.

### Proposed behavior

When a `DESIGN.md` file exists in the project, Kilo should automatically load or reference it for design-related tasks. Two approaches have been suggested:

1. **Skill-based approach (preferred):** Treat `DESIGN.md` as an auto-loadable skill, similar to how other skills work.
2. **Hardcoded fallback:** If the file exists, add a hint to the system prompt so the LLM knows to load it when handling design tasks.
title: Add automatic support for DESIGN.md

body:
## Description
When a diff view is already open in the chat and a new diff is opened, the view often remains scrolled to the previous position (below the content). The expected behavior is that the view should always scroll to the top when a new diff is opened.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Open a diff view in the chat (tool diff view)
2. Scroll down in the diff
3. Open a new diff view

## Expected Behavior
The new diff view should always be scrolled to the top when opened.

## Actual Behavior
The view remains at the previous scroll position, often showing content below the visible area.

## Context
This applies to tool diff views displayed in the chat interface.
title: Diff view should scroll to top when a new diff is opened

body:
### Description

Every Kilo session — whether opened via the VS Code extension or a terminal tab — 
spawns its own independent Bun process with no sharing between instances.

**Observed behaviour:**
- VS Code extension spawns one Bun process on startup
- Each additional terminal session (e.g. Warp tab running `kilo`) spawns another
- Each process consumes ~500MB+ at baseline, before any active task

**Impact:**
Running even a modest multi-agent setup (3–5 concurrent sessions) consumes 1.5–2.5GB 
of RAM on process overhead alone, before model I/O or context is factored in. 

**Expected behaviour:**
A single shared `kilo serve` daemon should be reusable across sessions. 
Per `AGENTS.md`, clients can either spawn *or connect to* a `kilo serve` process — 
a shared daemon mode (or at minimum a warning about per-session memory cost) 
would make multi-agent workflows viable on normal hardware.

### Plugins

_No response_

### Kilo version

7.2.34

### Steps to reproduce

1. Open up VS Code with the Kilo extension installed or navigate to repo and run kilo cli from terminal.
2. Open task manager and search for Bun processes.
3. Open a new tab on terminal and run kilo on new tab and observe task manager as new Bun processes get spawned every time a new kilo session is started.

### Screenshot and/or share link

https://drive.google.com/file/d/1843aH1ZjOGnDbQ8w1KZOc8YBvpiRORVU/view?usp=drive_link


### Operating System

Windows 11

### Terminal

Warp

Agent manager is currently the only product that uses a single bun process

@LuTheZy, I am currently implementing a system daemon for instance cross-sharing. I will message you as soon as I have a functional branch if you want to try it.

> > [@marius-kilocode](https://github.com/marius-kilocode) So this is by design and there's no plans to support sharing a single bun process outside of the agent manager?
>

Great, that sounds promising.

Hey @LuTheZy  ! I’m excited to share that I’ve released the daemon mode as an experimental feature. 
Right now, it’s only available for the CLI/TUI. 
You can get started by running:

```

Once it’s up and running, any other `kilo` instances you open will connect to the same server.

Let me know if you have any questions or feedback! 😊

body:
### Feature hasn't been suggested before.

- [x] I have verified this feature I'm about to request hasn't been suggested before.

### Describe the enhancement you want to request

Related to #9611 
Request: Add a follow-up mode to task so an orchestrator can send additional messages into an existing subagent session (foreground or background), rather than always spinning up a new one.

Why this matters:

Token / cache efficiency
Today every task is a cold start. If the parent wants to redirect or refine a subagent, it has to re-summarize prior work in the prompt. A follow-up mode lets the child keep its conversation history and KV cache warm, so subsequent turns are small and cheap — subagent follow-ups hit cached input tokens (~10× cheaper) instead of full re-prompting.

Orchestrator output reduction
The parent no longer needs to emit long recaps or restate full context in every delegation. That significantly cuts expensive orchestrator output tokens, which are often the costliest part of multi-agent flows.

Long workflows become practical
Multi-step audits, refactors, or feature work quickly blow up context windows when each subagent turn rehydrates the full state. Keeping a persistent subagent session fixes this.

Feature parity
Copilot CLI's multi-agent orchestration and Claude/Codex-style harnesses already do this: spin up a specialist agent, then send multi-turn follow-ups into the same session. Kilo has the right architecture (orchestrator + task + background agents); adding session-scoped follow-ups would make it competitive on real-world, long-running workflows.
title: [FEATURE] Multi - turn subagent/task

body:
### Feature hasn't been suggested before.

- [x] I have verified this feature I'm about to request hasn't been suggested before.

### Describe the enhancement you want to request

## Feature request

Please add support for custom BYOK OpenAI-compatible upstreams in Kilo Gateway.

Kilo Code already supports custom OpenAI-compatible providers in the client, but Gateway workflows appear to depend on Kilo’s hosted model catalog/routing layer. I’d like to use existing subscriptions/keys from providers such as Synthetic and CrofAI through the Gateway, so they work consistently across model picker, cloud/review workflows, and any Gateway-backed automation.

## Proposed behavior

Allow users or organizations to register a custom OpenAI-compatible upstream with:
- Provider ID, e.g. `synthetic`, `crofai`, `my-vllm`
- Display name
- Base URL, e.g. `https://.../v1`
- API key / BYOK secret
- Optional custom headers
- Model discovery via `/v1/models` when available
- Manual model metadata fallback when discovery is unavailable or incomplete:
  - model id
  - context window
  - max output tokens
  - tool support
  - vision support
  - reasoning support if applicable
  - pricing fields, if Kilo needs cost display/accounting
Models could then appear as normal Gateway model IDs, for example:

- `custom-provider/model-id`

## Why this matters

Client-side custom providers are useful for local/manual usage, but they don’t fully solve Gateway-backed workflows. If the Gateway is the routing layer for model selection, review automation, hosted/cloud sessions, or organization-level configuration, custom BYOK upstreams need to exist at the Gateway level too.

This would let users bring existing subscriptions and private gateways without requiring Kilo to add every provider as a hardcoded integration.

## Initial providers/examples
The two concrete providers I’m interested in are:

- Synthetic
- CrofAI

Both are useful examples because they expose OpenAI-compatible model access and are subscription/provider relationships users may already have outside of Kilo.

## Possible implementation shape

A generic OpenAI-compatible BYOK upstream would probably be more valuable than adding Synthetic/CrofAI as one-off providers. Synthetic and CrofAI could just be examples or presets on top of the same custom-provider plumbing.

comments.body:
I’d like to support this request as it aligns perfectly with the need for more flexibility in Kilo.

While the author mentioned Synthetic and CrofAI, I believe the most robust approach is indeed a fully generic OpenAI-compatible implementation. Personally, I am looking to integrate providers like Orcarouter (similar to OpenRouter), which aggregate multiple models through a single API.

Adding a generic provider would solve several issues at once:
Maintenance Efficiency: The maintainers won't need to add a specific integration for every new provider that pops up.
 Local Workflows: It would allow seamless integration of local inference servers (like Ollama or vLLM instances) directly into the Gateway, not just the client.

Future-Proofing: Any provider following the /v1/chat/completions standard becomes immediately compatible.

The suggestion of Model Metadata Fallback (context window, tool support) is crucial here because many generic or aggregated providers don't always expose complete metadata via /v1/models.

I would love to see this moving forward to make Kilo a truly agnostic gateway

title: [FEATURE]: Add Gateway support for BYOK custom OpenAI-compatible upstreams

body:
## Summary

The main Kilo VS Code extension's **Diff Viewer** has a diff-source selector that lets users switch between **Branch / Staged / Unstaged / Session** views. The **Agent Manager** panel, however, only shows a single workspace-vs-base-branch diff for each worktree session. We should bring the same selector (and corresponding data sources) into the Agent Manager web UI so users can inspect staged, unstaged, and session-scoped diffs per worktree without leaving the Agent Manager.

## Motivation

When working in an Agent Manager worktree, users frequently want to:

- See **only what the agent changed in this session** (independent of any pre-existing local changes in the worktree).
- See what is currently **staged** for the next commit.
- See **unstaged** changes (including untracked files) — important because Kilo's plan files in `.kilo/plans/*.md` are gitignored by default in user projects, so they show up as untracked rather than as part of the workspace diff. Today they are effectively invisible in the Agent Manager diff view.
- Quickly switch perspectives without opening VS Code's native SCM view or the standalone Diff Viewer panel.

## How this is implemented today (in the extension)

Investigation of `packages/kilo-vscode/`:

### 1. Available diff modes

Defined in `packages/kilo-vscode/src/diff/sources/types.ts`:

```ts
export type DiffSourceType = "workspace" | "session" | "turn" | "staged" | "unstaged"
```

`DiffSourceCatalog.listAvailable()` (`src/diff/sources/catalog.ts`) emits `workspace` + `staged` + `unstaged` whenever a workspace root exists, plus `session` when a session id is present. `turn` is reserved for inline "diff of this turn" entry points and is never shown in the picker.

### 2. UI selector

- `packages/kilo-vscode/webview-ui/diff-viewer/DiffPickerHeader.tsx` — renders the `<Select>` dropdown grouped into **Session** and **Git** sections. Labels/tooltips come from `webview-ui/src/i18n/en.ts` (`diffViewer.source.<type>.label` / `.tooltip`).
- `packages/kilo-vscode/webview-ui/diff-viewer/DiffViewerApp.tsx` — root component. On selection it posts `{ type: "selectSource", id }` to the extension host.

### 3. Data sources / git plumbing

Each source lives in `packages/kilo-vscode/src/diff/sources/`:

| Source | File | Underlying commands / API |
|---|---|---|
| `workspace` ("Branch") | `worktree.ts` + `shared/local-diff.ts` | `git merge-base HEAD <base>` → `git diff --name-status/--numstat --no-renames <merge-base>`; `git show <base>:<file>` for before, disk for after; `git ls-files --others --exclude-standard` for untracked. Supports revert. |
| `staged` | `staged.ts` | `git diff --cached --name-status/--numstat --no-renames HEAD`; before = `git show HEAD:<file>`, after = `git show :<file>` (index blob). |
| `unstaged` | `unstaged.ts` | `git diff --name-status/--numstat --no-renames`; before = `git show :<file>`, after = disk; untracked via `git ls-files --others --exclude-standard`. |
| `session` | `session.ts` | CLI SDK call: `client.session.diff({ sessionID, directory })` — uses per-turn snapshots. Stops polling when `config.snapshot === false`. |
| `turn` | `turn.ts` | CLI SDK call: `client.session.message({ sessionID, messageID, directory })`, reads `info.summary?.diffs`. One-shot. |

### 4. Fetch + display pipeline

- `packages/kilo-vscode/src/diff/SourceController.ts` is the coordinator. It builds the active source via `DiffSourceCatalog.build()`, polls every **2500 ms**, hashes results to de-duplicate pushes, and posts:
  - `setAvailableSources { descriptors, currentId }`
  - `diffViewer.diffs { diffs }`
  - `diffViewer.diffFile { file, diff }` (lazy per-file detail)
  - `diffViewer.loading`, `diffViewer.capabilities`
- `packages/kilo-vscode/src/diff/DiffViewerProvider.ts` handles webview → host messages: `selectSource`, `diffViewer.requestFile`, `diffViewer.revertFile`, `diffViewer.requestBranches`, `diffViewer.setBaseBranch`, `diffViewer.setMarkdownRender`, `diffViewer.close`.
- Rendering: `webview-ui/diff-viewer/DiffViewerApp.tsx` → `DiffViewerShell` → `DiffViewerContent` → `webview-ui/agent-manager/FullScreenDiffView.tsx` (shared with Agent Manager) → `<Diff>` from `@kilocode/kilo-ui/diff`. Markdown files can use `MarkdownDiffView`.

### 5. Agent Manager today

`packages/kilo-vscode/src/agent-manager/worktree-diff-controller.ts` already reuses `SourceController` but constructs a **single hard-coded `DiffSource`** (workspace vs resolved base branch) per session. It does not expose the catalog or honor `selectSource` per worktree. The Agent Manager webview (`webview-ui/agent-manager/DiffPanel.tsx`, `FullScreenDiffView.tsx`) only displays that one source — there is no picker.

This means:

- `WorktreeDiffController` already has the right shape to be generalized.
- `FullScreenDiffView` is the same renderer the standalone diff viewer uses, so the display side needs no changes — only the source selection + IPC wiring.

## Requirements / acceptance criteria

1. **Per-session diff source selector in the Agent Manager**
   - Each worktree session in the Agent Manager exposes a selector with at least: **Branch**, **Staged**, **Unstaged**, **Session**.
   - Reuse `DiffPickerHeader` (or a shared component) and the existing i18n keys (`diffViewer.source.*`, `diffViewer.group.*`) so labels/tooltips stay consistent with the standalone Diff Viewer.
   - The default selection on first open remains the current behavior (workspace vs base branch).
   - Selection is remembered per-session for the duration of the panel (does not need to persist across reloads in v1).

2. **Reuse existing data sources unmodified**
   - Generalize `WorktreeDiffController` to build sources via `DiffSourceCatalog` against the worktree's directory + session id, instead of hard-coding workspace.
   - All git work must happen against the **worktree's** working directory (not the parent repo).
   - Keep the existing polling cadence and dedupe hashing in `SourceController`.

3. **Untracked / gitignored plan files must be visible**
   - `.kilo/plans/*.md` are gitignored by default in user projects, so any agent-authored plan file lives as an untracked file in the worktree.
   - These files **must appear in the Unstaged view** (which already includes untracked via `git ls-files --others --exclude-standard`) and in the Session view (snapshot-based, ignores gitignore).
   - Add a test that confirms an untracked `.kilo/plans/foo.md` appears in both the Unstaged and Session sources for a worktree session.

4. **IPC**
   - Extend Agent Manager out-messages with the equivalents of `setAvailableSources` / source-scoped diff updates, scoped by `sessionId`.
   - Add an in-message `agentManager.selectDiffSource { sessionId, id }` handled by `WorktreeDiffController.activate(sessionId, id)`.

5. **Revert behavior**
   - Revert remains available only on `workspace` (matches the standalone viewer's `capabilities.revert`).
   - Apply / merge / PR flows that today consume the workspace diff must continue to use the workspace source regardless of what the user has selected for viewing.

6. **Tests**
   - Unit tests for the generalized `WorktreeDiffController` covering each source type.
   - Integration test for the new `selectDiffSource` IPC message.
   - Webview test confirming the picker only appears when more than one source is available and that selection round-trips through IPC.

7. **Docs**
   - Update `packages/kilo-docs/` Agent Manager page with a short section on the diff source selector, mirroring the wording in the standalone Diff Viewer docs.

## Out of scope

- Persisting selection across reloads.
- Surfacing the `turn` source in the Agent Manager picker (it remains an inline entry point only).
- Changes to the `<Diff>` renderer or `FullScreenDiffView` layout.

## Pointers (extension code)

- Diff sources: `packages/kilo-vscode/src/diff/sources/{types,catalog,worktree,staged,unstaged,session,turn}.ts`
- Coordinator: `packages/kilo-vscode/src/diff/SourceController.ts`
- Standalone viewer host: `packages/kilo-vscode/src/diff/DiffViewerProvider.ts`
- Standalone viewer UI: `packages/kilo-vscode/webview-ui/diff-viewer/{DiffViewerApp,DiffPickerHeader}.tsx`
- Agent Manager host (to generalize): `packages/kilo-vscode/src/agent-manager/worktree-diff-controller.ts`
- Agent Manager UI (to host the picker): `packages/kilo-vscode/webview-ui/agent-manager/{DiffPanel,FullScreenDiffView}.tsx`
- i18n: `packages/kilo-vscode/webview-ui/src/i18n/en.ts` (`diffViewer.source.*`, `diffViewer.group.*`)

---

Built for [Marius Wichtner](https://kilo-code.slack.com/archives/C0A4SA041DE/p1778617559684479?thread_ts=1778612095.380599&cid=C0A4SA041DE) by [Kilo for Slack](https://kilo.ai/slack)

comments.body: This feels like the right direction so it's transparent what is being shown and you can select based on what is relevant to things being edited. We also don't end up doing things like still showing a subset of dirs on an otherwise git-oriented view when they are ignored.

title: feat(agent-manager): add staged/unstaged/session diff selector to Agent Manager

body:
### Description

When using Kilo Code 7.2.52 with the OpenAI / ChatGPT Plus-Pro subscription provider, selecting GPT-5.5 causes even a minimal prompt such as hi to fail with:

An error occurred while processing your request.

The task appears to retry but does not complete after more than 5 minutes.

The same setup works without issue when GPT-5.4 is selected.

The failure occurs across both the Code agent and the Plan agent. It also occurs with both medium and extra high processing/reasoning settings for each agent.

This issue has also been observed on prior Kilo Code 7.2.x versions, not only 7.2.52. Based on repeated testing, this appears to be a persistent 7.2.x regression or provider-handling defect affecting the GPT-5.5 model path under the OpenAI ChatGPT Plus/Pro subscription provider.

This does not appear to be a general OpenAI authentication issue or a general Kilo issue, because GPT-5.4 works through the same provider/account.

### Plugins

_No response_

### Kilo version

7.2.52

### Steps to reproduce

_No response_

### Screenshot and/or share link

Provider / Model / Agent Settings

Provider: OpenAI / ChatGPT Plus-Pro subscription route

Model failing: GPT-5.5

Model working: GPT-5.4

Agents affected:

Code agent

Plan agent

Processing/reasoning settings affected:

Medium

Extra high

VS Code Environment

Version: 1.109.5
Commit: 072586267e68ece9a47aa43f8c108e0dcbf44622
Date: 2026-02-19T19:43:32.382Z
Electron: 39.3.0
ElectronBuildId: 13313481
Chromium: 142.0.7444.265
Node.js: 22.21.1
V8: 14.2.231.22-electron.0
OS: Linux x64 5.15.0-176-generic

Steps to Reproduce

Open VS Code with Kilo Code 7.2.52 installed.

Select the OpenAI / ChatGPT Plus-Pro subscription provider.

Select GPT-5.5 as the model.

Select either the Code agent or the Plan agent.

Select either medium or extra high processing/reasoning.

Start a new task.

Enter the prompt:

hi

Observe that Kilo reports:

An error occurred while processing your request.

The task retries but does not complete after more than 5 minutes.

Repeat with the other affected combinations:

Code agent + medium processing/reasoning

Code agent + extra high processing/reasoning

Plan agent + medium processing/reasoning

Plan agent + extra high processing/reasoning

Observe that the same failure occurs for each combination when GPT-5.5 is selected.

Switch the model to GPT-5.4 using the same provider/account.

Enter the same prompt:

hi

Observe that the request completes normally.

Expected Behavior

Kilo should successfully send a simple prompt to GPT-5.5 through the OpenAI ChatGPT Plus/Pro subscription provider and receive a normal response.

At minimum, if GPT-5.5 is unavailable, unsupported, or rejected by the underlying OpenAI/Codex subscription path, Kilo should display the actual provider/backend error instead of retrying indefinitely or timing out after several minutes.

Actual Behavior

GPT-5.5 fails even with a minimal prompt.

The failure occurs with both the Code agent and the Plan agent.

The failure occurs with both medium and extra high processing/reasoning settings.

Kilo displays:

An error occurred while processing your request.

The task retries but does not complete after more than 5 minutes.

GPT-5.4 works normally using the same OpenAI subscription provider.

Why This Appears Model-Specific

The issue does not appear to be a general authentication or provider configuration problem because:

The OpenAI ChatGPT Plus/Pro provider is authenticated and usable.

GPT-5.4 works through the same provider route.

The failure occurs only when GPT-5.5 is selected.

The prompt hi is minimal, so this is unlikely to be caused by task complexity, context size, tool use, or prompt formatting.

The failure occurs across multiple agents and processing/reasoning levels, so it does not appear isolated to one Kilo agent mode or one reasoning setting.

Requested Fix

Please fix Kilo Code's handling of GPT-5.5 through the OpenAI ChatGPT Plus/Pro subscription provider in the 7.2.x release line.

The model is selectable in the UI, but a trivial prompt fails at runtime while GPT-5.4 works through the same provider/account. The failure occurs across the Code agent and Plan agent, and across medium and extra high processing/reasoning settings. If GPT-5.5 is exposed as a selectable model, Kilo should be able to complete a simple request through that provider route.

At minimum, Kilo should not silently retry for more than 5 minutes behind a generic error message. If the underlying OpenAI/Codex provider returns a rejection, unsupported-model response, unavailable-model response, or other backend error, Kilo should surface the actual error and stop retrying when the failure is not recoverable.

Specific fixes requested:

Correct the GPT-5.5 model identifier and request path used by the OpenAI ChatGPT Plus/Pro provider.

Ensure the subscription-provider route supports GPT-5.5 consistently when the model is exposed in the Kilo UI.

Surface the raw backend/provider error instead of replacing it with only An error occurred while processing your request.

Stop retrying indefinitely or for excessive periods when the backend response is non-recoverable.

Add or update regression coverage so a minimal prompt to GPT-5.5 through the subscription provider does not fail while GPT-5.4 succeeds.

Workaround

Use GPT-5.4 through the same OpenAI ChatGPT Plus/Pro subscription provider. GPT-5.4 works without issue in the same environment.

Additional Notes

This appears to be a mismatch between the model list exposed in the Kilo UI and the actual runtime behavior of the OpenAI ChatGPT Plus/Pro subscription provider path.

The model is selectable, but runtime requests fail. Because this has been reproduced across prior 7.2.x versions and because GPT-5.4 works through the same provider/account, this should be treated as a GPT-5.5 provider-path bug in the 7.2.x release line rather than as a user configuration issue.



### Operating System

Ubuntu 24.04

### Terminal

_No response_

comments.body:
We are almost a month into after GPT 5.5 has been released, but it is still unusable.

Bump.

The following is an error with GPT-5.5 model but is GPT-5.4 works without issue, 

Invalid JSON schema: regex lookaround is not supported.
Found at $['$defs'].SplitDebitLine.properties.amount.anyOf[1].pattern.

Error type: invalid_request_error
Code: invalid_json_schema
Status: 400
Request ID: [redacted]
Cookie headers: [redacted]
Cloudflare headers: [redacted]

I have retested this on 7.3.50, and the issue still exists.

The bigger issue is no longer just that GPT-5.5 does not work through the OpenAI ChatGPT Plus/Pro provider route. Bugs happen. The real concern is that this was posted on May 12, the underlying problem has existed for roughly two months, and there has been no visible response, assignment, triage, linked fix, or acknowledgement from the Kilo team.

If GPT-5.5 does not work through this provider path, it should not be shown as supported through this provider path. A broken model option in a dropdown is not support.

The public signal right now is simple: GPT-5.5 is shown as available, it does not work, and Kilo has not responded after almost 6 weeks. **That raises a real concern about whether Kilo can be relied on as a serious development platform.**

title: GPT-5.5 via OpenAI ChatGPT Plus/Pro provider fails across 7.2.x with simple prompt; GPT-5.4 works

body:
## Summary
Users want the ability to drag a file from outside VSCode (e.g. from the Desktop or Finder/Explorer) directly into the Kilo extension chat input box, so the agent can read and work with the file contents — without adding the file to the repository.

## Use Case
A user wants to, for example, drag a CSV file from their Desktop into the chat box and say: _"Look at this CSV and the URLs in it, fix all the broken links."_ The file should be available to the agent as context only — it should not be committed or added to the repo.

## Current Behaviour
- Shift+drag works for files already inside VSCode/the workspace, but dragging files from outside VSCode (Desktop, Finder, Explorer) does not result in the file being attached or visible in the chat input.

## Expected Behaviour
- A user can drag a file from their OS file manager (Finder, Explorer, Nautilus, etc.) into the chat input box.
- The file is shown as attached in the chat input (similar to how images can be attached).
- The agent can read the file contents and use them as context.
- The file is **not** added to the workspace or repository.

## Investigation Needed
Please explore and document options for implementing this:
1. Review the [VSCode Webview API docs](https://code.visualstudio.com/api/extension-guides/webview) for drag-and-drop support (e.g. `WebviewPanel` drag and drop, `DataTransfer` API).
2. Check if VSCode's `onDidReceiveMessage` / drag events in webviews support external file drops.
3. Investigate how the file contents can be passed from the webview drop event back to the extension host and made available to the agent.
4. Note any limitations (e.g. file size, binary vs text files).

comments.body: +1, this is a feature I use a lot in Claude Code, parity would be great to have.

title: Allow dragging files from outside VSCode (e.g. Desktop/Finder) into the chat input box

body:
## Summary
When the v7 extension migrates a legacy `custom_modes.yaml` into `~/.config/kilo/kilo.jsonc` under the `agent` key, two related bugs corrupt any mode whose `customInstructions` uses YAML block-scalar syntax (`|-` / `|`) **or** whose `groups` list contains a nested `[edit, {fileRegex: ...}]` entry.

Other modes (single-line `customInstructions`, flat `groups`) migrate fine, so the issue is specific to these two YAML shapes.

## Repro

A minimal legacy `custom_modes.yaml` entry that triggers both bugs:

```yaml
- slug: my-mode
  name: My Mode
  roleDefinition: One-sentence role definition for this mode that is long enough to be visibly truncated when copied into the description field.
  customInstructions: |-
    First line of instructions.

    ### Section A
    - bullet a1
    - bullet a2

    ### Section B
    - bullet b1
    - bullet b2
  groups:
    - read
    - - edit
      - fileRegex: \.(md|tex)$
        description: Markdown and LaTeX files only
    - browser
    - mcp
    - command
  source: global
```

Upgrade to v7.x and open `~/.config/kilo/kilo.jsonc`.

## Actual result

```jsonc
"my-mode": {
  "prompt": "<roleDefinition>\n\nUSER'S CUSTOM INSTRUCTIONS\n\n... \n\nMode-specific Instructions:\nFirst line of instructions.",
  // ^ everything after the first line of customInstructions is dropped
  //   (Section A and Section B never make it into the prompt)
  "description": "One-sentence role definition for this mode that is long enough to be visibly tr",
  // ^ description is a mid-word truncation of roleDefinition
  "mode": "primary",
  "permission": {
    "read": "allow",
    "- edit": "allow",      // <-- invalid key: YAML list dash "- edit" taken literally
    "bash": "allow",
    "skill": "allow",
    "edit": "deny"          // <-- fileRegex restriction lost; blanket deny instead
  }
}
```

So the user ends up with:
- A prompt missing the bulk of the actual instructions.
- A garbage `"- edit": "allow"` permission key that does nothing.
- A blanket `edit: deny` instead of the original `.md`/`.tex` whitelist.

## Expected result

- `customInstructions` block-scalar content should be fully preserved (joined with `\n`) in the migrated `prompt`.
- The nested `[edit, {fileRegex: "\\.(md|tex)$"}]` group should be translated into the v7 glob form, e.g.:
  ```jsonc
  "edit": {
    "*.md": "allow",
    "*.tex": "allow",
    "*": "deny"
  }
  ```
- `description` should be derived more meaningfully (or left empty) rather than a mid-word truncation of `roleDefinition`.

## Workaround

Hand-edit `kilo.jsonc` to paste the full `customInstructions` body into `prompt` and rewrite the `permission.edit` block as a glob object. The original `custom_modes.yaml` is preserved at `~/Library/Application Support/Code/User/globalStorage/kilocode.kilo-code/settings/custom_modes.yaml` (macOS), so the source data isn't lost.

## Environment
- Kilo Code: v7.x (VS Code extension, upgraded from 6.x)
- OS: macOS 15 (Darwin 24.6.0)
- Config path: `~/.config/kilo/kilo.jsonc`

## Related
- #6481 (MCP settings not migrated to v7) — same family of legacy → v7 migration gap.
title: Legacy custom_modes.yaml → v7 agent migration: multi-line customInstructions truncated and fileRegex group produces invalid permission key

body:
## Bug Description

The `todowrite` tool throws a `SchemaError: Expected array, got \"[...]\"` error when passed a valid array of todo items.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Call the `todowrite` tool with a valid todos array:
```json
{
  "todos": [
    {"content": "Test todo", "priority": "low", "status": "completed"}
  ]
}
```

## Expected Behavior

The tool should accept the array and create/update the todos.

## Actual Behavior

```
SchemaError: Expected array, got "[{"content":"Test todo","priority":"low","status":"completed"}]"
```

## Analysis

The tool schema expects `todos` as a mutable array:
```typescript
export const Parameters = Schema.Struct({
  todos: Schema.mutable(Schema.Array(TodoItem)).annotate({ description: "The updated todo list" }),
})
```

The issue appears to be in how the LLM passes the array to the tool - the array is being serialized as a string instead of being passed as a proper JSON array.

## Environment

- Kilo version: 7.2.52
- Platform: linux

## Severity

Blocks basic task tracking functionality.
title: todowrite tool: SchemaError - Expected array when passing valid array

body:
The `Kilo Code Review` GitHub check has been failing on the majority of PRs across our repositories since 2026-05-11, with an upstream sandbox provisioning error. The error text itself instructs the client to retry, but Kilo's server-side retry appears to give up after ~3 minutes, leaving the check red.

## Error

The check details page surfaces:

```json
{
  "error": {
    "message": "Failed to create workspace directory: SandboxError: Container is starting. Please retry in a moment.",
    "code": -32603,
    "data": {
      "code": "INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR",
      "httpStatus": 500,
      "stack": "Error: Failed to create workspace directory: SandboxError: Container is starting. Please retry in a moment.\n    at setupWorkspace (index.js:45616:11)\n    at async prepareWorkspace (index.js:63624:76)\n    at async index.js:63741:27\n    at async resolveMiddleware (index.js:1893:20)\n    ...",
      "path": "prepareSession"
    }
  }
}
```

## Timing pattern

Successful Kilo runs on the same repos before the outage finished in **30–130 s**. Failures since 2026-05-11 stall at **~3:00–3:40** before going red, which suggests Kilo's backend *is* retrying internally but giving up before the container is ready. Manual retries (pushing empty commits to re-trigger Kilo) keep hitting the same failure — i.e. it isn't a brief blip; the container-startup path is consistently slower than the retry budget right now.

| PR | Date | Result | Kilo duration |
|---|---|---|---|
| rosswgray/studyfit#111 | 2026-05-04 | ✅ | 127 s |
| rosswgray/studyfit#114 | 2026-05-05 | ✅ | 49 s |
| rosswgray/studyfit#129 | 2026-05-09 | ✅ | 34 s |
| rosswgray/studyfit#137 | 2026-05-11 | ❌ FAILURE | 217 s |
| rosswgray/studyfit#139 | 2026-05-11 | ❌ FAILURE | 190 s |

## Affected check-run URLs (for your server logs)

- https://app.kilo.ai/code-reviews/7bb102f3-66e7-4098-a42c-7a9e920bdec5
- https://app.kilo.ai/code-reviews/e05dc8f5-45d5-45f5-8a64-1015f2ef9d0b
- https://app.kilo.ai/code-reviews/2a42a50f-d79e-40b1-b454-dbdb4854dd2c

## Suggested fix

The `setupWorkspace` retry loop should keep trying for longer than the container's cold-start time, since the error itself is correctly identified as transient ("Please retry in a moment"). A retry budget of ~5 minutes would have covered every case we've observed.

## Workaround for affected users

We've shipped an auto-retry GitHub Actions workflow on our end that pushes an empty commit (re-triggering Kilo) when the check fails with `SandboxError`/`prepareSession`, capped at 3 retries per PR. Even with three back-to-back retries, the check is still going red on every PR today, so this is a server-side capacity / startup-budget issue, not a client-retry budget one. Happy to share the YAML if it's useful for other affected users.

## Related

[#7065](https://github.com/Kilo-Org/kilocode/issues/7065) reports the same `prepareSession` failure mode against the interactive Cloud Agent (not PR Code Review). Different surface, same underlying API.

comments.body: Affects me for months...

title: Kilo Code Review check fails on PRs with prepareSession SandboxError: Container is starting

body:
Gemma 4 uses a specific token-based format for its thinking process, which is currently not recognized by Kilo's session processor, causing the thinking blocks to be rendered as plain text instead of being folded.

**Model Control Tokens:**
- Trigger Thinking: `<|channel>thought\n`
- End Thinking: `<channel|>`

**Analysis:**
The issue resides in `packages/opencode/src/session/processor.ts`. The `SessionProcessor` currently handles native `reasoning-delta` events but doesn't parse tags within the `text-delta` stream.

**Suggested Fix:**
Implement a state machine in `SessionProcessor` that:
1. Monitors `text-delta` for the `<|channel>thought\n` start tag.
2. Switches to a "thinking" state and initiates a reasoning part in the session.
3. Treats subsequent `text-delta` content as `reasoning-delta` until the `<channel|>` end tag is encountered.
4. Returns to the normal text state after the end tag.

This will ensure that Gemma 4's reasoning is correctly identified and folded in the UI.
title: Support for Gemma 4 thinking block recognition

body:
## Description

When a user configures a variant that disables thinking (e.g., `"none"` for GPT-5 or `"minimal"` for Gemini) on the parent session, subagents spawned via the Task tool still exhibit thinking behavior.

This happens because the variant (thinking/reasoning effort setting) is NOT inherited from the parent session when the subagent's model falls back to the parent's model (3rd priority fallback). The parent's `variant` value stored on the assistant message (`msg.info.variant`) is completely ignored.

### Root cause

In `packages/opencode/src/tool/task.ts`, line 126:

```
const variant = saved?.variant ?? (saved ? undefined : next.variant)
```

The variant resolution chain is:
1. CLI saved model variant → 2. Subagent's own configured variant → **STOPS HERE**

The parent's variant (`msg.info.variant`) is never consulted, even though the model (`modelID`/`providerID`) correctly falls back to the parent on lines 121-125.

### Impact

The subagent uses the model's *default* thinking behavior (e.g., `reasoningEffort: "medium"` for GPT-5, `thinkingConfig: { thinkingLevel: "high" }` for Gemini-3) instead of respecting the parent's variant choice. This causes:

- Unexpected thinking token consumption in subagents
- Inconsistent reasoning behavior between parent and child agents
- Users must redundantly configure `variant` on every subagent definition to avoid this

### Fix

Change the variant resolution to include parent variant as final fallback:

```
- const variant = saved?.variant ?? (saved ? undefined : next.variant)
+ const variant = saved?.variant ?? (saved ? undefined : (next.variant ?? msg.info.variant))
```

A fix branch `fix/subagent-variant-inheritance` with commit `6ccd42f096` already exists.

## Steps to reproduce

1. Use a model that supports thinking/reasoning (e.g., GPT-5, Gemini-3, Anthropic Sonnet 4.6)
2. Set variant to a no/low-thinking option (e.g., `"none"` for GPT-5, `"minimal"` for Gemini)
3. Start a session — parent agent does not think as expected ✓
4. Have the parent spawn any subagent via Task tool (e.g., general, explore)
5. Observe that the subagent session uses thinking/reasoning even though parent had it disabled ✗

## Operating System

macOS

## Kilo version

HEAD
title: Subagent does not inherit parent variant (thinking/reasoning effort) when model falls back

body: ### Description Playwright MCP files get committed to PR / show up in files changed, which inflates the changes and creates unclarity what changes are actually made. ### Plugins VS Code ### Kilo version 7.2.50 ### Steps to reproduce Steps to reproduce: 1. Ask the playwright MCP to check something in your browser. 2. Check the files changed afterwards 3. comments.body: @kilocode-bot please start working on this issue

A PR has been opened to fix this issue: **<https://github.com/Kilo-Org/kilocode/pull/10169>**

**Root cause:** The Playwright MCP server was writing screenshot/artifact files to the workspace root directory (no `--output-dir` was set), so those files got picked up by git and showed up in diffs.

**Fix:**

1. **`--output-dir` flag** — redirects all Playwright MCP artifacts to a system temp directory outside the repo
2. **`.gitignore` entries** — adds `page-*.png`, `page-*.jpeg`, and `playwright-mcp-output/` as a safety net for a known upstream Playwright MCP bug where custom-named screenshots can bypass `--output-dir`

title: Playwright MCP files get committed to PR / show up in files changed

body:
## Problem

When an agent (e.g. Kilo Claw) generates a file — such as a markdown document — during a chat session, there is no way to receive it as a downloadable attachment directly in the VS Code extension chat window. Users are forced to retrieve the file through an external channel (e.g. Discord) instead.

## Proposed Solution

Allow agents to send file attachments in the chat window, similar to how messaging platforms like Discord handle file sharing. When an agent produces a file as output, it should be surfaced in the chat as a downloadable/openable attachment rather than requiring the user to locate it manually or use another channel.

## User Story

As a Kilo Code VS Code extension user, I want agents to be able to attach generated files (e.g. markdown documents) directly in the chat window, so that I can download or open them without leaving the editor or switching to an external tool.

## Additional Context

Reported by a community member: the current workaround is to go to Discord and ask the agent to send the file there, which is a poor user experience for an editor-native workflow.
title: Support file attachments in chat responses
