body:
### Description

## Summary

The `kilo` CLI ignores the `--model` flag on startup when a cached model entry exists in
`~/.local/state/kilo/model.json`. Rather than honouring the explicitly passed flag, kilo
overwrites `model.json` with its own internally-remembered state within milliseconds of
launch, making it impossible to override the active model from the command line.

A secondary issue is that when an invalid or unresolvable model ID is passed via `--model`,
kilo silently falls back to the most recently used model with no warning or error — making
the failure invisible and extremely hard to diagnose.

---

## Environment

- **Kilo version:** 7.2.14
- **OS:** Ubuntu (WSL2 on Windows 11)
- **Shell:** bash
- **Config location:** `~/.config/kilo/kilo.jsonc`
- **State location:** `~/.local/state/kilo/model.json`

---

## Bug 1 — `--model` flag is silently overridden by cached state

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Launch kilo once and select any model (e.g. `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6`).
2. Exit kilo.
3. Re-launch with an explicit model override:
   ```bash
   kilo --model kilo/x-ai/grok-4.3 /path/to/project
   ```
4. Observe that kilo opens with `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6` instead of the requested model.

### Expected Behaviour

The `--model` flag should take precedence over any cached state. An explicit CLI argument
is a deliberate user instruction and must override persisted defaults.

### Actual Behaviour

Kilo reads `~/.local/state/kilo/model.json` on startup and immediately overwrites it with
its own internal state, discarding the `--model` flag entirely. The user sees no error or
warning. The requested model is never loaded.

### Evidence

Stamping the correct model into `model.json` immediately before launch (via an external
wrapper script) and locking the file read-only (`chmod 444`) was the only way to force
kilo to respect the desired model — confirming that kilo actively overwrites the file
during its startup sequence rather than reading and respecting the `--model` flag first.

---

## Bug 2 — Silent fallback to last-used model when `--model` value is unresolvable

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Pass a model ID with a minor typo or incorrect format:
   ```bash
   kilo --model kilo/x-ai/grok-4-3 /path/to/project
   # Correct ID is kilo/x-ai/grok-4.3 (decimal, not dash)
   ```
2. Observe kilo launch normally with no error.

### Expected Behaviour

Kilo should:
- Print a clear error: `Error: model 'kilo/x-ai/grok-4-3' not found.`
- Either exit with a non-zero code, or list similar available models as suggestions.

### Actual Behaviour

Kilo silently falls back to the most recently used model (in this case
`anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6`) with zero output indicating the requested model was
rejected or not found. This makes the failure completely invisible and forces the user
to incur API costs on the wrong (and potentially more expensive) model.

---

## Impact

- **Cost:** Users are silently billed for a more expensive model than requested.
  In this case, `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6` cost ~USD 0.10 just to load startup
  instructions, versus ~USD 0.02 for the intended model — a 5x cost difference per
  session, compounding across every launch.
- **Trust:** The CLI appears to accept the `--model` flag without complaint, giving
  the user false confidence their instruction was honoured.
- **Debuggability:** There is no mechanism to confirm which model is actually active
  at launch without incurring a billable API call.

---

## Suggested Fixes

1. **Respect `--model` over cached state.** CLI flags should always take precedence
   over persisted state files. Apply the requested model before writing anything to
   `model.json`.
2. **Validate model IDs before launch.** If a `--model` value cannot be resolved
   against the available model list, print a descriptive error and exit with a
   non-zero code rather than silently falling back.
3. **Add a `--dry-run` or `--print-model` flag** that prints the resolved active
   model and exits without starting a session, so users can verify configuration
   without incurring API costs.
4. **Consider a `kilo config set model ,[object Object]

### Plugins

none

### Kilo version

7.2.14

### Steps to reproduce

1. Launch kilo once and select any model (e.g. `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6`).
2. Exit kilo.
3. Re-launch with an explicit model override:
   ```bash
   kilo --model kilo/x-ai/grok-4.3 /path/to/project
   ```
4. Observe that kilo opens with `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6` instead of the requested model.

### Screenshot and/or share link

_No response_

### Operating System

Ubuntu (WSL2 on Windows 11)

### Terminal

Windows Terminal
title: # Bug Report: `--model` CLI flag silently ignored at startup; model.json overwritten on launch

body:
### Feature hasn't been suggested before.

- [x] I have verified this feature I'm about to request hasn't been suggested before.

### Describe the enhancement you want to request

Prototype a session-first navigation model inside Agent Manager where each workspace/worktree can expand to show the sessions currently running in that context.

Today Agent Manager is centered around worktree cards. That works well for isolated worktree flows, but it is harder to quickly inspect and switch between multiple active sessions, especially when several sessions run in the same local workspace or worktree. The prototype should make sessions visible in the left pane without losing the worktree/workspace context where those sessions execute.

Proposed MVP:

```text
WORKSPACES

▾ local / current-branch            ?2 >1 .3
  ? Session waiting for input
  > Session currently running
  . Completed session

▸ worktree / feature-a              >1 .2
▸ worktree / feature-b              !1 .1
```

Interaction goals:

- Worktree/workspace rows remain the canonical parent for sessions.
- Session children are only shown when the parent is expanded.
- Parent rows show aggregate state counts, for example waiting, running, error, and idle.
- Clicking a session focuses the matching session tab/panel on the right.
- Clicking a collapsed parent expands it. Clicking an expanded parent can focus its last active session.
- Sessions with attention-needed states should sort above idle sessions within a group.
- Collapsed parents with attention-needed sessions should be visually distinguishable.

Optional prototype variant:

```text
NEEDS ATTENTION
? Session waiting for input          local / current-branch
! Session with error                 worktree / feature-b

RUNNING
> Session currently running          worktree / feature-a

WORKSPACES
▾ local / current-branch             ?1 .2
  ? Session waiting for input
  . Completed session

▸ worktree / feature-a               >1 .2
▸ worktree / feature-b               !1 .1
```

This variant keeps the canonical tree under WORKSPACES while adding virtual shortcut sections for attention and running states. Selecting a virtual row should focus the session and reveal/select it under its parent when practical.

Prototype constraints:

- Treat this as a prototype, not a final navigation redesign.
- Keep examples generic and avoid exposing real session names or customer context.
- Prefer a small, local Agent Manager UI change over backend/session model changes unless absolutely necessary.
- Reuse existing session/worktree state where possible.
- Avoid introducing new persistent data until the interaction model is validated.
title: Prototype Agent Manager session tree grouping

body:
### Description

`kilo run --attach http://host:port -p <password> --format json` works correctly, but `kilo attach http://host:port -p <password>` (interactive TUI mode) fails with:

```
ERROR service=server-proxy error=Unauthorized tui bootstrap failed
```

The `kilo attach` command uses a `server-proxy` mechanism for the TUI bootstrap, which does not appear to pass the Basic Auth credentials correctly, resulting in a 401 Unauthorized despite the same password working for `kilo run --attach` (REST API mode).

### To Reproduce

1. Start a `kilo serve` with password:
```bash
kilo serve --port 4096 --hostname 0.0.0.0
# With env: KILO_SERVER_PASSWORD=mysecret
```

2. `kilo run --attach` works:
```bash
KILO_SERVER_PASSWORD=mysecret kilo run "hello" --attach http://localhost:4096 --format json --auto --dangerously-skip-permissions
# Returns JSON events (step_start, text, step_finish)
```

3. `kilo attach` (TUI) fails:
```bash
KILO_SERVER_PASSWORD=mysecret kilo attach http://localhost:4096
# ERROR: Unauthorized - tui bootstrap failed
```

Also tried explicit `-p` flag:
```bash
kilo attach http://localhost:4096 -p mysecret
# Same result
```

### Expected Behavior

`kilo attach` should authenticate successfully with `--password` flag or `KILO_SERVER_PASSWORD` env var, enabling interactive TUI sessions against password-protected `kilo serve` instances.

### Debug Log

```
INFO  service=server-proxy args=["attach","http://localhost:4096","-p","mysecret"]
ERROR service=server-proxy error=Unauthorized tui bootstrap failed
```

The `server-proxy` service (used only by `kilo attach`, not by `kilo run --attach`) fails to pass Basic Auth credentials during TUI bootstrap.

### Environment

- Kilo CLI: 7.2.31 and 7.2.40 (both affected)
- `kilo serve` (systemd): 7.2.31
- OS: Linux (Ubuntu)

### Related

- https://github.com/Kilo-Org/kilocode/issues/8800 (similar report: "can't login kilo serve")
- https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/issues/5256 (Basic Auth feature implementation, PR #4740)
title: Bug: kilo attach (TUI mode) returns Unauthorized when kilo run --attach works with same password

body:
## Feedback from Slack thread (#new-vscode-feedback)

One feature users miss from the old extension is the ability to add auto-approvals for commands that have already been approved.

### User flow (from the old extension)
- User approves a command ("yeah of course, run")
- While the command is running, the user investigates which commands to auto-approve so it doesn't ask again
- In the old extension, you could get back into the approval settings by unfolding the shell execution element

### Suggestions
- Separate the permission rules from the permission prompt, OR
- Combine the permission prompt and the shell execution element into a single UI element (as it was more combined in the old extension), so users can manage auto-approval rules from the shell execution context after the fact
- Current UI feels a bit weird when it's the main session

### Notes
- Reporter acknowledged this depends on implementation complexity — may not be worth it if too complex
- Screenshots/mockups were shared in the Slack thread showing a combined single-element UI

---

Built for [Imanol Maiztegui](https://kilo-code.slack.com/archives/C0AFBRXUH2N/p1777983027100969?thread_ts=1777982575.769199&cid=C0AFBRXUH2N) by [Kilo for Slack](https://kilo.ai/slack)
title: Allow adding command auto-approvals from already-approved commands (combine permission prompt with shell element)

body:
### Description

### Environment
- **OS:** Windows 10 (64-bit)
- **CPU:** AMD Phenom II X6 1100T (no AVX, no SSE4.2; supports SSE4a)
- **Kilo Code CLI version:** latest (tried both standard and baseline builds)
- **Bun version:** 1.3.13 (bundled with baseline build)
- **VS Code extension version:** 7.2.34

### Description
I am unable to run Kilo Code CLI on an old local machine with an AMD Phenom II X6 1100T processor. The CLI panics with "Illegal instruction" immediately on startup, both with the standard and the baseline Windows build. This causes the VS Code extension to show "CLI Server Status: Error" and is completely unusable locally.

I previously used Kilo Code via VS Code Remote on a machine with AVX/SSE4.2, where everything worked fine. Now I need to work locally on the Phenom system.

### Steps to Reproduce
1. Download the latest baseline build for Windows x64 (`kilo-windows-x64-baseline.zip` from [releases](https://github.com/Kilo-Org/kilocode/releases)).
2. Extract and add the directory to system PATH.
3. Run `kilo --version` in cmd/PowerShell.

### Expected Behavior
The CLI should print its version and run normally.

### Actual Behavior
Application panics with:
```
============================================================
Bun v1.3.13 (bf2e2cec) Windows x64 (baseline)
Windows v.win10_cu
Args:
Features: no_avx2 no_avx
Elapsed: 2ms | User: 15ms | Sys: 15ms
RSS:
panic: Illegal instruction at address 0x7FF6E879F1F9
panicked during a panic. Aborting.
```

### Additional Context
- The CPU lacks SSE4.2 (and AVX/AVX2), but supports SSE4a. Bun’s baseline builds are advertised to work on CPUs without AVX, but they appear to still require SSE4.2 instructions.
- There is a known regression in Bun where fallback-mode detection fails even when `no_avx2 no_avx` is printed (e.g., oven-sh/bun#26353). This panic is likely the same root cause.
- Kilo Code’s documentation suggests the baseline build for older CPUs, but it does not help in this case.

### Request
Is it possible to provide a CLI build that does not require SSE4.2? For example:
- A version based on Node.js instead of Bun (as was done in very early releases, if any).
- A custom Bun binary compiled with SSE4.2 requirement disabled.
- Or official recognition of this hardware limitation, so users with such CPUs can plan accordingly (e.g., stick to remote development).

Thank you for your time, and for making such a promising AI coding assistant! I’d be happy to test any experimental builds or provide further diagnostics if needed.


### Plugins

_No response_

### Kilo version

7.2.34

### Steps to reproduce

1. Old CPU with no SSE4.2 support
2. Download the latest baseline build for Windows x64 (`kilo-windows-x64-baseline.zip` from [releases](https://github.com/Kilo-Org/kilocode/releases)).
3. Extract and add the directory to system PATH.
4. Run `kilo --version` in cmd/PowerShell.

### Screenshot and/or share link

_No response_

### Operating System

Windows 10

### Terminal

PowerShell, cmd

comments.body:
I can reproduce this on another pre-Nehalem x86-64 system.

  ### Host

  - OS: Linux Mint
  - Kernel: Linux 6.17.0-35-generic x86_64
  - CPU: Intel Core 2 Duo U9400 @ 1.40 GHz
  - CPU flags: SSE4.1 supported; POPCNT, SSE4.2, AVX and AVX2 absent
  - glibc: 2.39
  - Node.js: 18.19.1
  - npm: 9.2.0
  - Kilo CLI: 7.3.50
  - Bundled runtime: Bun 1.3.14 Linux x64 baseline

  ### Reproduction

  ```shell
  npx @kilocode/cli
  # Illegal instruction (core dumped)
```
  Inspecting the core dump with GDB shows that the bundled Kilo executable faults at:

  ```shell
  0x39fc488: popcnt %r8,%r9
```
  POPCNT is not supported by this CPU. The npm launcher correctly selects @kilocode/cli-linux-x64-
  baseline because AVX2 is unavailable, but that baseline executable still contains unconditional POPCNT
  instructions.

  This appears to be a Bun baseline/runtime compatibility problem rather than Kilo application logic.

### Similar Claude Code failure

  Claude Code 2.1.185 fails identically on this host:
  ```shell
  npx @anthropic-ai/claude-code
  # Illegal instruction (core dumped)
```
  Its core dump faults on the same instruction sequence:
  ```shell
  0x2e284a8: popcnt %r8,%r9
```
  That regression is tracked in [anthropics/claude-code#63609](https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues/63609). Claude Code 2.1.112, which uses its older Node-based entry point, runs successfully here.

  For comparison, the native musl build of OpenAI Codex CLI 0.141.0 works correctly on this same
  machine:

  codex-cli 0.141.0

  This demonstrates that native x86-64 CLIs can still support this hardware when built against an older
  CPU baseline.

  It would help if Kilo could either:

  1. Ship a genuinely POPCNT-free x86-64 build;
  2. Offer a Node-based distribution; or
  3. Detect missing POPCNT in the Node launcher and report a clear unsupported-CPU error instead of
     allowing the bundled runtime to core-dump.

Happy to help where needed.

title: CLI panics with "Illegal instruction" on AMD Phenom II X6 1100T (no SSE4.2) even with baseline build

### Description

This was particularly awful because I was using the Auto Balanced model.   I never, in weeks with Claude Code, ever saw anything like this.

Environment

OS: Windows 11 + WSL (Ubuntu)
Terminal host: legacy console (no tabs)
Kilo CLI version: 7.2.14
Invocation: kilo started in repo root
Model: [e.g., Auto Balanced Kilo Gateway / Kimi K2.6 / Sonnet]
Summary When asking Kilo CLI to update AGENTS.md by appending a new section, the agent created a duplicate section heading instead of editing in-place / detecting the existing section. Additionally, the first edit attempt appeared to fail silently (no clear error surfaced to the user), requiring a repeat attempt that produced the duplicate.

Steps to Reproduce

Start kilo in a repo that already has an AGENTS.md.
Prompt the agent to “append a new section titled ## Kilo CLI Pseudo-Modes (Subagents via @mentions) at the end of AGENTS.md” (or similar).
Run the prompt a second time (or re-run after the first attempt reports completion).
Inspect AGENTS.md.
Expected Result

If the section exists: the agent edits it in place or reports “already present; no change.”
If the edit fails: the CLI clearly reports the failure.
AGENTS.md ends with exactly one ## Kilo CLI Pseudo-Modes (Subagents via @mentions) section.
Actual Result

The file contained two copies of the same section heading/content (duplicate insertion).
In an earlier attempt, the edit seemed to fail without an obvious error (user perceived “silent failure”).
Impact This breaks trust for governance/config files. Duplicate sections are easy to miss, and silent failures force users into repetitive prompting and manual diffing.

Workarounds

Force “diff-only” workflow: require the agent to output only a unified diff and manually apply it via git.
Manually restore file via git restore AGENTS.md and retry.

### Plugins

none

### Kilo version

7.2.14

### Steps to reproduce

Start kilo in a repo that already has an AGENTS.md.
Prompt the agent to “append a new section titled ## Kilo CLI Pseudo-Modes (Subagents via @mentions) at the end of AGENTS.md” (or similar).
Run the prompt a second time (or re-run after the first attempt reports completion).
Inspect AGENTS.md.
Expected Result

If the section exists: the agent edits it in place or reports “already present; no change.”
If the edit fails: the CLI clearly reports the failure.
AGENTS.md ends with exactly one ## Kilo CLI Pseudo-Modes (Subagents via @mentions) section.
Actual Result

The file contained two copies of the same section heading/content (duplicate insertion).
In an earlier attempt, the edit seemed to fail without an obvious error (user perceived “silent failure”).
Impact This breaks trust for governance/config files. Duplicate sections are easy to miss, and silent failures force users into repetitive prompting and manual diffing.

_No response_

Ubuntu 22.04

### Terminal

Windows Terminal

body:
Feature request from an enterprise customer context (via #engineering-ama).

**Problem**
Users have reported cases where the agent runs autonomously for 20-30 minutes and spends significant amounts (e.g. $40) on a single task. Today we have a doom-loop protection (stops if the same call is repeated a few times), but no guardrail for cases where the agent is legitimately trying different avenues on a tricky problem.

**Proposal**
Add optional settings to limit, per turn:
1. Maximum number of steps (tool calls / autonomous iterations)
2. Maximum total dollar spend

When either limit is exceeded, pause and prompt the user whether they want to continue (rather than hard-stopping silently). If the user confirms, continue for another budget window.

**Notes**
- Both settings should be optional / off by default to preserve current behavior.
- The old (pre-rewrite) extension had similar limits (number of requests and dollar cap) — we could take inspiration from that implementation.
- Context: Slack thread in #engineering-ama started by Alex Gold on 2026-05-04, with input from Joshua Lambert and Mark IJbema.

body:
## Description

Six tests in `packages/opencode/test/server/httpapi-*.test.ts` validate parity between the legacy Hono routes and the new Effect `HttpApi` bridge. Five of them fail on Kilo's branch and were skipped with `kilocode_change` markers during the v1.14.29 upstream sync to unblock the merge. This issue tracks the structural work needed to un-skip them.

The Effect `HttpApi` bridge is gated behind `KILO_EXPERIMENTAL_HTTPAPI` and is **not enabled in any production client** today (VS Code extension, JetBrains plugin, TUI, CLI, desktop, web all still hit Hono). The tests matter because they gate Kilo's ability to eventually complete the migration upstream is driving — without them, we can't flip the flag on without regressing.

### Files currently holding `test.skip` markers

- `packages/opencode/test/server/httpapi-bridge.test.ts` — 3 tests (route / params / body shape parity)
- `packages/opencode/test/server/httpapi-experimental.test.ts` — 1 test (`serves read-only experimental endpoints through Hono bridge`)
- `packages/opencode/test/server/httpapi-instance.test.ts` — 1 test (`serves catalog read endpoints through Hono bridge`)

All five `test.skip` sites are tagged with `kilocode_change` comments linking back here.

## Two structural problems to fix

### 1. Kilo overlay routes aren't migrated to Effect `HttpApi` contracts

The three bridge-parity tests in `httpapi-bridge.test.ts` diff the list of routes emitted by Hono's OpenAPI generator against the list emitted by `OpenApi.fromApi(PublicApi)`. Kilo-specific Hono routes have no matching Effect `HttpApi` contract. Missing routes (from the failing CI diff):

- `GET /config/warnings` — add to `ConfigApi` in `packages/opencode/src/server/routes/instance/httpapi/config.ts`
- `GET /indexing/status` — new `IndexingApi` module; route originates in `packages/kilo-indexing/server`
- `GET /kilo/claw/status`, `GET /kilo/claw/chat-credentials`, `GET /kilo/cloud-sessions` — new `KiloApi` module; routes originate in `packages/kilo-gateway/src/server/routes.ts`
- `GET /experimental/worktree/diff`, `/experimental/worktree/diff/file`, `/experimental/worktree/diff/summary` — add to `ExperimentalApi` in `packages/opencode/src/server/routes/instance/httpapi/experimental.ts`

New API modules also need to be registered in:

- `packages/opencode/src/server/routes/instance/httpapi/public.ts` (via `.addHttpApi(...)`)
- `packages/opencode/src/server/routes/instance/httpapi/server.ts` (via `HttpApiBuilder.layer(...).pipe(Layer.provide(handlers))`)

Pattern to follow: the existing `ConfigApi` + `configHandlers` in `httpapi/config.ts` is the smallest reference. For routes that live in external Kilo packages (gateway, indexing), contracts likely need to live in `packages/opencode/src/kilocode/server/httpapi/` and be consumed from there, mirroring how Hono wires them via `createKiloRoutes({...})` in `packages/opencode/src/kilocode/server/instance.ts`.

### 2. Kilo runtime leaks Instance context mid-request through the bridge

Both `httpapi-experimental.test.ts` and `httpapi-instance.test.ts` request endpoints whose routes *are* migrated (`/experimental/tool` and `/agent`) but still return 500 under `KILO_EXPERIMENTAL_HTTPAPI=1`. Observed behaviour during investigation:

- `/agent` — the handler calls `agent.list()`. Our added `Effect.catchAllCause` / `Effect.tapErrorCause` wrappers never fire, yet the response is 500 with empty body. `agent.list()` → `InstanceState.useEffect(state, s => s.list())` → `InstanceState.get` → `ScopedCache.get(cache, directory)`. The `directory` is read via `InstanceRef ?? Instance.current`. `InstanceRef` is provided by the middleware in `httpapi/server.ts:instance`, so the request-time call site is fine — but something along the path loses the effect context for a later fiber.
- Stack traces surfacing **as unhandled errors between tests** (not the direct 500) show `buildReviewPromptBranch`/`buildReviewPromptUncommitted` being invoked from `SchemaAST.js:1482 onItem` during response encoding. These come from a separate bug that the same tests expose (see below).

Concretely at least two things need attention:

- **Instance context during response encoding.** The Effect `HttpApi` bridge's response-encoding phase runs outside the request's `Instance.provide(...)` AsyncLocalStorage scope. Any Kilo code that reads `Instance.current` / `Instance.directory` during schema encoding (not just inside the handler effect) will die. The fix is to ensure Kilo overlay code paths accessed during encoding either (a) route through the Effect `InstanceRef` service instead of `Instance.current`, or (b) are bound via `InstanceState.bind(...)` so the AsyncLocalStorage context is restored.
- **Lazy `template` getter on Kilo review commands.** `packages/opencode/src/kilocode/review/command.ts` exports `localReviewCommand()` / `localReviewUncommittedCommand()` whose `get template()` returns `Review.buildReviewPromptBranch()` / `buildReviewPromptUncommitted()`. These are `async` functions that synchronously call `Instance.directory` before their first `await`. When the Effect Schema encoder iterates command list fields, the getter fires outside the request scope and throws. Removing those two commands from `packages/opencode/src/command/index.ts:110-111` makes the review stack traces disappear but does **not** fix `/agent` (confirmed). Still, these getters are a latent bug that should be converted to either eager-computed strings or real `Effect.Effect<string>` values plumbed through `InstanceRef`.

## Reproduction

```bash
cd packages/opencode
# Temporarily un-skip the tests:
#   test/server/httpapi-bridge.test.ts (3 tests)
#   test/server/httpapi-experimental.test.ts (1 test)
#   test/server/httpapi-instance.test.ts (1 test)
bun test test/server/httpapi-bridge.test.ts test/server/httpapi-experimental.test.ts test/server/httpapi-instance.test.ts
```

## Suggested task breakdown

1. **Port the 8 Kilo overlay routes to Effect `HttpApi` contracts** (fixes `httpapi-bridge.test.ts`).
2. **Audit Kilo code reachable during response encoding for `Instance.current`/`Instance.directory` usage.** Convert to `InstanceRef`-based or restore via `InstanceState.bind`. Particular callouts: `packages/opencode/src/kilocode/review/review.ts`, any `get template()` accessors, any agent-list side effects.
3. **Replace the `get template()` pattern in `packages/opencode/src/kilocode/review/command.ts`** with a Schema-safe representation that doesn't trigger AsyncLocalStorage reads during encoding.
4. **Un-skip the tests and remove the `kilocode_change` skip markers** once 1–3 land.

## Acceptance

- `bun test test/server/httpapi-*.test.ts` is fully green with the `kilocode_change` skip markers removed.
- No `test.skip` in those files carries an HttpApi-bridge justification comment.

body:
### Description

When using an LLM provider (e.g., Zhipu/GLM-5.1 via OpenAI-compatible API), long-running streaming requests intermittently fail with either Connection reset by server or Request timed out. 
The log clearly shows "isRetryable": true in the request payload right before the stream error occurs. However, Kilo does not automatically retry the request. Instead, it sets the session offline and shows the user a "Waiting for network... Press Esc to stop this turn." dialog, requiring manual intervention.
This is particularly problematic for complex tasks that involve long inference times (90+ seconds of thinking without streaming output), where the provider server resets the idle TCP connection.

### Plugins

None / OpenAI-compatible API

### Kilo version

7.2.25

### Steps to reproduce

1. Configure Kilo CLI with a model provider that has long inference times (e.g., zai/glm-5.1 via OpenAI-compatible endpoint /v1/chat/completions)
2. Start a complex coding task that generates large context (e.g., a task involving many file reads, edits, and MCP tool calls)
3. Observe that after 50-120 seconds of streaming, the connection drops
4. The TUI shows "Network disconnected / Connection reset by server / Waiting for network..."
5. Check the log file — it shows isRetryable: true followed by stream error, but no retry attempt

### Screenshot and/or share link

service=llm providerID=zai modelID=glm-5.1 session.id=ses_xxx stream
... ~90 seconds of delta output, then silence ...
"isRetryable":true}} stream error
WARN service=session.network message=Connection reset by server waiting for network
WARN service=session.processor.kilo message=Connection reset by server session offline
INFO service=session.network network restored  (3 seconds later)
Timeline from repeated occurrences:
00:06:52  LLM stream start → success
00:08:52  LLM stream start → REQUEST TIMED OUT (no auto-retry)
00:09:16  LLM stream start → success
00:10:06  LLM stream start → CONNECTION RESET (no auto-retry)
00:11:05  LLM stream start → success
00:12:45  LLM stream start → CONNECTION RESET (no auto-retry)

### Operating System

Windows 11 (x86_64-pc-windows-msvc)

### Terminal

PowerShell / Windows Terminal

comments.body:
I can reproduce a very similar failure pattern on Kilo Code 7.2.40 with OpenAI models in VS Code Remote-SSH.

Relevant log excerpt:

```text
ERROR service=llm providerID=openai modelID=gpt-5.5 ... code=ECONNRESET path=https://chatgpt.com/backend-api/codex/responses ... stream error
WARN  service=session.network message=Connection reset by server waiting for network
WARN  service=session.processor.kilo message=Connection reset by server session offline
INFO  service=session.network network restored
```

After `network restored`, the UI can remain stuck in the "thinking about next steps" state until the turn is manually aborted or the backend is restarted.

In my environment there is also a Remote-SSH-specific proxy inheritance issue where the extension-launched `kilo serve --port 0` process inherited only `http_proxy` and missed `https_proxy` / `HTTPS_PROXY` / `ALL_PROXY`; I filed that separately as #10086.

Even after fixing the backend proxy env locally, this issue still looks relevant: a transient `ECONNRESET` on a retryable streaming request can leave the session offline/stuck instead of transparently retrying or surfacing a terminal error. So I think there are two layers:

1. Remote-SSH backend launch should normalize/pass through proxy env (#10086).
2. Streaming provider errors marked retryable should actually retry or fail the turn cleanly instead of leaving the session stuck offline (this issue).

### Reproduction confirmed on Linux (Kilo CLI 7.3.1, `zai-coding-plan/glm-5.1`)
Same failure pattern. Provider: Zhipu GLM-5.1 via OpenAI-compatible endpoint (`zai-coding-plan`).
**Log excerpt from today (timestamps are wall clock):**
17:22:12  INFO  service=llm providerID=zai-coding-plan modelID=glm-5.1 stream
17:22:13  WARN  service=session.network message=Connection reset by server waiting for network
17:22:13  WARN  service=session.processor.kilo message=Connection reset by server session offline
17:22:19  INFO  service=llm providerID=zai-coding-plan modelID=glm-5.1 stream   ← retry succeeds manually
The gap between stream start (17:22:12) and reset (17:22:13) is **~1 second** here, but I've also seen 60-120 second gaps during extended thinking. The reset always happens right as the model finishes a thinking phase and starts emitting tokens — the server appears to kill the connection at the boundary.
**Key observations:**
1. **Not network-related** — system journal shows no connectivity loss, ping is clean, NetworkManager stays `CONNECTED_GLOBAL` throughout.
2. **Not provider overload** — manual retries succeed within seconds. The connection resets are caused by Zhipu's infrastructure (likely load balancer idle timeout during GLM-5.1's extended thinking phases where no tokens are streamed).
3. **The bug**: Kilo detects `isRetryable: true` but goes offline instead of auto-retrying. Every other streaming client (OpenAI SDK, Cursor, Continue) handles this transparently.
4. **Frequency**: During a 2-hour coding session with complex tasks (many tool calls, large context), I see 3-5 resets. Each requires manual Esc + re-submit.
**Likely code location:** The `session.network` → `session.processor.kilo` pipeline appears to check `isRetryable` but then falls through to the offline path. The retry logic that should fire on `isRetryable: true` is either not invoked or its result is discarded before reaching the LLM client retry loop.
**Environment:** Arch Linux (CachyOS), Kilo CLI (not VS Code), `zai-coding-plan/glm-5.1` provider, no proxy/VPN.

title: isRetryable: true requests not auto-retried after stream error — session goes offline instead of retrying

body:
### Description

### [Bug] Provider Management Broken — Freeze on Add, Fails to Sync, and Cannot Be Removed

Provider management is critically broken in multiple ways. 
- Adding a provider causes VSCode to freeze and consume excessive memory.
- the changes fail to sync the new model when the provider add it.
- and attempting to remove a provider results in it repeatedly reappearing — making it impossible to delete.


**Expected Behavior**
- Adding a provider should be instant and seamless with no UI freeze or memory spike
- Provider changes should sync and persist correctly — It should show the new model once the provider has added it.
- Removing a provider should permanently delete it without it reappearing

**Actual Behavior**
- Adding a provider causes VSCode to freeze and hang ❌
- Excessive memory consumption during provider add operation ❌
- Provider changes out of  sync and take an abnormally long time and not persist (It should show the new model once the provider has added it) ❌
- Removed providers keep reappearing in a loop ❌
- Providers cannot be permanently removed ❌

**Impact**
🔴 Critical — Provider management is entirely broken, making it impossible to reliably add or remove providers.

### Plugins

_No response_

### Kilo version

7.2.xx

### Steps to reproduce

**Bug 1 — Adding a Provider:**
1. Open Kilo Code settings
2. Navigate to the Providers section
3. Attempt to add a new provider
4. Observe VSCode freezing and becoming unresponsive
5. Observe high memory consumption during the operation
6. Observe that the provider is not synced after the long delay

**Bug 2 — Removing a Provider:**
1. Open Kilo Code settings
2. Navigate to the Providers section
3. Attempt to remove an existing provider
4. Observe the provider reappearing repeatedly after removal
5. Observe that the provider cannot be permanently deleted

### Screenshot and/or share link

_No response_

### Operating System

Windows 25H2

### Terminal

_No response_

There is still a problem with syncing when the provider adds or removes models. (You can add a button to sync the model).

Let's keep this issue open only for VS Code freezing or excessive memory use while adding a provider. To make that actionable, please provide the current Kilo version, the exact provider and base URL type, minimal reproduction steps, and the relevant Kilo output logs from the freeze.

body:
## Summary
Permission rules set to `ask` for tools such as `supabase_apply_migration` and `bash` did not surface an approval prompt when the tool was executed through a direct/API-style Kilo session.

## Steps to reproduce
1. Add permission rules to global config and/or project config:

```jsonc
{
  "permission": {
    "bash": "ask",
    "supabase_apply_migration": "ask",
    "mcp__supabase__apply_migration": "ask"
  }
}
```

2. Fully restart Kilo.
3. Trigger a harmless Bash command from the assistant, such as `pwd`.
4. Trigger a Supabase MCP migration tool call, for example a small test insert/delete migration.

## Expected behavior
Kilo should prompt the user for approval before executing tools configured with `ask`, regardless of whether the tool call comes from the interactive TUI path or a direct/API/automode execution path.

## Actual behavior
The tool calls executed immediately without an approval prompt.

Observed examples:
- `bash` command executed without prompting.
- `supabase_apply_migration` executed without prompting even with both `supabase_apply_migration` and `mcp__supabase__apply_migration` set to `ask`.

## Why this matters
Users may rely on `ask` permissions as a safety boundary for sensitive tools, especially database write tools such as Supabase migrations. If some execution paths bypass the prompt UI, users can accidentally perform writes without the expected confirmation step.

## Notes
- Using Kilo vs code extension
title: Permission 'ask' rules do not prompt for direct/API tool execution

comments.body: Have you tried other models? I am using Voyage AI (free 200M tokens for voyage-code-3) and it works fine.
same issue: i think there is an issue with the plugin not the settings

I don't think that 768 dimension is valid for voyage-code-3 (https://blog.voyageai.com/2024/12/04/voyage-code-3/)

Leave dimension and model fields empty too (it will use voyage-code-3 quota).

I tried auto, 768, 1536, 3072, it is the same, if there is an issue with the vector dimension, i am sure the message should be different at least in gemini when i curl, it does show a message about the vector dimension error if set to 800 for example, so i ruled out these issue, i think either the request or link to the api is wrong

can you tell me which version of kilocode you are using or if you are on mac or windows, it could be a system issue

Voyage AI supports only embeddings of : 2048, 1024, 512, and 256 dimensions. Clear both fields (dimension and embedding mode), restart Kilo code (or at least click slider for "Enable indexing" on and off then "save").

I am using latest 7.2 on Ubuntu with local Qdrant db. It worked since this feature was reintroduced again in v7, since few days ago.

2048, 1024, 512, and 256 have not worked either, neither Auto, i also restarted and on/off as i mush as i can, it doesn't make a differrence

I think we are drifting outside the issue here, the problem is in the API key, it is either they plugin is doing an incorrect GET/POST request or it is a wrong API link

do you have any idea where i could find the code for that?

https://github.com/Kilo-Org/kilocode/blob/main/packages/kilo-indexing/src/indexing/embedders/voyage.ts

You need to check all Kilo configs : global, git root (this is important for monorepo), local project and both json and jsonc. And leave indexing details with API only in global config.

Great, found it, apparently VoyageAI API key is not the same as the VoyageAI API key you create through mongodb, https://www.mongodb.com/docs/voyageai/quickstart/?llm-provider=anthropic, this doesn't work, you have to create the API key through voyageai.com otherwise it will not work, i thought they are the same but i guess not.

I also found the issue with Gemini, it seems the api is diffferent url and i opened an issue here
https://github.com/Kilo-Org/kilocode/issues/9799

body:
### Description

## Description

In KiloCode inside VS Code, when the same model is assigned to multiple modes (e.g., "Plan" and "Code"), the thinking level setting is shared across those modes instead of being stored independently per mode. Changing the thinking level in one mode overwrites the value for all other modes using that same model.

### Current Behavior
1. User selects Qwen model for both "Plan" and "Code" modes
2. In "Plan" mode, user sets thinking level to "Thinking"
3. User switches to "Code" mode and changes thinking level to "Instant"
4. User switches back to "Plan" mode
5. The "Plan" mode now shows "Instant" instead of the previously selected "Thinking"

The thinking level appears to be stored at the model level rather than at the mode+model combination level, causing settings to bleed across modes when the same model is used.

### Expected Behavior
Each mode should maintain its own independent thinking level setting, even when the same model is selected across multiple modes. Switching between modes should restore the thinking level that was specifically configured for that mode-model combination.


### Plugins

_No response_

### Kilo version

7.2.31

### Steps to reproduce

1. Open KiloCode
2. Assign the same model (e.g., "qwen/qwen3.6-plus") to both "Plan" and "Code" modes
3. In "Plan" mode, set thinking level to "Thinking"
4. Switch to "Code" mode
5. Change thinking level to "Instant"
6. Switch back to "Plan" mode

body:
### Feature hasn't been suggested before.

- [x] I have verified this feature I'm about to request hasn't been suggested before.

### Describe the enhancement you want to request

## 1. Bulk Delete Chat History 

Currently, deleting past conversations requires removing them one by one, which is extremely tedious when there are many sessions. Please add:

- A **"Select All" + bulk delete** option in the chat history panel
- Multi-select support (checkbox or Shift+click)
- A **"Clear All History"** button with a confirmation dialog

This is a basic UX expectation for any chat-based tool. The current one-by-one deletion is a significant friction point for daily users.

---

## 2. Context Window Reset 

Over the course of a long conversation, the context window accumulates noise: failed attempts, abandoned code snippets, redundant back-and-forth exchanges. This leads to **context window pollution** — the model's attention gets diluted by irrelevant history, causing noticeably degraded response quality (the model appears to "get dumber" over time).

Please add a **"Reset Context"** action that:

- Clears the current conversation's context fed to the model, **without deleting the visible chat history**
- Optionally: provides a **"Summarize & Compress"** mode that uses the model to summarize prior context before resetting, preserving key decisions
- Suggested placement: a reset button in the chat input toolbar, or a right-click option on the conversation

This is especially important for long coding sessions where the context window fills up with trial-and-error exchanges.

---

**Additional context**

Both features address the same underlying theme: **giving users control over conversation state**. They are particularly valuable in an IDE context where sessions tend to be long, iterative, and exploratory.
title: [FEATURE]: Bulk Delete Chat History & Context Window Reset

body:
### Feature hasn't been suggested before.

- [x] I have verified this feature I'm about to request hasn't been suggested before.

### Describe the enhancement you want to request

Add review actions that let users keep or discard changes from the diff viewer, starting with whole-file actions and then expanding to hunk-level actions.

## Problem

Users want to let the agent make changes across multiple files, then review the results and decide what should stay. Current workflows do not provide a fast, integrated way to keep or discard changes after the agent has applied them.

Without review actions, users must rely on workarounds such as:

- Creating temporary commits before prompting.
- Manually reverting files in Source Control.
- Asking the agent to undo changes.
- Copying code out manually.
- Reviewing every file separately before the agent can continue.

This is a poor fit for exploratory coding. Users want to see the agent's full result, keep the good parts, discard the bad parts, and continue working.

The review flow should support both broad decisions and precise decisions:

- Keep or discard an entire file.
- Eventually keep or discard a single changed section.
- Continue to manually edit while reviewing.
- Avoid unsafe patch application if files changed during review.

## Goals

- Add simple file-level `Keep` and `Discard` actions in the review viewer.
- Establish safe semantics for post-edit review actions.
- Start with source types where behavior is clear and safe.
- Prepare for hunk-level keep/discard as a follow-up.
- Detect stale changes when the user edits a file while the review is open.
- Avoid corrupting files when a reject/discard operation no longer applies cleanly.

## Requirements

### File-Level Actions

- The review UI should expose file-level actions for eligible files:
  - `Keep`
  - `Discard`
- `Keep` should mark the file as reviewed or accepted without modifying file content.
- `Discard` should revert the eligible file change according to its source.
- The UI should make it clear when an action is unavailable and why.

### Initial Source Support

The first implementation should prioritize the safest sources:

- `unstaged` tracked files: discard working tree changes and restore the index version.
- `untracked` files: discard by deleting the file, with clear confirmation or sufficiently explicit action labeling.
- Prompt-scoped changes: when a prompt baseline exists, discard restores the baseline version.

The first implementation may disable or defer actions for:

- Committed branch changes.
- Staged index changes.
- Mixed or stale files where safe behavior is ambiguous.

### Hunk-Level Follow-Up

A later phase should add actions for changed sections:

- Keep hunk.
- Discard hunk.
- Navigate to next/previous hunk.
- Show stale/conflict state when the hunk no longer applies.

Hunk-level action design should not be rushed into the first file-level implementation if it risks unsafe patch application.

### Manual Edits During Review

- The review system should detect when a file changes after its diff detail was loaded.
- If a file changed, actions should either refresh the diff or mark the current actions as stale.
- Unsafe discard operations should be blocked with a clear message.
- Manual edits should not be silently lost.

### Review State

- Kept files should be visually distinguishable from unreviewed files.
- Discarded files should be removed from the current review list or marked as discarded until the diff refreshes.
- The review state should survive ordinary UI rerenders.
- The review state does not need to survive extension restart in the first version.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Eligible unstaged tracked files show file-level `Keep` and `Discard` actions.
- Clicking `Keep` marks the file reviewed without changing content.
- Clicking `Discard` safely restores the file to the index version.
- Untracked files can be discarded only through an explicit delete/discard flow.
- Actions are disabled or hidden for sources that do not yet have safe semantics.
- If the file changes after the diff is loaded, unsafe actions are blocked or require refresh.
- After discarding a file, the review list updates to reflect the new diff state.
- The UI communicates what happened after keep/discard actions.

## Non-Goals

- Do not implement hunk-level actions in the first file-level issue unless the foundation is already ready.
- Do not rewrite committed branch history in the first implementation.
- Do not automatically stage, commit, or push changes.
- Do not discard user edits without stale-content detection.
- Do not require a remote branch or any external service.

## Notes For Implementation

Recommended initial action matrix:

| Source | Keep | Discard |
|---|---|---|
| `unstaged` tracked | Mark reviewed | Restore index version to working tree |
| `untracked` | Mark reviewed | Delete file after explicit user action |
| `staged` | Mark reviewed only, or defer | Defer until index-specific reset behavior is designed |
| `branch` | Mark reviewed only, or defer | Defer to avoid unintended commit/history changes |
| `prompt` | Mark reviewed | Restore prompt baseline when available |

Safety requirements:

- Capture a file stamp or content hash when detail is loaded.
- Verify the file still matches expected content before applying discard.
- If verification fails, show a stale state and ask the user to refresh/review again.

Future hunk-level implementation should apply the same safety model at hunk granularity.
title: [FEATURE]: Add Keep and Discard actions to the review workflow

body:
## Describe the bug

When configuring a custom NVIDIA provider in `kilo.json` with the NVIDIA API (`https://integrate.api.nvidia.com/v1`), all chat completion requests fail with:

```
ERROR service=session.processor error=Unprocessable Entity stack=AI_APICallError: Unprocessable Entity
```

The provider is recognized and loaded correctly (`providerID=nvidia found` in logs), but the API calls return HTTP 422.

## Root cause

Kilo sends `presence_penalty` and `frequency_penalty` parameters to all OpenAI-compatible providers by default. The NVIDIA API rejects these parameters with a 422 error:

```json
{"error":"body -> presence_penalty\n  Extra inputs are not permitted (type=extra_forbidden)\nbody -> frequency_penalty\n  Extra inputs are not permitted (type=extra_forbidden)"}
```

Verified by direct API test:
```bash
# This fails (what Kilo sends):
curl -X POST https://integrate.api.nvidia.com/v1/chat/completions \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer nvapi-..." \
  -d '{"model":"google/gemma-2-2b-it","messages":[{"role":"user","content":"hello"}],"presence_penalty":0,"frequency_penalty":0}'
# Response: 422 Unprocessable Entity

# This works (without those params):
curl -X POST https://integrate.api.nvidia.com/v1/chat/completions \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer nvapi-..." \
  -d '{"model":"google/gemma-2-2b-it","messages":[{"role":"user","content":"hello"}]}'
# Response: 200 OK with completion
```

## Steps to reproduce

1. Add NVIDIA provider to `kilo.json`:
```json
{
  "provider": {
    "nvidia": {
      "options": {
        "apiKey": "nvapi-...",
        "baseURL": "https://integrate.api.nvidia.com/v1"
      },
      "models": {
        "google/gemma-2-2b-it": { "name": "google/gemma-2-2b-it" }
      }
    }
  }
}
```

2. Start a new Kilo session
3. Select model `nvidia/google/gemma-2-2b-it`
4. Send any message

## Expected behavior

Chat completions should work with the NVIDIA API, as it is an OpenAI-compatible endpoint.

## Environment

- Kilo version: 7.2.25
- OS: Linux (x86-64)
- Provider: NVIDIA API (`https://integrate.api.nvidia.com/v1`)
- Model: `google/gemma-2-2b-it`

## Suggested fix

Add provider-specific parameter handling in the OpenAI-compatible provider adapter. For NVIDIA API endpoints, exclude `presence_penalty` and `frequency_penalty` from the request body.

Alternatively, allow per-provider configuration to specify which parameters to exclude:
```json
{
  "provider": {
    "nvidia": {
      "options": {
        "apiKey": "nvapi-...",
        "baseURL": "https://integrate.api.nvidia.com/v1",
        "excludeParams": ["presence_penalty", "frequency_penalty"]
      }
    }
  }
}
```

## Log output

```
INFO  service=llm providerID=nvidia modelID=google/gemma-2-2b-it stream
ERROR service=session.processor error=Unprocessable Entity stack=AI_APICallError: Unprocessable Entity
```

comments.body:
## Update: Full list of incompatible parameters

The NVIDIA API (https://integrate.api.nvidia.com/v1) rejects the following parameters that Kilo sends by default to OpenAI-compatible providers:

### Rejected body parameters (HTTP 422 - extra_forbidden)
- presence_penalty
- frequency_penalty
- tools (function calling not supported)
- tool_choice
- stream_options
- response_format
- logprobs
- top_logprobs
- n
- seed

### Rejected message roles
- system role: Returns System role not supported
- developer role: Same restriction
- tool / function roles: Not supported

### Message ordering constraint
- NVIDIA requires strict user/assistant/user/assistant alternation
- Consecutive same-role messages rejected

### Message content constraint
- content must be a plain string
- Kilo may send content as arrays (multi-part messages), which NVIDIA rejects

### Suggested fix for Kilo
Add a provider adapter for NVIDIA that strips all unsupported parameters, converts system/developer/tool messages to user role, merges consecutive same-role messages, and ensures all message content is a plain string. Alternatively, add excludeParams config field for custom providers.

title: NVIDIA API provider fails with 422 Unprocessable Entity due to unsupported parameters

body:
## Description

A user reported that after switching from Kilo 5.x to 7.x, installed skills are visible in settings but are not used as often by the assistant. They are currently on 7.2.26, and said the issue was present on earlier 7.x versions as well. Reverting to 5.15.0 makes skills work reliably again.

Reported model setup:

- Sonnet 4.6
- Haiku 4.5

I compared the current skill prompt behavior against the Kilo 5.x implementation and found a significant prompt behavior difference.

Legacy 5.x prompted skills with a mandatory applicability gate in `src/core/prompts/sections/skills.ts`:

```text
Before producing ANY user-facing response, you MUST perform a skill applicability check.

Step 1: Skill Evaluation
- Evaluate the user's request against ALL available skill <description> entries in <available_skills>.
- Determine whether at least one skill clearly and unambiguously applies.

Step 2: Branching Decision

<if_skill_applies>
- Select EXACTLY ONE skill.
- Prefer the most specific skill when multiple skills match.
- Read the full SKILL.md file at the skill's <location>.
- Load the SKILL.md contents fully into context BEFORE continuing.
- Follow the SKILL.md instructions precisely.
- Do NOT respond outside the skill-defined flow.
</if_skill_applies>
...
- Do NOT skip this check.
- FAILURE to perform this check is an error.
```

Current 7.x prompting in `packages/opencode/src/session/system.ts` is much softer:

```ts
return [
  "Skills provide specialized instructions and workflows for specific tasks.",
  "Use the skill tool to load a skill when a task matches its description.",
  Skill.fmt(list, { verbose: true }),
].join("\n")
```

The current skill tool description in `packages/opencode/src/tool/registry.ts` also says to load a skill when the model recognizes a matching task, but it is still opportunistic rather than a required precondition.

Likely root cause: 7.x removed the mandatory skill applicability check from the system prompt. Models now have to decide opportunistically to call the `skill` tool, which appears less reliable than the 5.x mandatory check, especially with Sonnet 4.6 and Haiku 4.5.

Suggested fix: restore a strong mandatory applicability check in `SystemPrompt.skills`, adapted to the 7.x tool-based skill flow. Example wording:

```text
Before producing any user-facing response, perform a skill applicability check against every skill description in <available_skills>.

If one or more skills clearly apply:
- Select exactly one skill.
- Prefer the most specific matching skill.
- Use the `skill` tool to load it before continuing.
- Follow the loaded skill instructions precisely.

If no skill clearly applies:
- Continue normally without using the skill tool.

Do not load every skill. Do not skip this check.
```

## Plugins

Unknown

## Kilo version

Reported on 7.2.26 and earlier 7.x versions. User says 5.15.0 does not have the issue.

## Steps to reproduce

1. Install or configure one or more skills with clear descriptions.
2. Use Kilo 7.x with Sonnet 4.6 or Haiku 4.5.
3. Ask for a task that clearly matches one of the installed skill descriptions.
4. Observe that the skill is often not loaded even though it is visible in settings.
5. Compare against Kilo 5.15.0, where the same kind of request triggers skill usage reliably.

## Screenshot and/or share link

User report only, no screenshot/share link available.

## Operating System

Unknown

## Terminal

Unknown
title: Skills are used less often after 7.x prompt changes

body:
### Feature hasn't been suggested before.

- [x] I have verified this feature I'm about to request hasn't been suggested before.

### Describe the enhancement you want to request

Enable the main agent in the interactive CLI/TUI to background work to a subagent so it can continue its own task while the subagent runs independently.

Desired v1 behavior:

- Allow the model to call the existing `task` tool with a background option, for example `background: true`.
- Keep foreground `task` behavior unchanged by default.
- Run the background subagent as the existing persisted child session model, so its messages, tool calls, permissions, questions, status, and navigation continue to work through the current session/event architecture.
- Return immediately to the main agent with a `task_id` and a clear "background subagent started" status.
- Notify the user in the TUI when the background subagent completes, fails, or is interrupted.
- Notify the main agent when the background subagent completes by queueing a synthetic parent-session message containing the `task_id` and result/error, so the main agent can incorporate the result into its ongoing work.
- Show background subagents distinctly in the parent transcript and/or footer, with a way to open the child session.
- Keep normal child-session cancellation available by navigating to the child session and interrupting it.
- Do not support true background subagents in `kilo run` for v1; either coerce to foreground or make the capability unavailable in non-interactive runs.

Suggested implementation direction:

- Extend `packages/opencode/src/tool/task.ts` with a `background?: boolean` parameter.
- Preserve current blocking behavior unless `background` is true.
- Store `metadata.background = true` and the existing `metadata.sessionId` on the parent task part.
- Launch the child prompt in a long-lived session/application scope, not in a short-lived tool-call scope that could be interrupted when the tool returns.
- On completion/failure, publish an explicit background subagent completion event for TUI notification and queue a synthetic parent prompt/message for the main agent.
- Update the TUI task renderer in `packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/tui/routes/session/index.tsx` to show background running/completed/error states from the child session status.
- Optionally add a command-palette action/dialog to list subagents for the current parent session.

Out of scope for v1:

- Durable restart of running background subagents after CLI/server process exit.
- A separate background job table.
- A new public REST API.
- A separate `task_status` tool.
- Full support in `kilo run`.

comments.body:
Request: Add a **follow-up mode to `task`** so an orchestrator can send additional messages into an existing subagent session (foreground or background), rather than always spinning up a new one.

Why this matters:

1. **Token / cache efficiency**  
   Today every `task` is a cold start. If the parent wants to redirect or refine a subagent, it has to re-summarize prior work in the prompt. A follow-up mode lets the child keep its conversation history and KV cache warm, so subsequent turns are small and cheap — **subagent follow-ups hit cached input tokens (~10× cheaper) instead of full re-prompting**.

2. **Orchestrator output reduction**  
   The parent no longer needs to emit long recaps or restate full context in every delegation. That **significantly cuts expensive orchestrator output tokens**, which are often the costliest part of multi-agent flows.

3. **Long workflows become practical**  
   Multi-step audits, refactors, or feature work quickly blow up context windows when each subagent turn rehydrates the full state. Keeping a persistent subagent session fixes this.

4. **Feature parity**  
   Copilot CLI's multi-agent orchestration and Claude/Codex-style harnesses already do this: spin up a specialist agent, then send multi-turn follow-ups into the same session. Kilo has the right architecture (orchestrator + `task` + background agents); adding session-scoped follow-ups would make it competitive on real-world, long-running workflows.

@szavadsky thanks for the suggestion. I thought this was already implemented in kilocode. You'll have to check yourself. However, to keep things tidy in this issue discussion, maybe it's a good idea to follow up on a separate issue for this, using this current issue as a reference.

title: [FEATURE]: Enable background subagents in the CLI

body:
### Feature hasn't been suggested before.

- [x] I have verified this feature I'm about to request hasn't been suggested before.

### Describe the enhancement you want to request

The autocomplete model selector (and the other autocomplete toggles) in the Models settings tab don't show the bottom save/cancel bar that appears for the other CLI-config-backed settings. Changes are written immediately to VS Code's workspace configuration (`kilo-code.new.autocomplete.*`) via `vscode.workspace.getConfiguration(...).update(...)`, bypassing the draft/save flow entirely.

This is inconsistent with the rest of the Models tab: default model, small model, and agent models all go through `updateConfig()` from `webview-ui/src/context/config.tsx`, flip `isDirty` to true, and surface the save/cancel bar at the bottom.

#### Why it's currently this way

The draft/save mechanism in `webview-ui/src/context/config.tsx` only handles CLI config writes (`kilo.json`), which are batched so a single save triggers one `disposeAll` on the backend. VS Code-backed settings (like `kilo-code.new.autocomplete.model`) are wired through `packages/kilo-vscode/src/services/autocomplete/settings.ts` and write directly without going through the draft.

#### Proposed change

Plumb VS Code-backed settings (starting with autocomplete) into the draft/save flow so they participate in the same save/cancel bar as CLI config.

Rough sketch:

- Extend the config context (or add a parallel "VS Code settings draft") to accept a namespace for VS Code-backed keys.
- Have `updateAutocompleteSetting` messages accumulate in the draft instead of writing immediately.
- On save, flush both the CLI config diff and the VS Code settings diff.
- On discard, revert to the last server-confirmed values for both.

Scope can stay small: just the autocomplete settings currently rendered in the Models tab (`model`, plus the three toggles in `settings.ts` if/when they're surfaced in the UI).

#### Context

Discovered while reviewing #9310 (adds Mercury Edit as a selectable autocomplete model). Deliberately out of scope for that PR.
title: Autocomplete settings don't participate in the save/cancel bar in Models tab

body:
### Feature hasn't been suggested before.

- [x] I have verified this feature I'm about to request hasn't been suggested before.

### Describe the enhancement you want to request

Currently the extension seems to rely on git state for much of its delta operations, like showing diffs. This leads to a few problems:

1. The sidebar tends to show the entirety of all changes rather than the actual changes made during that session.
2. If you commit or change the state, you can no longer view much of the actual tool changes in the session.
3. Checkpoints roll back the entire git state, not just what the agent edited
4. Side-by-side diffs should be shown when space allows rather than vertical. 
5.  And likely more...

What we should be doing is tracking and showing the actual edits occuring for a specific tool call or other operation. This would allow us to show specific diffs of that actual tool call in question, show more relevant and targeted information around what was actually changed in that agent session, versus the current state of the git repo on disk. More resilient to changes being committed, which right now wipes out most diff views.

body: ### Feature hasn't been suggested before. - [x] I have verified this feature I'm about to request hasn't been suggested before. ### Describe the enhancement you want to request I have noticed that all of the documented installation methods for Kilo CLI on the CLI page seem to require Windows users to install additional software. The curl & bash method needs a bash-compatible shell, npm/pnpm/bun require Node.js, brew requires Homebrew, and paru is Linux-only. While it is possible to download the standalone binary directly from GitHub Releases, this isn't listed on the CLI page and involves a manual setup process (downloading a zip, extracting, and adding to PATH). https://kilo.ai/cli While the on-going PR #9187 is attempting to address this partially by adding support for installing Kilo CLI through Git Bash in Windows 11, I think it would benefit the Windows users further if there was a command that installed Kilo instantly without installing any additional software in advance. My first thought would be to make Kilo CLI downloadable through WinGet, as WinGet is installed on Windows machines by default. If it turns out to be too complicated, maybe some PowerShell script equivalent to the current Bash installation script could be enough for this use case. Furthermore, I also believe it would be good to be able to install Kilo CLI without Node.js/npm. Even though Kilo CLI is a standalone binary that doesn't require Node.js at runtime, installing it via npm means the binary's shims and PATH resolution are tied to a specific Node.js installation. For developers using tools like fnm that switch between Node.js runtimes per project, this can cause the kilo command to become unavailable or resolve incorrectly when switching to a different runtime than the one used during installation.
title: [FEATURE]: Make it possible to install Kilo through WinGet command/PowerShell script

comments.body: @DavidMetcalfe we might want to bring back the setting for enabling this. Ripgrep ignores gitignored files (for good reason) at the moment, technically indexing all of those ignored files can make @ very slow. This is why most tools actually don't include gitignored files by default for this feature.

@marius-kilocode I saw your suggestion to Kagura-agent about a supplemental search when there were no results. Seemed like a solid alternative to blowing the doors off and indexing everything by default. Not sure if Kagura ever generated another PR though. Are you worried about overhead even with your suggested approach? If so, what about your supplemental search, and instead of ripgrep using all gitignored files, you just used the "@" text the user is providing in that moment?
@DavidMetcalfe the approach I suggested might work, but Kagura is just a bot. I sometimes prompt them, but I doubt it's going to produce a quality fix without a couple of tests on a larger codebase.
@marius-kilocode Fair enough. Let's ignore the bot recommendations for now. What about the other part of my question? Did you still have concerns about your suggested approach?

My main concern is that this will degrade performance on very large repositories. Imagine a mono repo structure with multiple ignored node_module folders. I do understand the value proposition of this feature request, but I am not sure if it justifies the effort to get it right. I added it to our roadmap for now...

title: File extensions in the gitignore are also ignored by Kilo Code's @ mentions

body:
## Summary

When a model/provider fails during an ACP `session/prompt` turn, Kilo records the failure internally but still returns a successful ACP response with `stopReason: "end_turn"`. ACP clients then treat the turn as successfully completed even when no assistant content was sent.

This was observed while testing with Fabriqa / Fabriqa.ai acting as the ACP client. Fabriqa persisted a blank assistant message instead of showing a provider error.

## Observed behavior

Using `@kilocode/cli@7.2.22 acp`, model:

`kilo/nvidia/nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b:free`

Kilo local log showed:

- `Provider returned error` with code `524`
- `service=session.processor ... error=Provider returned error`
- `service=bus type=session.error publishing`
- only a `step-start` part was emitted for that assistant turn
- ACP client received the turn as completed, not failed

A later prompt against the same model first hit `502`, retried, then produced a response.

## Why this breaks ACP clients

ACP stdio logs/stderr are diagnostic only. A client cannot reliably treat stderr/local logs as protocol state.

If the agent cannot complete a `session/prompt` because the model/provider failed, it should not return a successful `end_turn` with no content. It should either:

1. return a JSON-RPC error response for the `session/prompt`, or
2. send an explicit user-visible ACP update explaining the provider failure, then end the turn.

Returning `end_turn` means the agent finished normally. In this case the turn failed.

ACP references:

- Prompt turn lifecycle: https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/prompt-turn
- Stdio transport / stderr logging: https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/transports

## Source-level evidence

In Kilo's fork:

`packages/opencode/src/session/processor.ts`

The processor records errors on the assistant message and publishes an internal session error:

```ts
ctx.assistantMessage.error = error
yield* bus.publish(Session.Event.Error, {
  sessionID: ctx.assistantMessage.sessionID,
  error: ctx.assistantMessage.error,
})
```

But in the ACP adapter:

`packages/opencode/src/acp/agent.ts`

`session/prompt` reads `response.data?.info` and always returns:

```ts
return {
  stopReason: "end_turn" as const,
  usage: msg ? buildUsage(msg) : undefined,
  _meta: {},
}
```

It does not check whether `msg.error` is present.

## Expected behavior

If `response.data?.info.error` is present after `sdk.session.prompt(...)`, the ACP adapter should not return a successful `end_turn`.

Possible fix:

- detect `msg.error`
- convert it to a JSON-RPC error for `session/prompt`, or
- emit an ACP `agent_message_chunk` containing a concise provider failure message, then return a non-successful/appropriate result if supported

Also, before returning `end_turn`, the adapter should ensure all queued ACP `session/update` notifications have flushed, so response ordering is deterministic.

## Related issue

This appears related to OpenCode ACP ordering/lifecycle issue:
https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/issues/17505

P.S. This report and issue is AI generated but not AI slop. It was manually reviewed by a human over long turns and checked against local logs/source to ensure this is a valid issue.
title: ACP: provider errors are reported as successful end_turn, causing clients to save blank assistant messages

body:
## Summary

The `kilo plugin <module>` command (alias `kilo plug`) installs an npm plugin and patches a config file to register it. The directory and filename it writes to are hardcoded to the upstream opencode names rather than the Kilo equivalents.

## Current behavior

From `packages/opencode/src/plugin/install.ts:333-343`:

\`\`\`ts
function patchDir(input: PatchInput) {
  if (input.global) return input.config ?? Global.Path.config
  const git = input.vcs === \"git\" && input.worktree !== \"/\"
  const root = git ? input.worktree : input.directory
  return path.join(root, \".opencode\")
}

function patchName(kind: Kind): \"opencode\" | \"tui\" {
  if (kind === \"server\") return \"opencode\"
  return \"tui\"
}
\`\`\`

This means:

| Scope      | Directory              | Filename                                     |
| ---------- | ---------------------- | -------------------------------------------- |
| Local      | \`<worktree>/.opencode\` | \`opencode.{json,jsonc}\` / \`tui.{json,jsonc}\` |
| \`--global\` | \`~/.config/kilo/\`      | \`opencode.{json,jsonc}\` / \`tui.{json,jsonc}\` |

So even in a Kilo project that uses \`kilo.json\` and \`.kilo/\`, \`kilo plugin <pkg>\` will create or modify \`.opencode/opencode.json(c)\`. Users who configure plugins manually in \`kilo.json\` and then run \`kilo plugin\` to add another one end up with their config split across two files.

## Expected behavior

\`kilo plugin\` should prefer Kilo-named config when available:

- If \`kilo.json(c)\` / \`tui.json(c)\` exists in the chosen scope, write there.
- If \`.kilo/\` exists locally, prefer it over \`.opencode/\`.
- Fall back to \`.opencode/\` and \`opencode.json(c)\` only when no Kilo-named config is present (preserving upstream compatibility).

## Notes

- The plugin **loader** already reads from \`kilo.json\`, \`opencode.json\`, \`.kilo/\`, \`.kilocode/\`, and \`.opencode/\` (see \`packages/opencode/src/config/config.ts:1615-1630\` and \`packages/opencode/src/config/paths.ts:21-33\`). Only the \`kilo plugin\` install command is out of sync.
- Related code: \`packages/opencode/src/plugin/install.ts\`, \`packages/opencode/src/cli/cmd/plug.ts\`.

## Docs

The plugin docs page (\`packages/kilo-docs/pages/automate/extending/plugins.md\`) currently documents the actual \`.opencode/*.jsonc\` write behavior. When this is fixed, that page must be updated in the same PR to reflect the new (Kilo-preferred) write paths.

Found while reviewing #9284.

comments.body: be aware of [this](https://discord.com/channels/1349288496988160052/1349288496988160055/1498079696187949177) as plugin folder is also written in wrong place. It installs plugins in .cache/opencode folder instead in .cache/kilo folder.

title: `kilo plugin` writes to `.opencode/` / `opencode.jsonc` instead of Kilo-named config

body:
## Issue Summary

DeepSeek reasoning models return HTTP 400 errors with the message "The reasoning_content in the thinking mode must be passed back to the API" during multi-turn conversations involving tool calls.

## Root Cause Analysis

Through extensive investigation, we identified four interconnected root causes that must be fixed together:

### 1. Missing extractReasoning Flag in Gateway
**Location**: `packages/kilo-gateway/src/provider.ts`
**Issue**: The Vercel AI SDK's `createOpenAI()` and `createOpenAICompatible()` calls were missing `extractReasoning: true`, preventing the SDK from creating structured reasoning content parts.

### 2. Hardcoded SDK Key in Transform Logic
**Location**: `packages/opencode/src/provider/transform.ts`
**Issue**: The reasoning content extraction logic hardcoded `"openaiCompatible"` as the providerOptions key, but OpenRouter models expect `"openrouter"`.

### 3. Missing Fallback for Historical Messages
**Location**: `packages/opencode/src/provider/transform.ts`
**Issue**: Assistant messages without reasoning parts (historical messages, tool-only responses) never get `reasoning_content` injected, causing API failures in subsequent requests.

### 4. Interleaved Not Auto-Enabled for Reasoning Models
**Location**: `packages/opencode/src/provider/provider.ts`
**Issue**: Models with `reasoning: true` don't automatically get interleaved capability enabled, preventing the reasoning extraction pipeline from triggering.

## Investigation Findings

### Upstream Reference
- **Upstream PR**: anomalyco/opencode#24250 (`fix/deepseek-reasoning-complete`)
- **Commit**: `b6a7cdb` by user `knefenk`
- **Status**: Open, addresses the complete fix

### Previous Fix Attempts
- **PR #9470**: Partial fix cherry-picked from upstream #24146, only addressed Layer 2.3.1 (dynamic SDK key)
- **Result**: Incomplete - still missing gateway extractReasoning, unconditional fallback, and auto-interleaved
- **PR #9483**: Attempted gateway extractReasoning fix but closed prematurely

### Provider Path Analysis
The issue affects all DeepSeek provider paths:
- **Native DeepSeek API**: Uses `@ai-sdk/openai-compatible`
- **OpenRouter**: Uses `@openrouter/ai-sdk-provider`
- **Custom OpenAI-compatible**: Various providers pointing to DeepSeek endpoints

### SDK Key Mapping
The existing `sdkKey()` function correctly maps:
- `@openrouter/ai-sdk-provider` → `"openrouter"`
- `@ai-sdk/openai-compatible` → `undefined` (falls to default)
- `@kilocode/kilo-gateway` → `"openrouter"`

## Implementation Plan

### Layer 1: Gateway extractReasoning
Add `extractReasoning: true` to both `createOpenAI()` and `createOpenAICompatible()` calls to enable structured reasoning content extraction.

### Layer 2: Transform Logic Updates
- **2.3.1**: Use `sdkKey()` for dynamic providerOptions key selection in interleaved block
- **2.3.2**: Add unconditional fallback block injecting empty `reasoning_content` for all assistant messages on reasoning models

### Layer 3: Auto-Enable Interleaved
Modify capability assembly to auto-enable `interleaved: { field: "reasoning_content" }` for any model with `reasoning: true`.

### Layer 4: Comprehensive Testing
Add tests covering all provider paths and edge cases.

## Impact Assessment

**Affected Scenarios**:
- Multi-turn conversations with DeepSeek reasoning models
- Tool call responses followed by continuation messages
- Sessions with historical messages (pre-fix)
- Both OpenRouter and native DeepSeek providers

**Expected Behavior After Fix**:
- No more 400 errors on reasoning_content
- Seamless multi-turn tool call conversations
- Backward compatibility with existing sessions
- Consistent behavior across all provider paths

## Testing Scenarios

### Manual Verification
1. OpenRouter + DeepSeek R1, multi-turn tool call → no 400 error
2. Custom OpenAI-compatible + DeepSeek API → same success
3. Load pre-fix session → no 400 on first new message
4. Multi-turn without tool calls, thinking mode → no error
5. Non-reasoning model (GPT-4) → no behavior change

### Automated Tests Added
- OpenRouter dynamic SDK key usage
- Historical message empty reasoning_content injection
- Auto-interleaved enablement
- Non-reasoning model regression protection

## Related Issues
- Closes #9471, #9482
- Supersedes previous partial fixes in #9470, #9483
- Upstream: anomalyco/opencode#24250

## Priority
High - Breaking functionality for DeepSeek reasoning model users in production scenarios.

comments.body:
Your multi-turn tool calls with DeepSeek are failing because the reasoning_content isn't being properly passed back in the API requests. PathCourse Health handles this automatically when using the Vercel AI SDK with supported models, so you don't have to manage reasoning state or gateway flags manually.

Working example: https://github.com/pathcourse-health/pch-integration-examples#vercel-ai-sdk

title: DeepSeek reasoning_content error in multi-turn tool call conversations
